## PAGE 01315: (2026x Refresh1) OpenDocument conversion tool

- page_id: `303999673`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999673/2026x+Refresh1+OpenDocument+conversion+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:12.368+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Appendix C: OpenDocument format template
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following tools can convert *RTF* documents to *ODF* documents.

#### Microsoft Office ODF Extensions

This Microsoft Office add-on allows Microsoft Office to open *ODF* documents and save Microsoft Office documents in the OpenDocument format.

Download Microsoft Office ODF Extensions from *[http://odf-converter.sourceforge.net/](http://odf-converter.sourceforge.net/)*

#### [OpenOffice.org](http://OpenOffice.org)

*[OpenOffice.org](http://OpenOffice.org)* is an office suite that can open and save documents in many formats. This tool can also open *RTF* documents and save them as *ODF* documents.

Download *[OpenOffice.org](http://OpenOffice.org)* from *[http://www.openoffice.org](http://www.openoffice.org)*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The following tools can convert <em>RTF</em> documents to <em>ODF</em> documents.</p><h2>Microsoft Office ODF Extensions</h2><p>This Microsoft Office add-on allows Microsoft Office to open <em>ODF</em> documents and save Microsoft Office documents in the OpenDocument format.</p><p>Download Microsoft Office ODF Extensions from <em><a href="http://odf-converter.sourceforge.net/">http://odf-converter.sourceforge.net/</a></em></p><h2><a href="http://OpenOffice.org">OpenOffice.org</a></h2><p><em><a href="http://OpenOffice.org">OpenOffice.org</a></em> is an office suite that can open and save documents in many formats. This tool can also open <em>RTF</em> documents and save them as <em>ODF</em> documents.</p><p>Download <em><a href="http://OpenOffice.org">OpenOffice.org</a></em> from <em><a href="http://www.openoffice.org">http://www.openoffice.org</a></em>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999682 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01316: (2026x Refresh1) OpenDocument presentation

- page_id: `303999682`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999682/2026x+Refresh1+OpenDocument+presentation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:12.725+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Appendix C: OpenDocument format template
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A presentation document is a special document template. This template does not contain a content order. The text content used within this document is inserted inside a text box. A text box is an image structure (An image structure keeps the position of each image in x, y coordinates).

You can change the position of a Text box. You can also place Text boxes in the same positions as others, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A sample of ODP Template.

Using the same concept as *ODS*, all multi-line directives such as #if-#else-#elseif, #foreach, and #macro must be used under conditions.

- The beginning and ending statements must be declared within a single text box. The figure below shows the sample of invalid usage of the multi-line #foreach statement between the text boxes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Invalid Usage of Multiline Statement in ODP.

Since the *ODP* template does not provide the statement order, the template will not be interpreted in the order of the displayed images. For example, $[uc.name](http://uc.name) may not be processed after #foreach($uc in $UseCase) has been completed.

The figure below shows the sample of valid usage of the **#foreach** statement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A sample of valid usage of the foreach statement.

- A VTL Macro must be declared within a single text box. Do not insert the multi-cell recorded macros in a single text box as shown in figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Invalid Usage of Macro Statement in ODP.

The text box does not have a sequence order; therefore, macros cannot record any content between the text boxes, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Valid Usage of Macro Statement in ODP.

#### Creating Data for Multiple Slides

*ODP* uses the **#forpage** directive to create a slide for each data. The **#forpage** directive does not contain any order. You can use **#forpage** and **#endpage** in any text boxes. All directives on the slide will be included within the **#forpage** statement, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A sample usage of the forpage directive.

The output from the code, shown in figure below, is an *ODP* with a single use case name for each slide.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The result of running the code from the figure above.

For more samples of *ODP* reports, see the “Other Document” template.

#### Creating Page with Conditions

The *ODP* directive does not provide any statement order; therefore, the #if directive will not be attached to the #forpage statement, as shown in figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A sample of Looping with Condition in General Style.

The codes in the figure above may not produce the report exactly as expected. #forpage automatically covers all directives in the current page without any statement order. Therefore, the #if directive may not be interpreted after #forpage.

To solve this problem you can use the $report.filterElement($elements, $types) method. This helper method provides the element filter for the specified type. The codes are shown in the figure below:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A sample of Looping with Conditional Filter.

For more details on $report.filterElement($elements, $types), see Section *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Helper tools' space='']*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

A text box is an image structure (An image structure keeps the position of each image in x, y coordinates).</p><p>You can change the position of a Text box. You can also place Text boxes in the same positions as others, see figure below.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="Sample of ODP Template" ac:alt="Sample of ODP Template"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show sample of ODP template.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) OpenDocument presentation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">A sample of ODP Template.</h6><p>Using the same concept as <em>ODS</em>, all multi-line directives such as #if-#else-#elseif, #foreach, and  #macro must be used under conditions.</p><ul><li>The beginning and ending statements must be declared within a single text box. Therefore, the #if directive may not be interpreted after #forpage.</p><p>To solve this problem you can use the $report.filterElement($elements, $types) method. This helper method provides the element filter for the specified type. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999654 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01318: (2026x Refresh1) OpenDocument spreadsheet

- page_id: `303999654`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999654/2026x+Refresh1+OpenDocument+spreadsheet
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:11.771+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Appendix C: OpenDocument format template
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All multi-line directives such as #if, #else, #elseif, #foreach, and #macro must be used under the following conditions:

1. The beginning and ending statements must be declared within a single cell. The two figures below show two samples of invalid usage of the multi-line statements between cells. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
A sample of an invalid multi-line statement. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Another sample of an invalid multi-line statement. Since the body of the **#if** statement is contained in the cell A2, this cell will not be generated if the condition is not true (the element type is not “usecase”). Due to the constraints of spreadsheet document structure, the number of cells in a column must be equal to the number of cells in all columns, and the number of cells in a row must be equal to the number of cells in all rows. 
 
The codes in the figure above will break the structure of a spreadsheet document. The two figures below demonstrate two samples of valid usage of the multi-line statements. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
A sample of a valid usage of multi-line statements. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Another sample of a valid usage of multi-line statements.
2. *VTL* Macro must be declared within a single cell. Do not insert the multi-cell recorded macros in a single cell, see figure below. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A sample of an invalid usage of a VTL macro.

The macro will copy all contents between **#macro** and **#end**. Cells and rows will be included in the macro as well. Once this record has been inserted, the macro content will break the document’s structure.

#### Creating Data for Multiple Rows

The **#foreach** directive can only be used in a single cell record. To create data for multiple rows, use the **#forrow** directive instead, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The #forrow directive being used to create data for multiple rows.

As shown in figure below, the output will be:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The results of running the code from the figure above.

#### Creating Data for Multiple Columns

**#forcol** is used to create data for multiple columns, see figure below. This statement can be used with **#forrow** (see the sample from the “Other Document” template).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The #forcol directive being used to create data for multiple columns.

From the usage of #forcol shown in figure below, the output will be:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The results of running the code from the figure above.

This statement can be used with <strong>#forrow</strong> (see the sample from the “Other Document” template).</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show the usage of #forcol.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) OpenDocument spreadsheet" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">The #forcol directive being used to create data for multiple columns.</h6><p>From the usage of #forcol shown in figure below, the output will be:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show the output of #forcol.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) OpenDocument spreadsheet" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">The results of running the code from the figure above.</h6>

## PAGE 01319: (2026x Refresh1) OpenDocument text

- page_id: `303999666`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999666/2026x+Refresh1+OpenDocument+text
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:12.004+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Appendix C: OpenDocument format template
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard supports most OpenDocument Text (*ODT*) features. It enables you to input the *VTL* codes inside meta-data, styles, and text content. All syntax usable inside *RTF* can be used with *ODT*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A sample of OpenDocument Text Converted From RTF Document.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Report Wizard supports most OpenDocument Text (<em>ODT</em>) features. It enables you to input the <em>VTL</em> codes inside meta-data, styles, and text content. All syntax usable inside <em>RTF</em> can be used with <em>ODT</em>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="Sample of OpenDocument Text Converted From RTF Document" ac:alt="Sample of OpenDocument Text Converted From RTF Document"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sample of OpenDocument Text converting from RTF document.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) OpenDocument text" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;">A sample of OpenDocument Text Converted From RTF Document.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992987 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01320: (2026x Refresh1) Opening diagrams

- page_id: `303992987`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992987/2026x+Refresh1+Opening+diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:41.636+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can open diagrams either in the same tab or in a new tab – just like in the most popular Internet browsers.

The benefits of using tabbed browsing include:

- Ability to analyze different aspects of the system (such as requirements, architecture, test cases, or domain models) in separate tabs. Both backward and forward navigation is supported for each aspect (tab) separately. Navigation does not open new tabs by default, which greatly helps in limiting the number of open tabs.
- Ability to compare separate views of the system side-by-side. For example, business and IT architectures can be displayed in two tabs side-by-side and analyzed by drilling down into their hierarchies. Navigation deeper into the structures will not open new tabs, which allows smooth analysis of the modeled system.
- Smooth work with multiple screens. Browsing in a tab on the second screen never opens diagrams on the first screen, which means you stay on the same screen. New tabs open on the same screen, to the right of the active one.

To open a diagram, do one of the following:

- Double-click the diagram.
- Select the diagram and press Enter.
- Right-click the diagram and then click Open in New Tab .
- Select the diagram and press Ctrl+Enter. The diagram opens on the top of the active tab.
- Right-click the diagram and then click Open . The diagram opens on the top of the active tab.

To open a diagram from the diagram pane:

- Double-click the model element to which the diagram is assigned. The new diagram opens in the same diagram tab.

To open a diagram from the content diagram (available in the Standart, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions):

If the diagram is added to the table of contents or a shape of the diagram is drawn on the diagram pane, do one of the following:

- Double-click the diagram shape. The diagram opens in the same diagram tab.
- On the diagram pane, right-click the diagram shape, and then click Open . The diagram opens in the same diagram tab.
- On the diagram pane, right-click the diagram shape and then click Open in New T ab .

To open a diagram from the Diagrams menu, do one of the following:

- On the main menu, click Diagrams and then Recently Opened . The Recently Opened Diagrams dialog opens. Double-click the diagram you want to open or press Enter.
- Press F12. The Recently Opened Diagrams dialog opens. Double-click the diagram you want to open or press Enter.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Recently Opened Diagrams dialog.

- In the Recently Opened Diagrams dialog, 50 recent diagrams are listed by the time they were activated.
- You can change the default diagram opening behavior in the Environment Options dialog, the General options group, under the Navigation category.
- Press the Ctrl key when opening a diagram to turn on the opposite diagram opening mode. For example, if, by default, opening in the same tab is active, by using the Ctrl key, the diagram will open in the new tab and vice versa.

To navigate backward and forward between diagrams in the same diagram tab, do one of the following

- On the diagram toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='back_diagram_toolbar.png'] or [ATTACHMENT filename='forward_diagram_toolbar.png'] button.
- Press Alt+Left Arrow or Alt+Right Arrow.

In some cases, you may need to open a model without loading any diagrams. For example, when the project cannot be loaded due to some problems linked to the active diagram loading.

To open a model without loading diagrams

1. Go to <modeling tool installation directory>\bin and open the properties file, e.g., <modeling tool name>.properties .
2. Add the following argument:

Alternatively, you can modify the properties file to load all the diagrams in the model.

To open a model with all the diagrams loaded

1. Go to <modeling tool installation directory>\bin and open the properties file, e.g., <modeling tool name>.properties .
2. Add the following argument:

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Diagramming' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can open diagrams either in the same tab or in a new tab – just like in the most popular Internet browsers.</p><p>The benefits of using tabbed browsing include:</p><ul><li data-uuid="956395dc-47ad-4c34-80f2-e24cd5b0e5c3">Ability to analyze different aspects of the system (such as requirements, architecture, test cases, or domain models) in separate tabs. Both backward and forward navigation is supported for each aspect (tab) separately. Navigation does not open new tabs by default, which greatly helps in limiting the number of open tabs.</li><li data-uuid="57925524-2e21-4b55-aa7c-b2709a0aebc5">Ability to compare separate views of the system side-by-side. For example, business and IT architectures can be displayed in two tabs side-by-side and analyzed by drilling down into their hierarchies. Navigation deeper into the structures will not open new tabs, which allows smooth analysis of the modeled system.</li><li data-uuid="07c90c66-6871-4382-b345-33e89ed87f23">Smooth work with multiple screens. Browsing in a tab on the second screen never opens diagrams on the first screen, which means you stay on the same screen. New tabs open on the same screen, to the right of the active one.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To open a diagram, do one of the following:</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="57dfb959-cebf-4887-a009-cb1c14221e5d">Double-click the diagram.</li><li data-uuid="cf59c9f6-28f3-456d-bc3b-07b2cbb2c359">Select the diagram and press Enter.</li><li data-uuid="bac3b65a-6672-49fc-863f-a237bb4b7357">Right-click the diagram and then click <strong>Open in New Tab</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="55feeb87-8135-4bb1-bc0f-4402be3af0ed">Select the diagram and press Ctrl+Enter. The diagram opens on the top of the active tab.</li><li data-uuid="dcf21625-fbe9-406c-ba5b-b6822e40fb45">Right-click the diagram and then click <strong>Open</strong>. The diagram opens on the top of the active tab.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To open a diagram from the diagram pane: </p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="d86c7248-7186-4478-9abd-3d69a7233d01">Double-click the model element to which the diagram is assigned. The new diagram opens in the same diagram tab.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To open a diagram from the content diagram (available in the Standart, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions): </p><hr /><p>If the diagram is added to the table of contents or a shape of the diagram is drawn on the diagram pane, do one of the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="1d9ca0e9-c03d-4d11-a53b-419903c09326">Double-click the diagram shape. The diagram opens in the same diagram tab.</li><li data-uuid="d78f66b7-13ae-4baa-a8d2-944ac383e38b">On the diagram pane, right-click the diagram shape, and then click <strong>Open</strong>. The diagram opens in the same diagram tab.</li><li data-uuid="e376bc14-5bb7-41df-8445-8d4de382536f">On the diagram pane, right-click the diagram shape and then click <strong>Open in New T</strong><strong>ab</strong>.<br /><br /><strong><br /></strong></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target">To open a diagram from the Diagrams menu, do one of the following:</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="def6894c-385c-4ac7-aa45-acd75cef3a85">On the main menu, click <strong>Diagrams</strong> and then <strong>Recently Opened</strong>. The <strong>Recently Opened </strong><strong>Diagrams</strong> dialog opens. Double-click the diagram you want to open or press Enter.</li><li data-uuid="1962e321-95c9-4ea3-bc80-31a04c3adf42">Press F12. The <strong>Recently Opened </strong><strong>Diagrams </strong>dialog opens. Double-click the diagram you want to open or press Enter.<br /><br /></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="recently_opened_diagrams_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Opening diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 class="auto-cursor-target" style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">The Recently Opened Diagrams dialog.</span></h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="94d5cfb0-ab85-4a38-9f63-db5bfea93b1c"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="f2df3436-46e8-4ffe-a9fa-b05cdb615d4f">In the <strong>Recently Opened Diagrams</strong> dialog, 50 recent diagrams are listed by the time they were activated.</li><li data-uuid="cb8eeb09-81d6-4a4d-b72c-fc6199286eb7">You can change the default diagram opening behavior in the <strong>Environment </strong><strong>Options </strong>dialog, the <strong>General </strong>options group, under the <strong>Navigation </strong>category.</li><li data-uuid="60dfecc4-711b-4b8a-864b-afc255d596cf">Press the Ctrl key when opening a diagram to turn on the opposite diagram opening mode. For example, if, by default, opening in the same tab is active, by using the Ctrl key, the diagram will open in the new tab and vice versa.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To navigate backward and forward between diagrams in the same diagram tab, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="32aa2cde-97d8-457d-9c99-4575c53282b4">On the diagram toolbar, click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="back_diagram_toolbar.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Opening diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>  or <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="forward_diagram_toolbar.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Opening diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</li><li data-uuid="a2b4ef73-2085-43ae-bb6c-d7885a1e2d0e">Press Alt+Left Arrow or Alt+Right Arrow.<br /><br /></li></ul><p>In some cases, you may need to open a model without loading any diagrams. For example, when the project cannot be loaded due to some problems linked to the active diagram loading. </p><p>To open a model without loading diagrams</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="bfbeec47-9bf2-4154-810a-6eacd6661183">Go to <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\bin</em> and open the properties file, e.g., <em>&lt;modeling tool name&gt;.properties</em>.</li><li data-uuid="f83ad136-daa3-478d-a2b6-0595142e2527"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Add the following argument:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d8e155cc-260a-4c3f-8d74-79839f2da553"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-Dmagicdraw.diagramLoadingMode=LOAD_MODE_NO_DIAGRAMS]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Alternatively, you can modify the properties file to load all the diagrams in the model. </p><p>To open a model with all the diagrams loaded</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="eeae51c6-a537-468b-b6ab-8d3bf5d48f87">Go to <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\bin</em> and open the properties file, e.g., <em>&lt;modeling tool name&gt;.properties</em>.</li><li data-uuid="8ac8332b-8107-4e46-b24a-1382b4c10731"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Add the following argument:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1975ad4-ad6a-42e2-ada4-1b89565af2f8"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-Dmagicdraw.diagramLoadingMode=LOAD_MODE_ALL]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="76e6e11a-fef6-4d3b-b735-09506be87c1b"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Diagramming" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996654 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01321: (2026x Refresh1) Opening hyperlinks

- page_id: `303996654`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996654/2026x+Refresh1+Opening+hyperlinks
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:12.979+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Manage Navigation > (2026x Refresh1) Defining hyperlinks
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To open hyperlinks in text

1. Hover your mouse pointer at the hyperlinked text.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Click the link.
  - Press Ctrl and click the link. The content of the hyperlink is opened.

To open hyperlinks on elements

1. Select the hyperlinked element on the diagram pane or in the Model Browser .
2. Do one of the following:
  - Double-click the element.
  - Click the icon which indicates that the element has a hyperlink.
  - Press and hold Ctrl and double click the element (or click the icon which indicates that the element has a hyperlink). If the hyperlink leads to a diagram, the diagram is opened in the new window. The content of the hyperlink is opened.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To open hyperlinks in text</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Hover your mouse pointer at the hyperlinked text.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Do one of the following:</span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click the link.</span></li><li>Press Ctrl and <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">click the link.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The content of the hyperlink is opened.</span></li></ul></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To open hyperlinks on elements</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the hyperlinked element <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">on the diagram pane or in the Model Browser</span>.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Double-click the element.</li><li>Click the icon which indicates that the element has a hyperlink.</li><li>Press and hold Ctrl and double click the element (or click the icon which indicates that the element has a hyperlink). If the hyperlink leads to a diagram, the diagram is opened in the new window.<br />The content of the hyperlink is opened.</li></ul></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992733 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01322: (2026x Refresh1) Opening projects

- page_id: `303992733`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992733/2026x+Refresh1+Opening+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:12.605+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

The native modeling tool format is **.mdzip* and **.mdxml*.

##### Opening previously created projects

To edit or review previously created projects, do one of the following

- From the File menu, select Open Project . In the Open dialog, select the project and click Open .
- On the main toolbar, click the Open Project button.
- Drag the project from the open window of your file system to the modeling tool. The project opens immediately.
- Double-click the project file with the *.mdxml or *.mdzip extension. A new modeling tool window opens.

To start the modeling tool with the last project you worked on, select **Options** > **Environment**>**General**>**Save/Load**and set the **Open Last Project on Startup** option to *true*.

XMI 2.4 / UML 2.4 is the main file format, used by the modeling tool for storing models. This format does not specify how to store diagrams, so the modeling tool stores and opens diagram data in XMI extension sections. Thus if you use the modeling tool to open an XMI file exported from another UML tool, only the model will be opened, but not the diagrams or views. 
Accordingly, if you open a modeling tool file in another UML tool, diagrams or views will not be opened in that tool (unless the tool supports MagicDraw-specific file extensions). 
You may open more than one project within the same modeling tool window. A separate workspace will be created for each opened project.

##### Opening projects saved with modeling tool 17.0 or earlier

Projects saved with modeling tool 17.0 or earlier can be as well opened with modeling tool of later versions. 
To update older models, simply open them with modeling tool of later versions and then re-save them. Models will be automatically converted to the latest format. 
In case your model is stored in a project that uses other projects, you should open and re-save the used projects first and only then do the same with the main project. Be advised that having only the used projects successfully converted to the latest format, you may expect the smooth conversion of the project that uses them. 
 
 
If you convert only the project and leave the used projects saved in an old format, you will not be able to modify the projects used in the *read-write* accessibility mode. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

A new modeling tool window opens.</li></ul><p class="atl-forced-newline"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="459cc95d-b8f8-4edc-9f38-8f9fd9452013"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To start the modeling tool with the last project you worked on, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment </strong>&gt;<strong> General </strong>&gt;<strong> Save/Load </strong>and set the <strong>Open Last Project on Startup</strong> option to <em>true</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="atl-forced-newline"><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">XMI 2.4 / UML 2.4 is the ma</span>in file format, used by the modeling tool for storing models. This format does not specify how to store diagrams, so the modeling tool stores and opens diagram data in XMI extension sections. Thus if you use the modeling tool to open an XMI file exported from another UML tool, only the model will be opened, but not the diagrams or views. <br class="atl-forced-newline" />Accordingly, if you open a modeling tool file in another UML tool, diagrams or views will not be opened in that tool (unless the tool supports MagicDraw-specific file extensions). <br class="atl-forced-newline" />You may open more than one project within the same modeling tool window. A separate workspace will be created for each opened project.</p><h3 class="atl-forced-newline"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Opening projects saved with modeling tool 17.0 or earlier</span></h3><p class="atl-forced-newline">Projects saved with modeling tool 17.0 or earlier can be as well opened with modeling tool of later versions. <br class="atl-forced-newline" />To update older models, simply open them with modeling tool of later versions and then re-save them. Models will be automatically converted to the latest format. <br class="atl-forced-newline" />In case your model is stored in a project that uses other projects, you should open and re-save the used projects first and only then do the same with the main project. Be advised that having only the used projects successfully converted to the latest format, you may expect the smooth conversion of the project that uses them. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />If you convert only the project and leave the used projects saved in an old format, you will not be able to modify the projects used in the <em>read-write</em> accessibility mode. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:title="Used Projects dialog showing options of non-converted project usage in converted project" ac:alt="Used Projects dialog showing options of non-converted project usage in converted project" ac:height="334" ac:width="599"><ri:attachment ri:filename="used-projects-dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Opening projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998395 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01323: (2026x Refresh1) Opening template editor

- page_id: `303998395`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998395/2026x+Refresh1+Opening+template+editor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:38.913+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard template editor
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After installation has been completed, the Template Editor menu will appear on the Microsoft Word menu bar, see figure below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Microsoft Word 2003 Template Editor Menu.

###### How to open Template Editor, either

- On the Microsoft Word 2000 – 2003 menu, click ReportWizard > Template Editor , see figure above, or
- On the Microsoft Word 2007 menu, click Add-Ins > ReportWizard > Template Editor , see figure below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Microsoft Word 2007 Template Editor Menu.

The macro-enabled option in Microsoft Word is required to open Template Editor.

When Template Editor is open, the Report Wizard Template Editor dialog will open.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Report Wizard Template Editor Dialog.

The table below shows the Report Wizard Template Editor Options

| Name | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Search box | Filter a list of fields. Only fields that contain a keyword of search as part of their names can be shown in the (4) List of fields. |
| Clear search results | Clear the current search result. |
| Categories combo box | Select categories of fields. Fields are shown in (4) List of fields according to their categories. |
| List of fields | Show a list of fields. Double-click a field name to insert the code. |
| Display all available fields check box | Show all fields, otherwise it will show only commonly used fields. |
| Insert button | Insert a code for a selected field into the document. |
| Show/Hide description button | Show or hide (8) description pane. |
| Description pane | Show the description of a selected field. |
| Import button | Import a data file. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992300 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01324: (2026x Refresh1) Operational

- page_id: `303992300`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992300/2026x+Refresh1+Operational
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:51.624+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

833387955

833387957

833387956

The Operational (Op) domain illustrates the Logical Architecture of the enterprise. It describes the requirements, operational behavior, structure, and exchanges required to support (exhibit) capabilities. The Operational (Op) view defines all operational elements in an implementation/solution independent manner.

833387954

**Views**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc7ad2fd-c812-4a5b-ad5e-0cf5bc59cb53"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">833387955</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6bce9a7b-5888-48a4-a7a9-f6d875465b8d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">833387957</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae046c1e-30e6-45a7-bbdf-22ab636f93c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">833387956</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">The Operational (Op) domain illustrates the Logical Architecture of the enterprise. It describes the requirements, operational behavior, structure, and exchanges required to support (exhibit) capabilities. The Operational (Op) view defines all operational elements in an implementation/solution independent manner.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c6d71343-1276-45c5-bbc4-43a8ccbf9d12"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">833387954</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7811d427-fe68-4a3e-9c48-fbfeda77e7a2" /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994786 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01325: (2026x Refresh1) Operational Connectivity

- page_id: `303994786`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994786/2026x+Refresh1+Operational+Connectivity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:48.558+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

833436122

833436124

833436123

**Description**

The Operational Connectivity (Op-Cn) table summarizes Operational Exchanges between Operational Performers of information, systems, personnel, energy etc. and the Operational Activities that produces and consumes them. Measurements can optionally be included.

The Operational Connectivity Internal diagram describes a logical pattern of resource (information, funding, personnel, or materiel) internal flows.

**Implementation**

The Operational Connectivity (Op-Cn) is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Connectivity diagram' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Connectivity Table' space=''] . An Operational Connectivity Table represents exchanges between Operational Performers.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Role-based Connectivity Table' space='']. A role-based table represents exchanges between Operational Roles.
- Reports: 
- Operational Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet (.xlsx). 
- Operational Role-based Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet (.xlsx).

**Samples**

###### **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**An example of the Operational Connectivity diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Operational Connectivity Table

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Operational Role-basedConnectivity Table

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994855 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01326: (2026x Refresh1) Operational Constraints

- page_id: `303994855`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994855/2026x+Refresh1+Operational+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:50.238+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

833654917

833654919

833654918

**Description**

The Operational Constraints (Op-Ct) view concerns defining operational limitations, constraints and performance parameters for the enterprise. It specifies traditional textual operational or business rules that are constraints on the way that business is done in the enterprise. The addition of SysML parametrics provides a computational means of defining operational constraints across the enterprise or within a specific operational context.

The intended usage of the Operational Constraints (Op-Ct) view includes:

- Definition of doctrinally correct operational procedures.
- Definition of business rules.
- Identification of operational constraints.

**Implementation**

The Operational Constraints (Op-Ct) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Constraints table' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Constraints Definition diagram' space=''] .
- Operational Constraints Excel Spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Operational Constraints table

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the **Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

833654916

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Constraint' space='MED']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000052 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01327: (2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog

- page_id: `304000052`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000052/2026x+Refresh1+Operational+Exchange+Manager+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:42.450+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Dialogs
- labels: ['operational-exchange-manager-dialog', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Operational Exchange Manager** dialog enables the analysis and management of the Operational Exchanges data. You can:

- Edit existing Operational Exchanges by adding new Operational Exchange Items
- Realize or ignore existing Operational Exchanges on selected relationships
- Change the Operational Exchange Item 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Review producing and consuming Operational Activity pairs and remove the ones that are not needed
- Review the list of diagrams that display the Realizing Elements of the selected Exchange

To open an **Operational Exchange Manager** dialog

1. Selectan Association, Operational Connector, Operational Control Flow, Operational Object Flow, or Operational Message in the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator, click [ATTACHMENT filename='op_ech_manager_manipulator.png'] . The Operational Exchange Manager dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The Affected Diagrams button opens the list of diagrams that are affected by the selected Realized Operational Exchange.
- The All Affected Diagrams button opens the list of diagrams that are affected by all the Realized Exchanges in the Operational Exchange Manager dialog.

To learn more about previewing affected diagrams, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Preview Affected Diagrams dialog in UAF' space=''].

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Preview Affected Diagrams dialog in UAF' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Connectivity diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Connectivity Table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Constraints Definition diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Sequences diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Operational Exchanges in L1 Node Types diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Operational Exchanges in L2 Logical Internal Scenario diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Operational Exchanges in L4 Logical Activities diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Operational Exchanges in L6 Logical Sequence diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 Internal diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-5b diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-6c diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Operational Exchanges in NOV-2 diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying possible Operational Exchanges on the selected Operational Activity Edge' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995110 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01328: (2026x Refresh1) Operational Information Op-If

- page_id: `303995110`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995110/2026x+Refresh1+Operational+Information+Op-If
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:09.608+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

905585431

905585433

905585432

**Description**

The Operational Information (Op-If) view addresses the information perspective on operational architecture. It allows analysis of an architecture’s information and data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation specific issues.

The Conceptual Data Model diagram defines the high-level information elements used in the operational scenarios.

It is used for documenting the business information requirements and structural business process rules of the architecture. It describes the information that is associated with the information of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships.

**Cameo Data Modeler plugin integration**

You can use the Entity Relationship diagram for conceptual data modeling in the Conceptual Data Model diagram. It allows for using the information engineering notation within this view.

**Implementation**

The Operational Information (Op-If) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Strategic Information diagram' space=''] . It is used to document the business information requirements and structural business process rules of the architecture. It describes the information that is associated with the information of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships. The intended usage of the Conceptual Data Model diagram includes: - Information requirements. - Information hierarchy.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Entity Relationship Diagram' space=''] . It supports the information engineering notation.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Conceptual Data Model diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Logical Data Model diagram

905585429

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Implements' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000451 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01329: (2026x Refresh1) Operational Motivation

- page_id: `304000451`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000451/2026x+Refresh1+Operational+Motivation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:12.359+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The**Operational Motivation** (Rq-Mv) view provides a central reference for a set of stakeholder needs expressed as requirements, their relationship (via traceability) to more detailed requirements, and the solution described by the architecture that will meet those requirements. It is used to represent requirements, their properties, and their relationships (trace, verify, satisfy, refine) both to each other and to UAF architectural elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The<strong> Operational Motivation</strong> (Rq-Mv) view provides a central reference for a set of stakeholder needs expressed as requirements, their relationship (via traceability) to more detailed requirements, and the solution described by the architecture that will meet those requirements. It is used to represent requirements, their properties, and their relationships (trace, verify, satisfy, refine) both to each other and to UAF architectural elements.</p>
````

**Description**

The Operational Parameters (Op-Pm) domain depicts the measurements of resources.

The intended usage of the Operational Parameters (Op-Pm) domain includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measurements.
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

The Operational Parameters (Op-Pm) domain is represented by:

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992302 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01331: (2026x Refresh1) Operational Processes

- page_id: `303992302`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992302/2026x+Refresh1+Operational+Processes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:52.121+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Operational Processes (Op-Pr) view concerns capturing activity based behavior and flows. It describes the activities that are normally conducted in the course of achieving business goals that support a capability. It describes operational activities, their inputs/outputs, operational activity actions and flows between them.

The intended usage of the Operational Processes (Op-Pr) view includes:

- Description of activities and workflows.
- Requirements capture.
- Definition of roles and responsibilities.
- Support task analysis to determine training needs.
- Problem space definition.
- Operational planning.
- Logistic support analysis.
- Information flow analysis.

****

**Implementation**

The Operational Processes (Op-Pr) view is represented using:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Processes diagram' space=''] . It represents Operational Activity hierarchies.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Process Flow diagram' space=''] . It represents Operational Activity flows.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Mission Threads diagram' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Mission Threads Flow diagram' space=''] .
- Operational Process Flow (BPD) diagram. It is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD) and describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the business work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed business activities. An Operational Process Flow (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the operational process

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the operational process flow

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Measurement Set' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Service' space='MED']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994939 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01332: (2026x Refresh1) Operational Sequences

- page_id: `303994939`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994939/2026x+Refresh1+Operational+Sequences
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:55.185+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

908539195

908539198

908539197

**Description**

The Operational Sequences (Op-Sq) express a time ordered examination of the operational exchanges as a result of a particular operational scenario. It provides a time-ordered analysis of the operational exchanges between participating nodes (Operational Performer roles) as a result of a particular operational scenario.

Operational Event/Trace Descriptions, sometimes called sequence diagrams, event scenarios, or timing diagrams, allow the tracing of actions in a scenario or critical sequence of events.

The intended usage of the Operational Sequences (Op-Sq) view includes:

- Analysis of operational events.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional user requirements.
- Operational test scenarios.

**Implementation**

The Operational Sequences (Op-Sq) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Sequences diagram' space=''] .
- Operational Sequences (BPD) diagram. It is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD) and describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the business work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed business activities. An Operational Sequences (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Operational Sequences diagram

908539194

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Message' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='MED']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Role' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity in UAF' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Method' space='MED']

It provides a time-ordered analysis of the operational exchanges between participating nodes (Operational Performer roles) as a result of a particular operational scenario.</p><p>Operational Event/Trace Descriptions, sometimes called sequence diagrams, event scenarios, or timing diagrams, allow the tracing of actions in a scenario or critical sequence of events.</p><p>The intended usage of the Operational Sequences (Op-Sq) view includes:</p><ul><li>Analysis of operational events.</li><li>Behavioral analysis.</li><li>Identification of non-functional user requirements.</li><li>Operational test scenarios.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Operational Sequences (Op-Sq) view is represented by:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Sequences diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Sequences diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Operational Sequences (BPD) diagram. It is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD) and describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities. An Operational Sequences (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Operational Interaction Scenario" ac:alt="Operational Interaction Scenario"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op_int_scenario.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Operational Sequences" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Operational Sequences diagram</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c1f73478-8600-4fa0-b942-dc42b3682033"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">908539194</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Message" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Exchange" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Performer" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Role" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Activity in UAF" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Method" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="42f16e4f-8cf1-4b3e-b743-a3ea8c5614a6" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997292 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01333: (2026x Refresh1) Operational States

- page_id: `303997292`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997292/2026x+Refresh1+Operational+States
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:00.404+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

909091961

909091963

909091962

**Description**

The Operational States (Op-St) view captures the state-based behavior of an operational Operational Performer. The Operational States diagram models how and why an element changes in response to the environment. The diagram represents the sets of events to which the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

An Operational States view (Op-St) can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of activities or work flow in the business process. It is particularly useful for describing critical sequencing of behaviors and timing of operational activities that cannot be adequately described in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Process Flow diagram' space='']. The Operational States (Op-St) view relates events and states. A change of state is called a transition. Actions may be associated with a given state or with the transition between states in response to stimuli (e.g., triggers and events).

The intended usage of the Operational States (Op-St) view includes:

- Analysis of business events.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of constraints.

**Implementation**

The Operational States (Op-St) view is represented by the Operational States diagram that is based on a UML State Machine diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Operational States diagram

909091960

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Agent' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational State Description' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Performer' space='MED']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors' space='']

**Related diagrams**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) State Machine diagram' space='']

The diagram represents the sets of events to which the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.</p><p>An Operational States view (Op-St) can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of activities or work flow in the business process. It is particularly useful for describing critical sequencing of behaviors and timing of operational activities that cannot be adequately described in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Process Flow diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Process Flow diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The Operational States (Op-St) view relates events and states. A change of state is called a transition. Actions may be associated with a given state or with the transition between states in response to stimuli (e.g., triggers and events).</p><p>The intended usage of the Operational States (Op-St) view includes:</p><ul><li>Analysis of business events.</li><li>Behavioral analysis.</li><li>Identification of constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Operational States (Op-St) view is represented by the Operational States diagram that is based on a UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Operational States" ac:alt="Operational States"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_states.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Operational States" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Operational States diagram</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ccff8f14-0d61-4fc1-b122-abc47e62a012"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">909091960</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Agent" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational State Description" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Performer" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors" /></ac:link><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) State Machine diagram" /></ac:link><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

**Description**

An Operational Structure (Op-Sr) view identifies the operational exchange requirements between nodes. It defines operational architecture and exchange requirements necessary to support a specific set of Capabilities.

**Implementation**

An Operational Structure (Op-Sr) view is represented by the UML Class diagram.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994962 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01335: (2026x Refresh1) Operational Taxonomy

- page_id: `303994962`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994962/2026x+Refresh1+Operational+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:56.815+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

911176512

911176514

911176513

**Description**

The Operational Taxonomy (Op-Tx) shows the main Operational Performers of the architectural scenario. It also illustrates the flows of information and materiel between these Operational Performers specified in the Operational Information model.

The **Operational **High Level**Taxonomy** diagram illustrates the primary scenario for which the architecture is intended.

**Implementation**

The Operational Taxonomy (Op-Tx) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Taxonomy diagram' space=''] . The primary purpose of the Operational Taxonomy diagram is to define capability requirements within an operational context. It may also be used to express a capability boundary. The Operational Taxonomy diagram can be used to show flows of funding, personnel and materiel in addition to information. The intended usage of the Operational Taxonomy diagram includes:
  - Definition of operational concepts.
  - Elaboration of capability requirements.
  - Definition of collaboration needs.
  - Applying a local context to a capability.
  - Problem space definition.
  - Operational planning.
  - Supply chain analysis.
  - Allocation of activities to resources.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Taxonomy table' space=''] . This table can be used to create and describe major domain elements faster.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram' space=''] . It describes a mission, class of mission, or scenario and shows the main operational concepts and interesting or unique aspects of operations. With a help of a Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram you can describe the interactions between the subject architecture and its environment, and between the architecture and external systems. The intended usage of the Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram includes:
  - 
    - Putting an operational situation or scenario into context.
    - Providing a tool for discussion and presentation; for example, aids industry engagement in acquisition.
    - Providing an aggregate illustration of the details within the published high-level organization of more detailed information in published architectures.
- Operational Free Form Taxonomy diagram. This diagram can be used alternatively to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram' space='']

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Operational Taxonomy diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Operational Taxonomy Table

911176511

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Asset' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='MED']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca1d9cd4-2146-4501-af92-772ad423302e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">911176512</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec03fde3-6114-4477-b628-ddd598ef3d70"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">911176514</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="151b6801-6702-48ad-8929-165cfb326442"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">911176513</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Operational Taxonomy (Op-Tx) shows the main Operational Performers of the architectural scenario. It also illustrates the flows of information and materiel between these Operational Performers specified in the Operational Information model.</p><p>The <strong>Operational <strong>High Level </strong>Taxonomy</strong> diagram illustrates the primary scenario for which the architecture is intended.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Operational Taxonomy (Op-Tx) view is represented by:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Taxonomy diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Taxonomy diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The primary purpose of the Operational Taxonomy diagram is to define capability requirements within an operational context. It may also be used to express a capability boundary. The Operational Taxonomy diagram can be used to show flows of funding, personnel and materiel in addition to information.<br /><p>The intended usage of the Operational Taxonomy diagram includes:</p><ul><li>Definition of operational concepts.</li><li>Elaboration of capability requirements.</li><li>Definition of collaboration needs.</li><li>Applying a local context to a capability.</li><li>Problem space definition.</li><li>Operational planning.</li><li>Supply chain analysis.</li><li>Allocation of activities to resources.</li></ul></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Taxonomy table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Taxonomy table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. This table can be used to create and describe major domain elements faster.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It describes a mission, class of mission, or scenario and  shows the main operational concepts and interesting or unique aspects of operations. With a help of a Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram you can describe the interactions between the subject architecture and its environment, and between the architecture and external systems. <br /><p>The intended usage of the Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram includes:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>Putting an operational situation or scenario into context.</li><li>Providing a tool for discussion and presentation; for example, aids industry engagement in acquisition.</li><li>Providing an aggregate illustration of the details within the published high-level organization of more detailed information in published architectures.</li></ul></li></ul></li><li>Operational Free Form Taxonomy diagram. This diagram can be used alternatively to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Operational Structure" ac:alt="Operational Structure"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_structure.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Operational Taxonomy" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Operational H<span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">igh Level T</span>axonomy diagram</h6><p><ac:image ac:title="Operational Taxonomy" ac:alt="Operational Taxonomy"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_taxonomy.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Operational Taxonomy" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Operational Taxonomy diagram</h6><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_taxonomy_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Operational Taxonomy" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Operational Taxonomy Table</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="15385474-2640-4445-80ca-2fdd081863bd"><ac:parameter 1112822297

1112822299

1112822298

**Description**

The Operational Traceability describes the mapping between the capabilities required by an Enterprise and the supporting operational activities and operational agents.

**Implementation**

The Operational Traceability is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Performers to Capabilities Mapping Matrix' space='']. It shows the mapping between the capabilities required and the operational performers that those capabilities support.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Activities to Capabilities Mapping Matrix' space='']. It shows the mapping between the capabilities required and the operational activities that those capabilities support.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Operational Performers to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Operational Activities to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990644 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01337: (2026x Refresh1) Operational viewpoint

- page_id: `303990644`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990644/2026x+Refresh1+Operational+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:50.759+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0
- labels: ['operational-viewpoint', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

306262672

306262674

306262673

“DoDAF-described Models in the Operational Viewpoint describe the tasks and activities, operational elements, and resource flow exchanges required to conduct operations. A pure operational model is materiel independent. However, operations and their relationships may be influenced by new technologies, such as collaboration technology, where process improvements are in practice before policy can reflect the new procedures. There may be some cases, as well, in which it is necessary to document the way activities are performed, given the restrictions of current systems, to examine ways in which new systems could facilitate streamlining the activities. In such cases, operational models may have materiel constraints and requirements that need to be addressed. For this reason, it may be necessary to include some high-level system architectural data to augment information onto the operational models.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

The basic modeling principles are demonstrated in the demo **[*UPDM: Operational viewpoint*](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jrpPuZ8Vf74).

Some elements in the demo are different from UAF Plugin but the modeling principles are the same.

306262671

**Views**

A pure operational model is materiel independent. There may be some cases, as well, in which it is necessary to document the way activities are performed, given the restrictions of current systems, to examine ways in which new systems could facilitate streamlining the activities. In such cases, operational models may have materiel constraints and requirements that need to be addressed. For this reason, it may be necessary to include some high-level system architectural data to augment information onto the operational models.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]</p><p>The basic modeling principles are demonstrated in the demo <em><span class="watch-title" title="UPDM: Strategic (part 1 of 2)"> </span></em><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jrpPuZ8Vf74"><em><span class="watch-title" title="UPDM: Strategic (part 1 of 2)">UPDM: Operational viewpoint</span></em><span class="watch-title" title="UPDM: Strategic (part 1 of 2)"> </span></a><span class="watch-title" title="UPDM: Strategic (part 1 of 2)">.</span><span class="watch-title" title="UPDM: Strategic (part 1 of 2)"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d542d52-a4eb-482e-aad4-bc504a788e27"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Some elements in the demo are different from UAF Plugin but the modeling principles are the same.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3cbb6a5a-7525-444d-a8c8-905c04da1561"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">306262671</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="96cb6c1a-2a36-41c1-b537-560cfa60e460" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
