<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989895 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01338: (2026x Refresh1) Oracle database modeling extensions

- page_id: `303989895`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303989895/2026x+Refresh1+Oracle+database+modeling+extensions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:18:31.543+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Data-related modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Database support > (2026x Refresh1) Database modeling
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When the Oracle flavor is chosen for database top level element (schema or database), additional Oracle extensions are brought in. Elements that are in the scope of this schema or database element obtain additional Oracle-specific properties in the Specification windows (under the separate **Oracle**property group). These properties carry an additional information, that is then used when generating DDL scripts for Oracle.

Most often there is just one **Additional Properties**property - allowing entering free-form text that is then used when generating (this can be used to specify any extension texts - such as tablespace options for tables).

Oracle extensions provide means to model synonyms. Synonym is mapped as follows.

- Element of the same type (that is, table, materialized view, stored procedure, sequence) as the one being aliased is created. It is stereotyped as appropriate, but have no other data - just its name is important.
- Additionally, stereotype «OraSynonym» will be applied on the element. It has ref:Element[1] tag for pointing to the element being aliased. Synonyms of synonyms are handled in the same way.

Oracle extensions provide means to model materialized views. Materialized view can be created from Oracle SQL diagram. It is an ordinary SQL view, but with the additional «OraMaterializedView» stereotype applied (in diagrams, a shortened keyword «materialized» is used for the sake of compactness).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When the Oracle flavor is chosen for database top level element (schema or database), additional Oracle extensions are brought in. Elements that are in the scope of this schema or database element obtain additional Oracle-specific properties in the Specification windows (under the separate <strong>Oracle </strong>property group). These properties carry an additional information, that is then used when generating DDL scripts for Oracle.</p><p>Most often there is just one <strong>Additional Properties </strong>property - allowing entering free-form text that is then used when generating (this can be used to specify any extension texts - such as tablespace options for tables).</p><p>Oracle extensions provide means to model synonyms. Synonym is mapped as follows.</p><ul><li>Element of the same type (that is, table, materialized view, stored procedure, sequence) as the one being aliased is created. It is stereotyped as appropriate, but have no other data - just its name is important.</li><li>Additionally, stereotype «OraSynonym»  will be applied on the element. It has ref:Element[1] tag for pointing to the element being aliased. Synonyms of synonyms are handled in the same way.</li></ul><p>Oracle extensions provide means to model materialized views. Materialized view can be created from Oracle SQL diagram. It is an ordinary SQL view, but with the additional «OraMaterializedView» stereotype applied (in diagrams, a shortened keyword «materialized» is used for the sake of compactness).</p>
````

1487876040

1487876041

1487876041

**Description**

An Organization Structure Diagram represents the hierarchical structure of an organization. This diagram allows showing organization departments, roles inside departments, and actual persons inside an organization. The diagram can also represent the reporting structure of an organization.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Organization Structure diagram

1487876040

**Related elements**

- Resource
- Organization Unit
- Role
- Person
- Information system
- Composition

## PAGE 01340: (2026x Refresh1) Organization Unit

- page_id: `303989654`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303989654/2026x+Refresh1+Organization+Unit
- version_number: 2

- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1487944659

1487944661

1487944661

**Description**

An organization represents a group of persons, associated for a particular purpose. An Element may denote an organization, a department, or a working group inside an organization. e. g., the company, sale department, etc. This element is combined with the Participant definition.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1487944659

**Related elements**

- Resource
- Role
- Person
- Information system
- Pool and Lane
- Activities
- Choreography Activities

**Related diagrams**

- Organization Structure Diagram
- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram
- BPMN Resources Usage Matrices

**Related Procedure**

- Using Organization Structure Diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

definition.</p><p><strong>Notation</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="organization_unit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Organization Unit" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997588 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01341: (2026x Refresh1) Organizing rows

- page_id: `303997588`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997588/2026x+Refresh1+Organizing+rows
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:27.245+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Tables > (2026x Refresh1) Basic tasks in tables > (2026x Refresh1) Working with rows
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Sorting rows

You can change the order of rows in the following ways:

- 
- 

sortTo sort rows by a selected column

- Click the header of the column by which you want to sort the rows in the table. A small arrow showing the automatic sort order appears on the column header. Clicking the header once sorts the rows in ascending order. Clicking the header twice sorts the rows in descending order. All the rows in the table are automatically renumbered after sorting. [ATTACHMENT filename='instance_table_column_sort_orders.png']

multisortTo sort rows by multiple columns

- Press Ctrl + click on the first column header you want to sort the rows in the table. Repeat this action for the next column header you want to sort the rows in the table. A minimum of two column headers should be selected to multi-sort rows. A small arrow with a number showing the automatic multi-sort order appears on the column header. All the rows in the table are automatically renumbered after multi-sorting. [ATTACHMENT filename='multi_sort.png']

##### Moving rows up and down

To move a row up or down

1. Select the row.
2. Do either: The selected row, as well as associated rows, are automatically renumbered after moving.
  - On the table toolbar, click the Up or Down button appropriately.
  - Right-click the row and select the Up or Down command appropriately.
  - Press Ctrl+Up Arrow or Ctrl+Down Arrow appropriately.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Sorting rows</h3><p>You can change the order of rows in the following ways:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="sort"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Sort the rows by one column.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="multisort"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Sort rows by multiple columns.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01118a60-88b9-4fd3-a851-de20f95f8ed3"><ac:parameter ac:name="">sort</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To sort rows by a selected column</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the header of the column by which you want to sort the rows in the table. A small arrow showing the automatic sort order appears on the column header. Clicking the header once sorts the rows in ascending order. Clicking the header twice sorts the rows in descending order. All the rows in the table are automatically renumbered after sorting.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Sort order identification on columns" ac:alt="Sort order identification on columns"><ri:attachment ri:filename="instance_table_column_sort_orders.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Organizing rows" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e3578331-63d5-49cb-980f-fad2d53e7d46"><ac:parameter ac:name="">multisort</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To sort rows by multiple columns</p><hr /><ul><li>Press Ctrl + click on the first column header you want to sort the rows in the table. Repeat this action for the next column header you want to sort the rows in the table. A minimum of two column headers should be selected to multi-sort rows. A small arrow with a number showing the automatic multi-sort order appears on the column header. All the rows in the table are automatically renumbered after multi-sorting.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="multi_sort.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Organizing rows" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><br /></p><h3>Moving rows up and down</h3><p>To move a row up or down</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the row.</li><li>Do either:<br /><ul><li>On the table toolbar, click the <strong>Up</strong> or <strong>Down</strong> button appropriately.</li><li>Right-click the row and select the <strong>Up</strong> or <strong>Down</strong> command appropriately.</li><li>Press Ctrl+Up Arrow or Ctrl+Down Arrow appropriately.</li></ul>The selected row, as well as associated rows, are automatically renumbered after moving.</li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to Top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999730 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01342: (2026x Refresh1) Organizing your model

- page_id: `303999730`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999730/2026x+Refresh1+Organizing+your+model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:13.875+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before modeling your system, we strongly recommend that you organize your model structure. The correct organization of packages, diagrams, model libraries, and profiles allows you to reuse them easily, ensures quick navigation, control the access, manage configurations, etc.

For more details read the following pages:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Before modeling your system, we strongly recommend that you organize your model structure. The correct organization of packages, diagrams, model libraries, and profiles allows you to reuse them easily, ensures quick navigation, control the access, manage configurations, etc.</p><p>For more details read the following pages:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7659d5dc-c5b5-41b7-ae44-80916f88c458" /></p>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

| Property Name | Description | Applied For: | Reference Through: | Value elements type | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Base Classifier, All General Classifiers | The Base Classifier property gathers classifiers from which the current element directly inherits. The All General Classifiers property gathers classifiers from which the current element directly or indirectly inherits. | Classifier | Relationships: Generalization. | Classifier | Example |
| Participates in Activity | The property shows either the activities wherein the classifier is used as an object type, or the activities that are owned by the classifier. | Classifier | Property Chain | Activity | Example |
| Participates in Interaction | The property shows either the interactions wherein the classifier is used as an object type, or the interactions that are owned by the classifier. | Classifier | Property Chain | Interaction | Example |
| Describing Behavior | The property gathers behavior diagrams (state, activity, sequence, communication, and interaction) that describe the use case. | Use Case | Properties: ownedBehavior Tags: Exceptional Flow of Events Diagrams, Basic Flow of Events Diagrams, Alternative Flow of Events Diagrams. | State, Activity, Sequence, Communication, and Interaction Diagrams | Example |
| Specific Classifier | The property shows directly inherited classifiers. | Classifier | Relationships: Generalizations. | Classifier | Example |
| All Specific Classifiers | The property gathers directly or indirectly inherited classifiers. | Classifier | Relationships: Generalization. | Classifier | Example |
| Used Classifier | The property gathers what classifiers are used in the activity as object types, or the classifier that owns the activity. | Activity | Property Chain | Classifier | Example |
| Used Classifier | The property gathers what classifiers are used in the activity as object types, or the classifier that owns the activity. | InterAction | Property Chain | Classi | Example |
| Described Use Case | The property shows the described use cases by behavior or behavior diagram. | State, Activity, Sequence, Communication, and Interaction Diagrams | Properties: ownedBehavior Tags: Exceptional Flow of Events Diagrams, Basic Flow of Events Diagrams, Alternative Flow of Events Diagrams. | Use Case | Example |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991569 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01345: (2026x Refresh1) OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic

- page_id: `303991569`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991569/2026x+Refresh1+OV-1+High-Level+Operational+Concept+Graphic
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:50.588+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-1-high-level-operational-concept-graphic', 'ov-1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

306325535

306325538

306325536

**Description**

The OV-1 describes a mission, class of mission, or scenario. It shows the main operational concepts and interesting or unique aspects of operations. It describes the interactions between the subject architecture and its environment, and between the architecture and external systems. The OV-1 is the pictorial representation of the written content of the AV-1 Overview and Summary Information. Graphics alone are not sufficient for capturing the necessary architectural data.

The OV-1 provides a graphical depiction of what the architecture is about and an idea of the players and operations involved. An OV-1 can be used to orient and focus detailed discussions. Its main use is to aid human communication, and it is intended for presentation to high-level decision-makers.

The intended usage of the OV-1 includes:

- Putting an operational situation or scenario into context.
- Providing a tool for discussion and presentation; for example, aids industry engagement in acquisition.
- Providing an aggregate illustration of the details within the published high-level organization of more detailed information in published architectures.

**Implementation**

OV-1 can be represented using:

- OV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='ov_1.png']
- OV-1 Free Form diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- Link to an external document.
- UML Composite Structure Diagram.

306325534

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='High Level Operational Concept' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Concept Role' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="af4548cb-5c4a-45a8-94b0-580c40e6430a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">306325535</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67c9cdc7-1ccd-4a58-a3a7-d48b20f4fe65"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">306325538</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="507d7b87-9074-4889-a9e7-2fe7ce2afccf"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">306325536</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The OV-1 describes a mission, class of mission, or scenario. It shows the main operational concepts and interesting or unique aspects of operations. It describes the interactions between the subject architecture and its environment, and between the architecture and external systems. The OV-1 is the pictorial representation of the written content of the AV-1 Overview and Summary Information. Graphics alone are not sufficient for capturing the necessary architectural data.</p><p>The OV-1 provides a graphical depiction of what the architecture is about and an idea of the players and operations involved. An OV-1 can be used to orient and focus detailed discussions. Its main use is to aid human communication, and it is intended for presentation to high-level decision-makers.</p><p>The intended usage of the OV-1 includes:</p><ul><li>Putting an operational situation or scenario into context.</li><li>Providing a tool for discussion and presentation; for example, aids industry engagement in acquisition.</li><li>Providing an aggregate illustration of the details within the published high-level organization of more detailed information in published architectures.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>OV-1 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>OV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic" ac:alt="OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ov_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>OV-1 Free Form diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li><p>Link to an external document.</p></li><li><p>UML Composite Structure Diagram.</p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5451aa2-f71c-462b-809f-c02b93cefd42"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">306325534</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="High Level Operational Concept" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Concept Role" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991564 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01346: (2026x Refresh1) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description

- page_id: `303991564`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991564/2026x+Refresh1+OV-2+Operational+Resource+Flow+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:49.945+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-2-operational-resource-flow-description', 'ov-2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

306477774

306477776

306477775

**Description**

The OV-2 DoDAF-described Model applies the context of the operational capability to a community of anticipated users. The primary purpose of the OV-2 is to define capability requirements within an operational context. The OV-2 may also be used to express a capability boundary.

New to DoDAF V2.0, the OV-2 can be used to show flows of funding, personnel and materiel in addition to information. A specific application of the OV-2 is to describe a logical pattern of resource (information, funding, personnel, or materiel) flows. The logical pattern need not correspond to specific organizations, systems or locations, allowing Resource Flows to be established without prescribing the way that the Resource Flows are handled and without prescribing solutions.

The intended usage of the OV-2 includes:

- Definition of operational concepts.
- Elaboration of capability requirements.
- Definition of collaboration needs.
- Applying a local context to a capability.
- Problem space definition.
- Operational planning.
- Supply chain analysis.
- Allocation of activities to resources.

**Implementation**

OV-2 can be represented using:

- OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Composite Structure diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.
- SysML Internal Block diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

TheOperational Connectorestablished in an OV-2 can be realized by resources and their interactions in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] model or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space=''] model.

In developing an Architectural Description, OV-2 and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''] are often the starting points and these may be developed iteratively. OV-2 focuses on the Operational Resource Flows, with the activities being a secondary adornment. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''], on the other hand, places first-order attention on operational activities and only second-order attention on Resource Flows, which can be shown as annotations or Swimlanes on the activities.

OV-2 diagram name in DoDAF differs from the one in MODAF, though the diagram concept in both architecture frameworks is the same. OV-2 is one of the starting views of the architecture for DoDAF and requires the Strategic Viewpoint to be developed for MODAF.

306477773

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

306683033

306683035

306683034

**Description**

A specific application of the Operational Resource Flow Internal Description is to describe a logical pattern of resource (information, funding, personnel, or materiel) internal flows.

**Implementation**

OV-2 can be represented using:

- OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Composite Structure diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.
- SysML Internal Block diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Operational Connector established in an OV-2 can be realized by resources and their interactions in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] model or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space=''] model.

In developing an Architectural Description, OV-2 and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space=''] are often the starting points and these may be developed iteratively. OV-2 focuses on the Operational Resource Flows, with the activities being a secondary adornment. The OV-5b, on the other hand, places first-order attention on operational activities and only second-order attention on Resource Flows, which can be shown as annotations or Swimlanes on the activities.

- labels: ['ov-3-operational-resource-flow-matrix', 'ov-3', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

312389490

312389492

312389491

**Description**

The OV-3 addresses operational Resource Flows exchanged between Operational Activities and locations.

Resource Flows provide further detail of the interoperability requirements associated with the operational capability of interest. The focus is on Resource Flows that cross the capability boundary.

The intended usage of the OV-3 includes definition of interoperability requirements.

**Implementation**

OV-3 can be represented using:

- OV-3 table. [ATTACHMENT filename='ov_3.png']
- OV-3 role-based table. A role-based table represents exchanges between Operational Roles (the usages of Operational Performers). [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- OV-3 spreadsheet report.

312389489

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991681 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01349: (2026x Refresh1) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart

- page_id: `303991681`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991681/2026x+Refresh1+OV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:01.338+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-4-organizational-relationships-chart', 'ov-4', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

312601796

312601798

312601797

**Description**

The OV-4 shows organizational structures and interactions. The organizations shown may be civil or military. The OV-4 exists in two forms; role-based (e.g., a typical brigade command structure) and actual (e.g., an organization chart for a department or agency).

A role-based OV-4 shows the possible relationships between organizational resources. The key relationship is composition, i.e., one organizational resource being part of a parent organization. In addition to this, the architect may show the roles each organizational resource has, and the interactions between those roles, i.e., the roles represent the functional aspects of organizational resources. There are no prescribed resource interactions in DoDAF V2.0: the architect should select an appropriate interaction type from the DM2 or add a new one. Interactions illustrate the fundamental roles and management responsibilities, such as supervisory reporting, Command and Control (C2) relationships, collaboration and so on.

An actual OV-4 shows the structure of a real organization at a particular point in time, and is used to provide context to other parts of the architecture such as AV-1 and the CVs.

The intended usage of the role-based OV-4 includes:

- Organizational analysis.
- Definition of human roles.
- Operational analysis.

The intended usage of the actual OV-4 includes:

- Identify architecture stakeholders.
- Identify process owners.
- Illustrate current or future organization structures.

**Implementation**

OV-4 can be represented using:

- OV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The organizations that are modeled using OV-4 may also appear in other models, for example in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] as organizational constituents of a capability or a resource and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships' space=''] where organizations own projects. In a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''], for instance, the organizational resources defined in a typical OV-4 may be part of a capability or resources. Also, actual organizations may form elements of a fielded capability which realizes the requirements at the system-level (again, this may be depicted on a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='']).

312601794

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993427 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01350: (2026x Refresh1) OV-5 Operational Activity Model

- page_id: `303993427`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993427/2026x+Refresh1+OV-5+Operational+Activity+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:09.102+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-5', 'ov-5a-operational-activity-decomposition-tree', 'ov-5b-operational-activity-model', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

312777547

312777549

312777548

**Description**

The OV-5a and the OV-5b describe the operations that are normally conducted in the course of achieving a mission or a business goal. It describes operational activities (or tasks); Input/Output flows between activities, and to/from activities that are outside the scope of the Architectural Description.

The OV-5a and OV-5b describes the operational activities that are being conducted within the mission or scenario.

The OV-5a and OV-5b can be used to:

- Clearly delineate lines of responsibility for activities when coupled with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] .
- Uncover unnecessary Operational Activity redundancy.
- Make decisions about streamlining, combining, or omitting activities.
- Define or flag issues, opportunities, or operational activities and their interactions (information flows among the activities) that need to be scrutinized further.
- Provide a necessary foundation for depicting activity sequencing and timing in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) OV-6a Operational Rules Model' space=''] , the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) OV-6b Operational State Transition Description' space=''] , and the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description' space=''] .

The OV-5b describes the operational, business, and defense portion of the intelligence community activities associated with the Architectural Description, as well as the:

- Relationships or dependencies among the activities.
- Resources exchanged between activities.
- External interchanges (from/to business activities that are outside the scope of the model).

An Operational Activity is what work is required, specified independently of how it is carried out. To maintain this independence from implementation, logical activities and locations in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] are used to represent the structure which carries out the Operational Activities. Operational Activities are realized as System Functions (described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='']) or Service Functions (described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='']) which are the how to the Operational Activities what, i.e., they are specified in terms of the resources that carry them out.

The intended usage of the OV-5a and OV-5b includes:

- Description of activities and workflows.
- Requirements capture.
- Definition of roles and responsibilities.
- Support task analysis to determine training needs.
- Problem space definition.
- Operational planning.
- Logistic support analysis.
- Information flow analysis.

**Implementation**

OV-5 can be represented using:

- OV-5a diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='ov_5a.png']
- OV-5b diagram for Operational Activity flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='ov_5b.png']
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Activity diagram.
- SysML Block diagram.
- SysML Activity diagram.

You can also create OV-5 Business Process Diagram (BPD). In order to do that, use the Cameo Business Modeler plugin.

The OV-5 and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] model are, to a degree, complements of each other. The OV-5 focuses on the operational activities whereas [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] model focuses on the operational activities in relation to locations. Due to the relationship between locations and operational activities, these types of models should normally be developed together.

312777546

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990666 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01351: (2026x Refresh1) OV-5 Operational Activity Model BPD

- page_id: `303990666`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990666/2026x+Refresh1+OV-5+Operational+Activity+Model+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:51.557+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

OV-5 Operational Activity Model (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

OV-5 Operational Activity Model (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>OV-5 Operational Activity Model (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities.</p><p>OV-5 Operational Activity Model (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</p><p> </p>
````

## PAGE 01352: (2026x Refresh1) OV-6a Operational Node Parametric

- page_id: `303999949`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999949/2026x+Refresh1+OV-6a+Operational+Node+Parametric
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:28.808+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF Operational Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF. OV-6a Operational Rules Model
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

497663498

497663500

497663499

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'] to constrain the properties of another Operational Performer. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.

497663497

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Parametric diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.</p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c16fea62-76ff-4d4d-ae92-0dd3ceae57cc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">497663497</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Parametric diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

497172585

497172588

497172586

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'] to constrain the properties of another Operational Performer. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.

497172583

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Parametric diagram' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990663 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01354: (2026x Refresh1) OV-6a Operational Rules Model

- page_id: `303990663`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990663/2026x+Refresh1+OV-6a+Operational+Rules+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:51.421+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-6a-operational-rules-model', 'ov-6a', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

393268360

393268362

393268361

**Description**

An OV-6a specifies operational or business rules that are constraints on the way that business is done in the enterprise. At a top-level, rules should at least embody the concepts of operations defined in OV-1 High Level Operational Concept Graphic and provide guidelines for the development and definition of more detailed rules and behavioral definitions that should occur later in the Architectural definition process.

The intended usage of the OV-6a includes:

- Definition of doctrinally correct operational procedures.
- Definition of business rules.
- Identification of operational constraints.

**Implementation**

OV-6a can be represented using:

- OV-6a table. [ATTACHMENT filename='OV-6a.PNG']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) OV-6a Operational Resource Parametric' space=''] .
- OV-6a spreadsheet report.

Rules defined in an OV-6a may optionally be presented in any other OV. For example, a rule “battle damage assessment shall be carried out under fair weather conditions” may be linked to the Conduct BDA activity in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='']. Any natural language rule presented (e.g., in a diagram note) should also be listed in OV-6a.

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the**Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

393268359

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Constraint' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Performer' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating OV-6a table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) OV-6a Operational Resource Parametric' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990650 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01355: (2026x Refresh1) OV-6b Operational State Transition Description

- page_id: `303990650`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990650/2026x+Refresh1+OV-6b+Operational+State+Transition+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:50.997+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-6b-operational-state-transition-description', 'ov-6b', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The OV-6b is a graphical method of describing how an Operational Activity responds to various events by changing its state. The diagram represents the sets of events to which the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

An OV-6b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of activities or work flow in the business process. The OV-6b is particularly useful for describing critical sequencing of behaviors and timing of operational activities that cannot be adequately described in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='']. The OV-6b relates events and states. A change of state is called a transition. Actions may be associated with a given state or with the transition between states in response to stimuli (e.g., triggers and events).

The intended usage of the OV-6b includes:

- Analysis of business events.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of constraints.

**Implementation**

OV-6b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The OV-6b reflects the fact that the explicit sequencing of activities in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space=''].

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The diagram represents the sets of events to which the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.</p><p>An OV-6b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of activities or work flow in the business process. The OV-6b is particularly useful for describing critical sequencing of behaviors and timing of operational activities that cannot be adequately described in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b Operational Activity Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The OV-6b relates events and states. A change of state is called a transition. Actions may be associated with a given state or with the transition between states in response to stimuli (e.g., triggers and events).</p><p>The intended usage of the OV-6b includes:</p><ul><li <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990647 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01356: (2026x Refresh1) OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description

- page_id: `303990647`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990647/2026x+Refresh1+OV-6c+Operational+Event-Trace+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:50.889+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-6c-operational-event-trace-description', 'ov-6c', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

393648787

393648789

393648788

**Description**

The OV-6c provides a time-ordered examination of the Resource Flows as a result of a particular scenario. Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

Operational Event/Trace Descriptions, sometimes called sequence diagrams, event scenarios, or timing diagrams, allow the tracing of actions in a scenario or critical sequence of events. The OV-6c can be used by itself or in conjunction with an OV-6b State Transition Description to describe the dynamic behavior of activities.

The intended usage of the OV-6c includes:

- Analysis of operational events.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional user requirements.
- Operational test scenarios.

**Implementation**

OV-6c can be represented using a UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The information content of messages in an OV-6c may be related with the Resource Flows in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space=''] and information entities in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space=''].

393648786

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Message' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Performer' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Role' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-6c diagram' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990653 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01357: (2026x Refresh1) OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description BPD

- page_id: `303990653`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990653/2026x+Refresh1+OV-6c+Operational+Event-Trace+Description+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:51.114+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

An OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities.</p><p>An OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992394 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01358: (2026x Refresh1) Overview of the model

- page_id: `303992394`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992394/2026x+Refresh1+Overview+of+the+model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:55.476+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x Refresh1) Sample models > (2026x Refresh1) Creating a simple model
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The electrical circuit is made of three simple components: *a ground*, *an electrical source*, and *a resistor* (see the picture below). The source and the resistor both have two pins, and the ground has one pin. The positive pin of the source is connected to the negative pin of the resistor. The positive pin of the resistor is connected to the negative pin of the source. The ground is also connected to the negative pin of the source.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The electrical circuit is made of three simple components: <em>a ground</em>, <em>an electrical source</em>, and <em>a resistor</em> (see the picture below). The source and the resistor both have two pins, and the ground has one pin. The positive pin of the source is connected to the negative pin of the resistor. The positive pin of the resistor is connected to the negative pin of the source. The ground is also connected to the negative pin of the source.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="electric_circuite_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Overview of the model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p />
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993108 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01359: (2026x Refresh1) Overviewing other diagrams

- page_id: `303993108`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993108/2026x+Refresh1+Overviewing+other+diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:47.828+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

569180833

569180835

569180834

This feature is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

You can also learn about overviewing diagrams while analyzing the Diagram overview sample. 
To open the sample, do one of the following:

- On the Welcome screen, select Samples, and then in the Product Features section, click Diagram overview .
- Go to <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\product features and open the diagram overview.mdzip file.

You can overview various diagrams, including dependency matrices, tables, and relation maps, on a diagram pane. Use the diagram overview shape in order to do that.

Read and learn how to use the diagram overview shape in the following sections:

##### How to...

This section gives some remarks that can be useful when struggling to arrange the sizes of your diagram overview shapes.

###### How to make a couple of diagram overview shapes to be of the same size?

Let’s say you have two diagram overview shapes. You want their zoom ratio to be 1:1 and shape areas to be of the same height (or width) at the same time.

**Solution**

- For both diagram overview shapes, set the Fit Content to the Shape Area property value to false .
- For both diagram overview shapes, set the Autosize property value to false .
- Make both diagram overview shapes of preferred size.
- Make both diagram overview shapes of the same height (or width) by dragging corners of the shape areas.

###### How to set desired shape sizes in the diagram overview shape?

**Solution**

- Set the Fit Content to the Shape Area property value to true for this diagram overview shape.
- Set the desired shape content size by dragging corners of the shape area.
- Set the Fit Content to the Shape Area property value to false for this diagram overview shape.

In case the diagram overview shape shows the full content of the corresponding diagram, all new shapes added to the diagram pane in the diagram overview shape will be of the previously set size.

If you want to reset the shape sizes to a ratio zoom 1:1, do one of the following:

- Set for the diagram overview shape the Autosize property value to true .
- Make the diagram overview shape of the preferred size.

569180832

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating a diagram overview shape' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modifying the content of a diagram overview shape' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997310 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01360: (2026x Refresh1) Owner display mode

- page_id: `303997310`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997310/2026x+Refresh1+Owner+display+mode
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:02.240+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Owner of model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can change the display position of a qualified name on an element shape.

To change the qualified name position

1. On the diagram, right-click the shape. From the shortcut menu, select the Symbol Properties command or select the shape and press Alt+Enter.
2. In the open Symbol Properties dialog, change the property Show Owner . Select one of the four property modes:

| Show Owner Property Mode | Shape | Description | Notation |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Do Not Display |  | Only the element name is displayed on the element shape. This is the default value. | - |
| Below Element Name |  | Owner is displayed below the element name. | This is the modeling tool style notation. The owner name is constructed from the names of the containing namespaces starting at the root of the hierarchy and ending with the owner of the NamedElement itself. Containing namespaces are separated by a dot and the owner is displayed in brackets. |
| In Same Line With Name |  | Element owner is displayed in the same line as the element name. | This is a notation from UML specification. The qualified name is constructed from the names of the containing namespaces, starting at the root of the hierarchy and ending with the name of the NamedElement itself. Containing namespaces are separated by double colons. The double colon is shown to separate containing namespaces and element name. |
| Above Element Name |  | Owner is displayed above the element name. | This notation is the same as the In Same Line With Name option notation. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Containing namespaces are separated by a dot and the owner is displayed in brackets.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>In Same Line With Name</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="63"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_owner_property_name_is_the_same_line_with_name.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Owner display mode" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p><span>Element owner is displayed in the same line as the element name. </span></p></td><td><p><span>This is a notation from UML specification. The qualified name is constructed from the names of the containing namespaces, starting at the root of the hierarchy and ending with the name of the NamedElement itself. Containing namespaces are separated by double colons. The double colon is shown to separate containing namespaces and element name. </span></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Above Element Name</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="64"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_owner_property_name_above_element_name.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Owner display mode" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p><span>Owner is displayed above the element name. </span></p></td><td><p><span>This notation is the same as the </span><strong>In Same Line With Name </strong><span>option notation. </span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995322 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01361: (2026x Refresh1) Owner of model elements

- page_id: `303995322`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995322/2026x+Refresh1+Owner+of+model+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:27.185+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1077038250

1077038252

1077038251

Model elements and diagrams belong to a package, model (system boundary), subsystem, or other appropriated model element, which is called the owner.

The name of the owner is displayed in the model element name compartment in parentheses.

To add a model element to a package, model (system boundary), or subsystem, do one of the following

- Drag a model element to the desired package on the diagram pane or in the Model Browser.
- Open the Package, Model , or Subsystem [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''] , the Inner Elements property group. Click Create and select a model element or diagram you want to add. Define a model element or diagram in the open Specification window and click OK .
- From the selected owner shortcut menu in the Model Browser, select Create Element . From the list, select the desired model element. The element is created.

To display/hide the package/system boundary/subsystem name (the owner of an actor) on a model element

- From the symbol shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog' space=''] opens. In the Show Owner drop down list, select one of the owner display modes: Do Not Display, Below Element Name, In Same Line With Name, Above Element Name. For more information about the owner display mode, see " Owner display mode".
- From the Options menu, select Project. The Project Options dialog opens. Select the desired model element. In the Show Owner drop down list, choose one of the owner display modes. If you want to apply changes for previously created model elements, click Apply.

1077038249

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995498 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01362: (2026x Refresh1) P1 Resource Types

- page_id: `303995498`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995498/2026x+Refresh1+P1+Resource+Types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:39.413+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p1-resource-types', 'p1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

825460242

825460244

825460243

**Description**

The P1 Resource Types view specifies the types of Resources, Technology and Competences required for the architecture. It can also be used to specify properties of Resources. This view collects together all the Resource Types in the architecture. It can also be used to identify Technologies and Competences and map these to resource types. Technologies and Competences can also be set against a timeline indicating when they are expected to be in use.

The P1 view may be used for:

- Identifying Resource Taxonomies.
- Forecasting technology readiness against time.
- HR trends analysis.
- Recruitment planning.
- Planning technology insertion.
- Input to options analysis.
- Definition of performance characteristics.
- Identification of non-functional requirements (input to SRD).

**Implementation**

The P1 view can be represented using:

- P1 Resource Types diagram. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- P1 Systems to Systems Matrix. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table. [ATTACHMENT filename='P1_typical.png']
- P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements table. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- P1 Resource Types Technology Forecast table. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- P1 Resource Types Service Provision which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The ServiceInterfaces are used as the row elements and the Resources are used as the column elements. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

825460240

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994517 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01363: (2026x Refresh1) P2 Resource Internal Structure

- page_id: `303994517`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994517/2026x+Refresh1+P2+Resource+Internal+Structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:18.372+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p2-resource-structure', 'p2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

827013152

827013155

827013153

**Description**

The P2i Resource Internal Structure view addresses the composition and (high-level) interaction of resources.

The P2i view links together the operational and systems architecture views by depicting how resources are structured and interact in order to realize the logical architecture specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) L2 Logical Scenario' space='']. The P2i view may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) L2 Logical Scenario' space=''] view(i.e. in a to-be architecture) and so there may be many alternative Resource view suites that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in an as-is architecture, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) L2 Logical Internal Scenario' space='']view may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the P2i to allow communication of key information flows to non-technical stakeholders.

The P2i view can be used to specify typical (or template) organization structures, and also identify how human resources interact with each other and with systems.

This view can provide a simplified representation of a pathway or network, usually depicted graphically as a connector (i.e. a line with possible amplifying information). The P2i view depicts interactions between resources that are of interest to the architect.

Resources may be decomposed in the P2i view to any level (i.e. depth) of decomposition that the architect sees fit. This view may also identify the physical asset (e.g. platforms) at which resources are deployed and can show the operational nodes that utilize those resources. In many cases, an operational node depicted in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) L2 Logical Scenario' space=''] view may well be the logical representation of the resource shown in the P2i view.

The P2i view may be used for:

- Definition of system concepts.
- Definition of system options.
- Human – System interactions.
- Typical Organization structures.
- Interface requirements capture.
- Capability integration planning.
- System integration management.
- Operational planning (capability configuration definition).

**Implementation**

The P2i view can be represented using:

- P2 Resource Internal Structure diagram, which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='P2.png']
- UML Composite Structure diagram.
- SysML Internal Block diagram.

827013151

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Role' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interface' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Connector' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994531 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01365: (2026x Refresh1) P3 Resource Connectivity

- page_id: `303994531`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994531/2026x+Refresh1+P3+Resource+Connectivity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:20.225+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p3-resource-connectivity', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

828276772

828276775

828276774

**Description**

The P3 Resource Connectivity view describes communications networks and pathways that link communications systems, and provides details regarding their configuration. The purpose of P3 view is to provide a comprehensive specification of how systems are connected, what interfaces each system exposes (ports), the hardware interface used and the protocols transmitted across the interface.

The networks and pathways documented through these views represent the physical implementation of the information needlines identified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) L2 Logical Internal Scenario' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) L3 Node Interactions' space='']view.

The P3 view focuses on the physical characteristics of each link by specifying attributes. An example of this might be the geographic location and layout of network components such as routers, switches, amplifiers and repeaters.

**Implementation**

The P3 view can be represented using:

- P3 Resource Connectivity table. [ATTACHMENT filename='P3.PNG']
- P3 Resource Role Connectivity table. [ATTACHMENT filename='P3_role_connectivity.png']
- P3 spreadsheet report.

828276771

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994033 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01366: (2026x Refresh1) P4 Resource Functions

- page_id: `303994033`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994033/2026x+Refresh1+P4+Resource+Functions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:50.361+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p4-resource-functions', 'p4', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

828581287

828581290

828581288

**Description**

The P4 Resource Functions view addresses resource (human and non-human) functions – i.e. the activities performed by Resources.

The primary purposes of the P4 view are to:

- Develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by each resource.
- Ensure that the functional connectivity is complete (i.e. that a resource’s required inputs are all satisfied).
- Ensure that the functional decomposition reaches an appropriate level of detail.
- Provide implementation-specific realizations of the operational activities specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) L4 Logical Activities' space=''] view.

The Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the:

- Allocation of functions to resources.
- Flow of data between functions.

The P4 view is the systems view counterpart to the Activity Model ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) L4 Logical Activities' space='']) of the operational view.

The P4 view may be used for:

- Description of task workflow.
- Identification of functional system requirements.
- Functional decomposition of systems.
- Relate human and system functions.

**Implementation**

The P4 view can be represented using:

- P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram for Function hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- P4 Resource Functions diagram for Function flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix. [ATTACHMENT filename='P4_matrix.png']
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Activity diagram.
- SysML Block diagram.
- SysML Activity diagram.

828581285

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000446 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01367: (2026x Refresh1) P5 Resource States

- page_id: `304000446`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000446/2026x+Refresh1+P5+Resource+States
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:11.435+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p5-resource-states', 'p5', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The P5 Resource States view describes how Resources change state in response to events and other stimuli. The P5 view identifies the states a Resource can be, the allowable changes between those states, and the triggers that cause the state changes.

The P5 view may be used to:

- Define states, events and state transitions (behavioural modelling).
- Identify constraints (input to System Requirements Document).

**Implementation**

The P5 view can be represented using a P5 Resource States diagram which is based on the UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource State Description' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Software' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994060 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01368: (2026x Refresh1) P6 Resource Sequence

- page_id: `303994060`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994060/2026x+Refresh1+P6+Resource+Sequence
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:53.248+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p6-resource-sequence', 'p6', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The P6 Resource Sequence view provides a time-ordered examination of the interactions between Resources. These are usually depicted as event-trace diagrams and will have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

P6 products are valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of functions and system data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or system port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, in order to perform its assigned functionality.

The P6 view may be used for:

- Analysis of resource events impacting operation.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional system requirements (input to System Requirement Document).

**Implementation**

The P6 view can be represented using a P6 Resource Sequence diagram which is based on the UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Message' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Role' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Natural Resource' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Software' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994052 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01369: (2026x Refresh1) P7 Physical Data Model

- page_id: `303994052`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994052/2026x+Refresh1+P7+Physical+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:52.311+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p7-physical-data-model', 'p7', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

829238245

829238248

829238246

**Description**

The P7 Physical Data Model view defines the structure of data used by the Resources in the architecture. It is used to describe how the information represented in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) L7 Logical Data Model' space='']L7 Logical Data Model is implemented. While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (eg entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.

**Implementation**

The P7 view can be represented using:

- P7 Physical Data Model diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='P7.png']

- UML Class diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.

829238243

**Related elements**

- Property
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Association' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0061d916-ec08-40ef-a5ed-e64f2fcdff59"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">829238245</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e1462c3-1a4c-4af3-94ac-6efbb415296f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">829238248</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bab9406f-e47a-47b0-af35-46eaefd1d644"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">829238246</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The P7 Physical Data Model view defines the structure of data used by the Resources in the architecture. It is used to describe how the information represented in the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) L7 Logical Data Model" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">L7 Logical Data Mode</span>l</ac:link-body></ac:link> is implemented. While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (eg entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The P7 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li>P7 Physical Data Model diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:title=" P7 Physical Data Model diagram" ac:alt=" P7 Physical Data Model diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="P7.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) P7 Physical Data Model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cf0a0a08-39fd-44b2-bdf9-aa0cb5e98722">UML Class diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cf0a0a08-39fd-44b2-bdf9-aa0cb5e98722">SysML Block Definition diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="218d97ba-97c2-4714-9473-16344ee6a907"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">829238243</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li>Property</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Association" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Information" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Information Model" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="e5486927-a967-4ef6-8626-4c72c2a9d5c3" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994072 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01370: (2026x Refresh1) P8 Resource Constraints

- page_id: `303994072`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994072/2026x+Refresh1+P8+Resource+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:54.653+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p8-resource-constraints', 'p8', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

830788941

830788943

830788942

**Description**

The P8 Resource Constraints view specifies functional and non-functional constraints on the implementation aspects of the architecture (i.e. the structural and behavioral elements of the Resource viewpoint). It constraints on the resources, functions, data and ports that make up the Resource architecture. The constraints are specified in text and may be functional or structural (i.e. non-functional).

The P8 view may be used to:

- Define implementation logic.
- Identify resource constraints.

**Implementation**

The P8 view can be represented using:

- P8 Resource Constraints table. [ATTACHMENT filename='P8.PNG']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) P8 Resource Parametric' space=''] diagram.
- P8 spreadsheet report.

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the**Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

830788940

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Constraint' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Software' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='MED']

**Related procedures**
