Each cell of the grid is an artifact to deliver in the systems engineering process. The method is based on existing studies in the field and real-life findings in managing models for organizations from different systems engineering domains.</span></span></p><p>MagicGrid contains a number of predefined MetricSuites that you can use to calculate Requirement coverage and Requirement covering elements. To learn more about the predefined Metric Suites for MagicGrid, see:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4661523d-70b6-48ed-ac5b-c53ea1713e94" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000624 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01413: (2026x Refresh1) Predefined Metric Suites in UAF

- page_id: `304000624`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000624/2026x+Refresh1+Predefined+Metric+Suites+in+UAF
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:28.797+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures > (2026x Refresh1) Coverage analysis in UAF
- labels: ['metric-suites', 'capabilities-covered-by-nodes', 'capabilities-covered-by-opactivities', 'nodes-covered-by-sysresources', 'opactivitie-covered-by-functions', 'opactivities-covered-by-nodes', 'capabilities-covered-by-sysinterfaces', 'capabilities-covered-by-capconfiguration', 'capabilities-covered-by-projects', 'orgresources-covered-by-projects', 'projects-covered-by-milestones', 'functions-covered-by-sysresources', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Metric suite is a collection of parameters and metric definitions used to calculate metrics that evaluate specific aspects of your model.

See the following figure for better understanding of the predefined metric suites hierarchy.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Actual Organizational Resources Covered by Actual Projects

The Metric Suite "Actual Organizational Resources Covered by Actual Projects" counts the percentage of the Actual Organization Resources covered by the Actual Projects in the particular package. The number of Actual Organization Resources, Actual Projects, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Functions Covered by Resource Performers

The Metric Suite "Functions Covered by Resource Performers" counts the percentage of the Functions covered by the Resource Performers in the particular package. The number of Functions, Resource Performers, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Actual Projects Covered by Milestones

The Metric Suite "Projects Covered by Milestones" counts the percentage of the Actual Projects covered by the Actual Milestones in the particular package. The number of Actual Projects, Actual Milestones, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Actual Projects Covered by Actual Organizational Resources

The Metric Suite "Actual Projects Covered by Actual Organizational Resources" counts the percentage of the Actual Projects covered by the Actual Organization Resources in the particular package. The number of Actual Projects, Actual Organization Resources, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Operational Activities Covered by Functions

The Metric Suite "Operational Activities Covered by Functions" counts the percentage of the Operational Activities covered by Functions in the particular package. The number of Operational Activities, Functions, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Operational Activities Covered by Operational Performers

The Metric Suite "Operational Activities Covered by Operational Performers" counts the percentage of the Operational Activities covered by Operational Performers in the particular package. The number of Operational Activities, Operational Performers, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Operational Performers Covered by Resource Performers

The Metric Suite "Operational Performers Covered by Resource Performers" counts the percentage of the Operational Performers covered by Resource Performers in the particular package. The number of Operational Performers, Resource Performers, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Capabilities Covered by Actual Project

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by Projects" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by the Actual Projects in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Actual Projects, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Capabilities Covered by Operational Activities

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by Operational Activities" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Operational Activities in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Operational Activities, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Capabilities Covered by Capability Configuration

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by Capability Configuration" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Capability Configurations or Physical Architectures in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Capability Configurations or Physical Architectures, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Capabilities Covered by Operational Performers

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by Operational Performers" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Operational Performers in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Operational Performers, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner As Requirement)

Metric suite tracks requirements coverage with design - checks incoming satisfy relations and requirements coverage with test cases - checks incoming verify relations. 
Each requirement is treated as separate element:

- If the grouping requirement has incoming satisfy relation, its child requirements are not treated as covered unless they have incoming satisfy relations.
- The same thing applies in the opposite direction. If all child requirements have incoming satisfy relation grouping, the requirement is not treated as satisfied unless it also has an incoming satisfy relation.

##### Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner As Grouping Element)

Metric suite tracks requirements coverage with design - checks incoming satisfy relations and requirements coverage with test cases - checks incoming verify relations. 
Grouping requirement is not treated as independent requirement:

- If the grouping requirement has incoming satisfy relation, its child requirements are treated as covered independent of whether have they incoming satisfy relations or not.
- The same thing applies in the opposite direction. If all child requirements have incoming satisfy relation, then the grouping requirement is treated as satisfied.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Metric suite is a collection of parameters and metric definitions used to calculate metrics that evaluate specific aspects of your model.</p><p>See the following figure for better understanding of the predefined metric suites hierarchy.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Metric Suite hierarchy" ac:alt="Metric Suite hierarchy"><ri:attachment ri:filename="MetricSuite_hierarchy.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Predefined Metric Suites in UAF" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Actual Organizational Resources Covered by Actual Projects</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Metric Suite &quot;Actual Organizational Resources Covered by Actual Projects&quot; counts the percentage of the Actual Organization Resources covered by the Actual Projects in the particular package. The number of Actual Organization Resources, Actual Projects, and the coverage average are also counted.</span></p><h3>Functions Covered by Resource Performers</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Metric Suite &quot;Functions Covered by Resource Performers&quot; counts the percentage of the Functions covered by the Resource Performers in the particular package. The number of Functions, Resource Performers, and the coverage average are also counted.<br /></span></p><h3>Actual Projects Covered by Milestones</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Metric Suite &quot;Projects Covered by Milestones&quot; counts the percentage of the Actual Projects covered by the Actual Milestones in the particular package. The number of Actual Projects, Actual Milestones, and the coverage average are also counted.<br /></span></p><h3>Actual Projects Covered by Actual Organizational Resources</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Metric Suite &quot;Actual Projects Covered by Actual Organizational Resources&quot; counts the percentage of the Actual Projects covered by the Actual Organization Resources in the particular package. The number of  Actual Projects,  Actual Organization Resources, and the coverage average are also counted.</span></p><h3>Operational Activities Covered by Functions</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Metric Suite &quot;Operational Activities Covered by Functions&quot; counts the percentage of the Operational Activities covered by Functions in the particular package. The number of Operational Activities, Functions, and the coverage average are also counted.</span></p><h3>Operational Activities Covered by Operational Performers</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Metric Suite &quot;Operational Activities Covered by Operational Performers&quot; counts the percentage of the Operational Activities covered by Operational Performers in the particular package. The number of Operational Activities, Operational Performers, and the coverage average are also counted.</span></p><h3>Operational Performers Covered by Resource Performers</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Metric Suite &quot;Operational Performers Covered by Resource Performers&quot; counts the percentage of the Operational Performers covered by Resource Performers in the particular package. The number of Operational Performers, Resource Performers, and the coverage average are also counted.</span></p><h3>Capabilities Covered by Actual Project</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by Projects&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by the Actual Projects in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Actual Projects, and the coverage average are also counted.</span></p><h3>Capabilities Covered by Operational Activities</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by Operational Activities&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Operational Activities in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Operational Activities, and the coverage average are also counted.</span></p><h3>Capabilities Covered by Capability Configuration</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by Capability Configuration&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Capability Configurations or Physical Architectures in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Capability Configurations or Physical Architectures, and the coverage average are also counted.</span></p><h3>Capabilities Covered by Operational Performers</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by Operational Performers&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Operational Performers in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Operational Performers, and the coverage average are also counted.</span></p><h3>Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner As Requirement)</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Metric suite tracks requirements coverage with design - checks incoming satisfy relations and requirements coverage with test cases - checks incoming verify relations.<br />Each requirement is treated as separate element:</span></p><ul><li style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">If the grouping requirement has incoming satisfy relation, its child requirements are not treated as covered unless they have incoming satisfy relations.</span></li><li style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The same thing applies in the opposite direction. If all child requirements have incoming satisfy relation grouping, the requirement is not treated as satisfied unless it also has an incoming satisfy relation.<br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></span></li></ul><h3>Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner As Grouping Element)</h3><p style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Metric suite tracks requirements coverage with design - checks incoming satisfy relations and requirements coverage with test cases - checks incoming verify relations.<br />Grouping requirement is not treated as independent requirement:</span></p><ul><li style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">If the grouping requirement has incoming satisfy relation, its child requirements are treated as covered independent of whether have they incoming satisfy relations or not.</span></li><li style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The same thing applies in the opposite direction. If all child requirements have incoming satisfy relation, then the grouping requirement is treated as satisfied.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992535 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01414: (2026x Refresh1) Predefined Relation Maps

- page_id: `303992535`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992535/2026x+Refresh1+Predefined+Relation+Maps
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:19.703+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 diagram descriptions
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use all predefined relation maps to represent the traceability of system requirements and design elements. The main purpose of relation maps is to review and analyze relations among the elements and create new elements directly in the relation map. It is a special kind of diagram that automatically updates and renders an element's dependency tree according to predefined dependency criteria. You can create five kinds of predefined relation maps:

- .
- .
- .
- .
- .

You can create predefined relation maps only if you have the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling' space=''] installed. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Installation, licensing, and system requirements' space='']>]]>

The different purposes for each relation map are illustrated below:

- activityActivity Decomposition Map displays an Activity decomposition of the selected context. You can review, analyze, and decompose the Activities. The example below is created by using the [ATTACHMENT filename='distiller model.mdzip'] sample model that only comes with SysML v1 Plugin. [ATTACHMENT filename='activity_decomposition_map.png']
- structureStructure Decomposition Map displays the decomposition of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] related through the Composition relationship of the specified context. You can review, analyze, and decompose the Part Properties . The example below is created by using the [ATTACHMENT filename='VehicleStructure.mdzip'] sample model that only comes with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation' space='MMA'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='structure_decomposition_map .png']

- instanceInstance Map displays a hierarchy of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MED'] of the selected context. You can review and analyze the hierarchy of Instance Specifications. The example below is created by using the [ATTACHMENT filename='SpacecraftMassRollup_HTMLTable.mdzip'] sample model that only comes with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation' space='MMA'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='instance_map.png']

- containRequirement Containment Map displays the decomposition of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Requirement' space='MED'] related through the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment' space='MED'] of the specified context. You can review, analyze, and decompose the Requirements. The example below is created by using the [ATTACHMENT filename='hybrid sport utility vehicle.mdzip'] sample model that only comes with the SysML v1 Plugin. [ATTACHMENT filename='requirement_containment_map.png']

- deriveRequirement Derivation Map displays the decomposition of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Requirement' space='MED'] that are related trough the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Derive' space='MED'] relationship. You can review, analyze, and create Derive relationships among the Requirements. The example below is created by using the [ATTACHMENT filename='hybrid sport utility vehicle.mdzip'] sample model that only comes with the SysML v1 Plugin. [ATTACHMENT filename='requirement_derivation_map .png']

#### NOTE: Additional relation map

Additional relation map

You can also create a Relation Map Diagram if you want a rapid review, analysis, and creation of relationships among the elements of the entire model. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Relation Map' space='']>]]>

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Relation Map' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995671 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01415: (2026x Refresh1) Predefined traceability rules

- page_id: `303995671`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995671/2026x+Refresh1+Predefined+traceability+rules
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:42.933+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Traceability
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1965944762

1965944764

1965944763

Besides allowing you to create your own traceability rules, our modeling tool allows you to use a set of predefined element properties for traceability purposes. The traceability solution also includes a report template used for coverage analysis. You can customize this template or create your own templates for producing your own traceability analysis reports.

The main objective of the predefined traceability rules is to allow the use of the traceability engine instantly (out- of-the-box) for a typical project. The predefined rules allow for analyzing and verifying links between elements residing in Analysis, Design, and Implementation layers of your models. Elements in these layers are extended with a set of properties, pointing to elements from the higher abstraction layer (backward traceability) and the lower abstraction layer (forward traceability).

Overall, there are three groups of predefined traceability properties, which will be detailed in subsequent sections:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Forward traceability - realization' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Backward traceability – specification' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Other traceability relations' space='']

1965944761

**Related pages**

- Traceability
  - Creating traceability relations
  - Traceability relations representation
  - Navigating between different levels of abstraction
  - Analyzing traceability relations
  - Custom traceability rules

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f14a44cd-b4c9-44fb-9166-2652ceab93b0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1965944762</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="63d48b39-173b-41e0-a7bc-508cf132dd5b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1965944764</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7501e2ec-a114-4433-a095-3e4e33b7459d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1965944763</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>Besides allowing you to create your own traceability rules, our modeling tool allows you to use a set of predefined element properties for traceability purposes. The traceability solution also includes a report template used for coverage analysis. You can customize this template or create your own templates for producing your own traceability analysis reports. </span></p><p><span>The main objective of the predefined traceability rules is to allow the use of the traceability engine instantly (out- of-the-box) for a typical project. The predefined rules allow for analyzing and verifying links between elements residing in Analysis, Design, and Implementation layers of your models. Elements in these layers are extended with a set of properties, pointing to elements from the higher abstraction layer (backward traceability) and the lower abstraction layer (forward traceability).</span></p><p>Overall, there are three groups of predefined traceability properties, which will be detailed in subsequent sections:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Forward traceability - realization" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Backward traceability – specification" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Backward Traceability – Specification]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Other traceability relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Other traceability relations ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p></div></div><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2315f64e-6a9b-4f4f-8671-e91ed8ca3b6c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1965944761</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Traceability">Traceability</a><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Creating+traceability+relations">Creating traceability relations</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Traceability+relations+representation">Traceability relations representation</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Navigating+between+different+levels+of+abstraction">Navigating between different levels of abstraction</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Analyzing+traceability+relations">Analyzing traceability relations</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Custom+traceability+rules">Custom traceability rules</a></li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995859 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01416: (2026x Refresh1) Predefined validation suites

- page_id: `303995859`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995859/2026x+Refresh1+Predefined+validation+suites
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:39.484+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Validation
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can validate your model against a set of constraints called validation suites. You must perform the validation manually to check your model against the predefined validation suites. The predefined validation suites have the «validationSuite» stereotype applied.

Each Profile contains its own validation suites. You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating validation suites' space=''].

The following predefined validation suites appear in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Validation dialog' space='']**Validation** dialog or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Validation Suites dialog' space='']**Validation Suites** dialog:

- **Composition Integrity** contains validation rules that check the structure of the project, such as recovered elements, references, versions of project usages, etc.
- **Project Integrity Correctness** contains validation rules that check cyclical dependencies between current project and used projects.
- **Spelling** contains validation rules that check the spelling of properties and tagged values.
- **Text References Validation** contains validation rules that check if the hyperlink text and the referenced element are coherent.
- **UML completeness constraints** contains validation rules that check if the model is complete, that there are no gaps, and the essential information fields in the elements have been filled in (e.g. checks that all the properties have type specified etc.).
- **UML correctness constraints** contains validation rules that check if the model is correct, that related information is consistent across the model (e.g. the multiplicity between elements coincides between their instances).

You can find predefined validation suites in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Containment tab' space=''] when the **Show Auxiliary Resources** option is enabled, expand the *UML Standard Profile* Package> *Validation Profile* Package.

To show the auxiliary resources

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser' space=''] , click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button_in_model_browser.png'] and enable the Show Auxiliary Resources option.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Starting the validation' space='']Learn how to start the validation >>

Limiting the scope of the constraints to be validated against

If you select the **All Validation Suites** as the **Validation Suite** in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Validation dialog' space='']**Validation** dialog, your model is validated against all validation suites. To limit the scope of the constraints to be validated against, you can select a more specific validation suite, such as **Numbering Validation**. The model or its part is validated against the selected validation suite. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel' space='']**Validation Results** panel shows the results, listing all elements that do not conform to constraints in the selected validation suite. These elements are called “invalid” elements and are highlighted.

Customizing validation options

You can customize the Validation options in the**Project Options** dialog.

To open the Validation options

1. Open the**Project Options** dialog in one of the following ways:
  - On the main menu, select **Analyze** > **Validation** > **Validation Options**.
  - On the main menu, select **Options** > **Project**. In the [**Project Options**](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Setting+project+options) dialog, select **General** > **Validation**.
2. Specify the options you need in the **Validation** area. [How to use the **Project Options** dialog >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Setting+project+options)
3. Click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Validation options in the Project Options dialog.

**Related pages**

- Active validation suites
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 validation suites' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 active validation suites' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Requirements validation suites' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can validate your model against a set of constraints called validation suites. You must perform the validation manually to check your model against the predefined validation suites. The predefined validation suites have the «validationSuite» stereotype applied.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="861f2d9f-cb9a-431c-ab64-893247d5056d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Each Profile contains its own validation suites. You can <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating validation suites" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create your own validation suites]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The following predefined validation suites appear in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Validation</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation Suites dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Validation Suites</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>:</p><ul><li data-uuid="10d26e86-c9b2-4432-8b5b-bad26c3ce40c"><p><strong>Composition Integrity</strong> contains validation rules that check the <span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">structure of the project, such as recovered elements, references, versions of project usages, etc.   </span><br /></span></p></li><li data-uuid="cefc2e01-4604-42c9-80f4-dc30f30681be"><p><strong>Project Integrity Correctness</strong> contains validation rules that check <span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">cyclical dependencies between current project and used projects.</span><br /></span></p></li><li data-uuid="44c005d0-588d-46a1-9e43-5b1ddab4d1c9"><p><strong>Spelling</strong> contains validation rules that check the spelling of properties and tagged values.</p></li><li data-uuid="374bbd2d-be4e-4099-bca5-bab3cfeba982"><p><strong>Text References Validation</strong> contains validation rules that check if the hyperlink text and the referenced element are coherent.</p></li><li data-uuid="f6bf8ce4-1dbc-4287-a194-7400e47d8fd7"><p><strong>UML completeness constraints</strong> contains validation rules that check if the model is complete, that there are no gaps, and the essential information fields in the elements have been filled in (e.g. checks that all the properties have type specified etc.).</p></li><li data-uuid="e19f7433-bf4a-4e27-99b0-e12540f98377"><p><strong>UML correctness constraints</strong> contains validation rules that check if the model is correct, that related information is consistent across the model (e.g. the multiplicity between elements coincides between their instances).</p></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2dd220e4-7112-4724-90ad-c80a600dfdcf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can find predefined validation suites in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> when the <strong>Show Auxiliary Resources</strong> option is enabled, expand the <em>UML Standard Profile</em> Package&gt; <em>Validation Profile</em> Package.</p><p>To show the auxiliary resources</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser" /></ac:link>, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button_in_model_browser.png" /></ac:image> and enable the <strong>Show Auxiliary Resources</strong> option.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Starting the validation" /><ac:link-body>Learn how to <span class="confluence-link">start the validation &gt;&gt;</span></ac:link-body></ac:link></p><p>Limiting the scope of the constraints to be validated against</p><p>If you select the <strong>All Validation Suites</strong> as the <strong>Validation Suite</strong> in the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation dialog" /><ac:link-body> <span><strong>Validation</strong> dialog</span></ac:link-body></ac:link>, your model is validated against all validation suites. To limit the scope of the constraints to be validated against, you can select a more specific validation suite, such as <strong>Numbering Validation</strong>. The model or its part is validated against the selected validation suite. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation Results panel" /><ac:link-body><strong>Validation Results</strong> panel</ac:link-body></ac:link> shows the results, listing all elements that do not conform to constraints in the selected validation suite. These elements are called “invalid” elements and are highlighted.</p><p>Customizing validation options</p><p>You can customize the Validation options in the<strong> Project Options</strong> dialog.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the Validation options</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="b6dede10-0146-4fc3-a0ca-70da212bb857"><p>Open the<strong> Project Options</strong> dialog in one of the following ways:</p><ul><li><p>On the main menu, select <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Validation</strong> &gt; <strong>Validation Options</strong>.</p></li><li><p>On the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. In the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Setting+project+options"><strong>Project Options</strong></a> dialog, select <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>Validation</strong>.</p></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="e3502cc4-adc0-489f-aa00-96b276f3d57e"><p>Specify the options you need in the <strong>Validation</strong> area. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Setting+project+options">How to use the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog &gt;&gt;</a></p></li><li data-uuid="0bbfe3cc-5775-46b4-b532-4a6a35ae1214"><p>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="validation_options.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Predefined validation suites" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Validation options in the Project Options dialog.</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="with-breadcrumbs" data-uuid="ed8bc80c-86e3-4bda-88ed-7fa82b629ac0"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Active+validation+suites">Active validation suites</a></li><li class="with-breadcrumbs" data-uuid="bac41601-0ec3-4163-9104-f49ba7d9a4cf"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 validation suites" /></ac:link></li><li class="with-breadcrumbs" data-uuid="f6b5ff4a-0116-41b5-916b-99051b0e615c"><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 active validation suites" /></ac:link></p></li><li class="with-breadcrumbs" data-uuid="232eb94e-400c-4a6f-954a-8e7b982e089a"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Requirements validation suites" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997408 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01417: (2026x Refresh1) Preparing for merge

- page_id: `303997408`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997408/2026x+Refresh1+Preparing+for+merge
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:08.161+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools > (2026x Refresh1) Model Merge
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can customize the model merge procedure by changing **Merge and Compare**options in the **Environment Options**dialog.

To access **Merge and Compare** environment options

1. In the main menu, select **Options** > **Environment**.
2. In the **Environment Options** dialog, expand the **General** option group and select **Merge and Compare** options. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can use **Merge and Compare** environment options to customize model merge in the following ways:

- Change the optimization mode: To merge models faster, set the Optimize for option to Speed . This is the default setting and it is sufficient for most projects. However, the Speed option requires more memory. To merge large models, set the Optimize for option to **Memory**. Sometimes the memory required for the **Speed** mode exceeds the memory allocated for the modeling tool. In this case, switch to the **Memory** mode, which conserves memory. However, this option slows the procedure down considerably.
- Disable order changes detection in properties/tags: In the Do Not Detect Order Changes for option value cell, specify the names of properties/tags whose order changes should not be detected while merging. Property/tag names must be written in camel case, for example, *ownedAttribute*, *ownedElement*, etc.

- Enable the detection of changes in derived properties: In the Detect Changes in Derived Properties option value cell, specify the derived properties whose changes you want to track while merging. Derived properties should be specified using the following format: *<ElementType>.<derivedPropertyName>*. Element type must be written the same way as it is shown in the element Specification window, and derived property name must be written in camel case. For example, the **Type** property of a Lifeline should be specified as *Lifeline.type*.Keep in mind that tracking derived properties reduces merge performance.

- Change the default conflict resolution contributor: Conflicting changes detected during merge are automatically resolved by accepting changes either from the target or the source version. To accept changes from the target version when a conflict occurs, set the **Automatically Resolve Conflicts by Choosing** option to **Target**. To accept changes from the source version when a conflict occurs, set the **Automatically Resolve Conflicts by Choosing** option to **Source.**
- Select which changes to detect in diagrams: To detect changes only in diagrams that were loaded (open) when the changes were made, set the Detect Changes in Diagrams option to Direct Changes . To detect changes in all diagrams of the model, set the Detect Changes in Diagrams option to All Changes .
- Choose whether to detect changes in the diagrams of used projects: If you want to track changes in the diagrams of used projects in Teamwork Cloud, set the Detect Changes in Diagrams from Used Projects in Teamwork Cloud option to true . Otherwise, set the option to false . Setting the option to f alse may improve the merge performance.

#### INFO: For server projects

For server projects

- Partial locking of a project occurs when merging changes of a project in the Teamwork Cloud server. Only changed/updated and merging elements are locked in the project, not the whole project. Thus, actions with other server project elements are not interrupted for other users by locking and merging activities.
- While a user performs the merge process in a server project on the Teamwork Cloud server, other users are not able to commit project changes to the server.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Understanding merge types' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Understanding change concept' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Starting Model Merge' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Analyzing and managing merge results' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Finishing and canceling merge' space='']

Sometimes the memory required for the <strong>Speed</strong> mode exceeds the memory allocated for the modeling tool. In this case, switch to the <strong>Memory</strong> mode, which conserves memory. However, this option slows the procedure down considerably.<br /><br /></span></li><li><strong>Disable order changes detection in properties/tags:<br /></strong>In the <strong>Do Not Detect Order Changes for</strong> option value cell, specify the names of properties/tags whose order changes should not be detected while merging.<ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="efcc90ea-7b77-47fc-8fb6-f9287eca717c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Property/tag names must be written in camel case, for example, <em>ownedAttribute</em>, <em>ownedElement</em>, etc.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><ul><li><strong>Enable the detection of changes in derived properties:<br /></strong>In the <strong>Detect Changes in Derived Properties</strong> option value cell, specify the derived properties whose changes you want to track while merging.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="56b5ac86-14bc-47c1-bbf2-4fe0098dae30"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Derived properties should be specified using the following format: <em>&lt;ElementType&gt;.&lt;derivedPropertyName&gt;</em>. Element type must be written the same way as it is shown in the element Specification window, and derived property name must be written in camel case. For example, the <strong>Type</strong> property of a Lifeline should be specified as <em>Lifeline.type</em>.</li><li>Keep in mind that tracking derived properties reduces merge performance.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><ul><li><strong>Change the default conflict resolution contributor:</strong> <br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Conflicting changes detected during merge are automatically resolved by accepting changes either from the target or the source version. To detect changes in all diagrams of the model, set the <strong>Detect Changes in Diagrams</strong><span> option to </span><strong>All Changes</strong><span>.<br /><br /></span></li><li><strong>Choose whether to detect changes in the diagrams of used projects:</strong><br />If you want to track changes in the diagrams of used projects in Teamwork Cloud, set the <strong>Detect Changes in Diagrams from Used Projects in Teamwork Cloud</strong> option to <em>true</em>. Otherwise, set the option to <em>false</em>. Setting the option to f<em>alse </em>may improve the merge performance.</li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f2a45f3d-e00f-4aeb-9b8d-34145636a27f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">For server projects</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Partial locking of a project occurs when merging changes of a project in the Teamwork Cloud server. Only changed/updated and merging elements are locked in the project, not the whole project. Thus, actions with other server project elements are not interrupted for other users by locking and merging activities.</li><li>While a user performs the merge process in a server project on the Teamwork Cloud server, other users are not able to commit project changes to the server.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul class="childpages-macro conf-macro output-block"><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Understanding merge types" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Understanding change concept" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Starting Model Merge" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Analyzing and managing merge results" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Finishing and canceling merge" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995837 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01418: (2026x Refresh1) Preparing for project comparison

- page_id: `303995837`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995837/2026x+Refresh1+Preparing+for+project+comparison
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:26:08.574+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Comparing projects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can customize the project comparison procedure by changing **Merge and Compare**options in the **Environment Options**dialog.

To access **Merge and Compare** environment options

1. In the main menu, select **Options** > **Environment**.
2. In the **Environment Options** dialog, expand the **General** option group and select **Merge and Compare** options. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can use **Merge and Compare** environment options to customize model comparison in the following ways:

- Change the optimization mode: To compare models faster, set the Optimize for option to Speed . This is the default setting, and it is sufficient for most projects. However, the Speed option requires more memory. To compare large models, set the Optimize for option to **Memory**. Sometimes the memory required for the **Speed** mode exceeds the memory allocated for the modeling tool. In this case, switch to the **Memory** mode, which conserves memory. However, this option slows the procedure down considerably.
- Disable order changes detection in properties/tags: In the Do Not Detect Order Changes for option value cell, specify the names of properties/tags whose order changes should not be detected while comparing projects. Property/tag names must be written in camel case, for example, *ownedAttribute*, *ownedElement*, etc.

- Enable the detection of changes in derived properties: In the Detect Changes in Derived Properties option value cell, specify the derived properties whose changes you want to track while comparing projects. Derived properties should be specified using the following format: *<ElementType>.<derivedPropertyName>*. Element type must be written the same way as it is shown in the element Specification window, and derived property name must be written in camel case. For example, the **Type** property of a Lifeline should be specified as *Lifeline.type*.Keep in mind that tracking derived properties reduces merge performance.

- Select which changes to detect in diagrams: To detect changes only in diagrams that were loaded (open) when the changes were made, set the Detect Changes in Diagrams option to Direct Changes . To detect changes in all diagrams of the model, set the Detect Changes in Diagrams option to All Changes .

#### INFO: For server projects

For server projects

- Partial locking of a project occurs when merging changes of a project in the Teamwork Cloud server. Only changed/updated and merged elements are locked in the project, not the whole project. Thus, actions with other server project elements are not interrupted for other users by locking and merging activities.
- While a user performs the merge process in a server project on the Teamwork Cloud server, other users are not able to commit project changes to the server.

For example, the <strong>Type</strong> property of a Lifeline should be specified as <em>Lifeline.type</em>.</li><li>Keep in mind that tracking derived properties reduces merge performance.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><ul><li><strong>Select which changes to detect in diagrams:<br /></strong>To detect changes only in diagrams that were loaded (open) when the changes were made, set the <strong>Detect Changes in Diagrams</strong> option to <strong>Direct Changes</strong>. Only changed/updated and merged elements are locked in the project, not the whole project. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998805 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01419: (2026x Refresh1) Presentation Mode

- page_id: `303998805`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998805/2026x+Refresh1+Presentation+Mode
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:08.749+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

57060115

INLINE

57060123

INLINE

57079926

INLINE

**On this page**

**33**

57060122

INLINE

Presentation Mode enables users to present a selected diagram or sequence of diagrams in Full Screen mode without showing the status line, diagram palette, Model Browser, toolbars, and main menu. Additionally, it helps prevent unintended changes that may occur during a presentation.

700500

##### Presentation Mode workflow

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Content Diagram Wizard' space='']
2. 
3. 
4. 

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Enabled Presentation Mode with a defined sequence of diagrams

Creating sequenceTo create a sequence of diagrams

1. Open the created Content diagram with the displayed diagram content.
2. Do one of the following:
  - In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface' space=''] , select Presentation Flow. [ATTACHMENT filename='diagram_palette_presentation_mode.png']
  - Select a diagram shape and then in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Smart manipulator toolbar' space=''] , click Presentation Flow. [ATTACHMENT filename='presentation_flow_smart_manipulator.png']
3. Connect the selected diagrams in the sequence you want to present them.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can navigate through diagrams that are connected using Presentation Flow only.

Only diagram shapes can be connected in a sequence.

Enter presentation modeTo enter Presentation Mode

Do one of the following:

- In the main menu, click Window > Editor Tabs > Enter Presentation Mode .
- In the bottom right corner of the status line, click the Presentation Mode icon. [ATTACHMENT filename='presentation_mode_from_bar.png']
- In the smart manipulator toolbar of the diagram you want to present, click the Presentation Mode icon. [ATTACHMENT filename='entering_presentation_mode_from_smart_manipulator_toolbar.png']

You can enter Presentation Mode from any diagram in a defined sequence.

Diagrams cannot be opened in new tabs if Presentation Mode is active.

Exit presentation modeTo switch back to the normal mode

Do one of the following:

- Press the Esc button on your keyboard.
- Click X in the diagram tab next to the diagram name. [ATTACHMENT filename='closing_presentation_mode.png']

##### Simulation in Presentation Mode

You can show diagram simulations in Presentation Mode as well. Once the simulation is started, a group of buttons for managing a simulation flow appears at the bottom of the window next to the Presentation Mode control buttons (see the diagram below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To learn more about diagram simulation, click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram' space='MMA'].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993707 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01420: (2026x Refresh1) Presentation Report Designer diagram

- page_id: `303993707`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993707/2026x+Refresh1+Presentation+Report+Designer+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:38.802+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Report Designer
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In this section, you will learn how to create the Presentation Report Designer diagram and generate it to a PPTX template that you can add in Report Wizard for generating reports later.

To create the Presentation Report Designer diagram and generate it to a PPTX template

1. Right-click a Package in the Containment tree and select Report Designer Diagrams > Presentation Report Designer Diagram . [ATTACHMENT filename='Presentation Report Designer Diagram.png']
2. Create diagram elements in the working area of the diagram and specify values.
3. Arrange diagram elements as needed. [ATTACHMENT filename='Arranging Diagram Elements3.png']
4. In the Containment tree, right-click **Presentation Report Designer Diagram** and select **Generate Template** > **PPTX Template** to generate the template. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] NoteGenerated templates get only symbols on the diagram. Elements in the Containment tree are not used to generate templates.
5. Refine more layouts, style, and code logic in Microsoft PowerPoint.
6. Add the complete template to Report Wizard.
7. Generate the report from the added template. [ATTACHMENT filename='Generating Report from Added Template2.png'] [ATTACHMENT filename='Generating Report from Added Template3.png']

#### Diagram elements

The Presentation Report Designer diagram provides many diagram elements represented in Velocity syntax/directive that can be created from the toolbar on the diagram. You can see diagram elements description in the [Document Report Designer diagram](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Document+Report+Designer+diagram) page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In this section, you will learn how to create the Presentation Report Designer diagram and generate it to a PPTX template that you can add in Report Wizard for generating reports later.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create the Presentation Report Designer diagram and generate it to a PPTX template</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click a Package in the Containment tree and select <strong>Report Designer Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Presentation Report Designer Diagram</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Presentation Report Designer Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Presentation Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Create diagram elements in the working area of the diagram and specify values.</li><li>Arrange diagram elements as needed.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Arranging Diagram Elements3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Presentation Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click <strong>Presentation Report Designer Diagram</strong> and select <strong>Generate Template</strong> &gt; <strong>PPTX Template</strong> to generate the template.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="PPTX Template.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Presentation Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="346b6768-2d5f-48fb-9981-cb53fa835ce4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Generated templates get only symbols on the diagram. Elements in the Containment tree are not used to generate templates.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Refine more layouts, style, and code logic in Microsoft PowerPoint.</li><li>Add the complete template to Report Wizard.</li><li>Generate the report from the added template.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generating Report from Added Template2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Presentation Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generating Report from Added Template3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Presentation Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h2>Diagram elements</h2><p>The Presentation Report Designer diagram provides many diagram elements represented in Velocity syntax/directive that can be created from the toolbar on the diagram. You can see diagram elements description in the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Document+Report+Designer+diagram">Document Report Designer diagram</a> page.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998998 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01421: (2026x Refresh1) Preserving Sub Projects in PV-2

- page_id: `303998998`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998998/2026x+Refresh1+Preserving+Sub+Projects+in+PV-2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:21.377+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > (2026x Refresh1) Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Issue**

If Actual Project type is read-only, its Sub Projects do not migrate in PV-2 diagram. There is no possibility to create a Sub Projects for Actual Projects that type is read only element. Anyway, all non migrated Sub Projects of each Actual Project are listed in Specification window [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using To Do property' space=''].

It is possible to create a Sub Projects for Actual projects which types are not read only in UAF.

**Solution**

To avoid this issue, it is recommended to change the Actual Project type from read only to editablebefore the migration process.

If the type is used from another project, you need to move/copy that type to current project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Issue</strong></p><p>If Actual Project <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">type is read-only</span>, its Sub Projects do not migrate in PV-2 diagram. There is no possibility to create a Sub Projects for Actual Projects that type is read only element. Anyway, all non migrated Sub Projects of each Actual Project are listed in Specification window <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using To Do property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To DO property]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="12d22ee9-61cb-4c74-ab88-d3a68a71b98c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is possible to create a Sub Projects for Actual projects which types are not read only in <span>UAF</span>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Solution</strong></p><p>To avoid this issue, it is recommended to change the Actual Project type from read only to <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">editable </span>before the migration process.<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eadc2f9b-a48c-45ad-9643-89d8737271f7"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">If the type is used from another project, you need to move/copy <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">that type</span> to current project.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

## PAGE 01422: (2026x Refresh1) Preserving Sub Projects in PV-2 in UAF 1.2

- page_id: `303999233`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999233/2026x+Refresh1+Preserving+Sub+Projects+in+PV-2+in+UAF+1.2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:41.612+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2 > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Issue**

If Actual Project type is read-only, its Sub Projects do not migrate in PV-2 diagram. There is no possibility to create a Sub Projects for Actual Projects that type is read only element. Anyway, all non migrated Sub Projects of each Actual Project are listed in Specification window [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using To Do property' space=''].

It is possible to create a Sub Projects for Actual projects which types are not read only in UAF.

**Solution**

To avoid this issue, it is recommended to change the Actual Project type from read only to editablebefore the migration process.

If the type is used from another project, you need to move/copy that type to current project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Issue</strong></p><p>If Actual Project <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">type is read-only</span>, its Sub Projects do not migrate in PV-2 diagram. There is no possibility to create a Sub Projects for Actual Projects that type is read only element. Anyway, all non migrated Sub Projects of each Actual Project are listed in Specification window <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using To Do property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To DO property]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="12d22ee9-61cb-4c74-ab88-d3a68a71b98c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is possible to create a Sub Projects for Actual projects which types are not read only in <span>UAF</span>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Solution</strong></p><p>To avoid this issue, it is recommended to change the Actual Project type from read only to <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">editable </span>before the migration process.<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eadc2f9b-a48c-45ad-9643-89d8737271f7"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">If the type is used from another project, you need to move/copy <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">that type</span> to current project.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991943 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01423: (2026x Refresh1) Preview Affected Diagrams dialog

- page_id: `303991943`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991943/2026x+Refresh1+Preview+Affected+Diagrams+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:21.417+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Item Flow management
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Preview Affected Diagrams** dialog is dedicated to reviewing the diagrams that will be affected when the Item Flow is realized. The dialog can be opened from the following:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Item Flow' space='']Create/Edit Item Flowdialog
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Item Flow Manager dialog' space='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The **Preview Affected Diagrams** dialog contains all the affected diagrams. You can preview them one by one. The affected elements where the created flows will be realized, are marked in the diagram preview pane.

While previewing the diagrams, you can select the **Update Diagram** check box for those diagrams you want to show the Conveyed Items of the selected flow.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The <strong>Preview Affected Diagrams</strong> dialog is dedicated to reviewing the diagrams that will be affected when the Item Flow is realized. The dialog can be opened from the following:</span></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating Item Flow" /><ac:link-body>Create/Edit Item Flow<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>dialog</span></ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Item Flow Manager dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Item Flow Manager]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="preview_affected_diagrams_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Preview Affected Diagrams dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><span><br /></span></p><p><span>The <strong>Preview Affected Diagrams</strong> dialog contains all the affected diagrams. You can preview them one by one. The affected elements where the created flows will be realized, are marked in the diagram preview pane.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">While previewing the diagrams, you can select the <strong>Update Diagram</strong> check box for those diagrams you want to show the Conveyed Items of the selected flow.</span></p><p><br /></p>
````

- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000037/2026x+Refresh1+Preview+Affected+Diagrams+dialog+in+UAF
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:40.710+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Dialogs
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Preview Affected Diagrams** dialog is dedicated to review the diagrams which will be affected when the <viewpoint name>* Exchange will be realized. The dialog can be opened from the following:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Preview Affected Diagrams dialog contains all the affected diagrams. You can preview them one by one. The affected elements where the created Exchanges will be realized, are marked in the diagram preview pane.

While previewing the diagrams, you can select the **Update Diagram** check box for those diagrams you want to show the Conveyed Items of the selected exchange.

*** <viewpoint name>**- Operational, Personnel, Resources, Services.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The dialog can be opened from the following:</span></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Realized Service Exchange wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Service Exchange Manager dialog" /></ac:link></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /></ac:link></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /></ac:link></span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="preview_affected_v2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Preview Affected Diagrams dialog in UAF" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The Preview Affected Diagrams dialog contains all the affected diagrams. ## PAGE 01425: (2026x Refresh1) Print dialog

To open the Print dialog, do one of the following

- From the File menu, select Print .
- Press CTRL+P.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995818 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01426: (2026x Refresh1) Printing

- page_id: `303995818`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995818/2026x+Refresh1+Printing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:26:05.090+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can print:

- Active diagram
- Any diagram(s)
- All open diagrams
- Selected symbols of active diagram

You can specify the printing options or preview pages by using the following dialogs:

- Print Dialog
- Print Options Dialog
- Print Preview Window

To print an active diagram

1. Open a diagram you want to print.
2. Do one of the following:
  - In the main toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='print.png'] Print Active Diagram button.
  - From the File menu, select Print . On the Print dialog, Print what area, select Active diagram , and click the Print button.

To print any diagram(s)

1. From the File main menu, select Print . The Print dialog opens.
2. In the Print what area, choose Selected diagram(s) . The Select Diagram dialog opens.
3. Select diagram(s) you want to print.
4. Click OK .
5. Click Print .

To print all open diagrams

1. From the File main menu, select the Print command. The Print dialog opens.
2. In the Print what area, select All opened diagram(s) .
3. Click Print .

To print selected symbols of active diagram

1. On the diagram, select symbols you want to print.
2. Do one of the following:
  - From the selected symbols' shortcut menu, select Print Selected .
  - From the File menu, select Print . In the Print dialog, Print what area, choose Selected symbols of active diagram , and click the Print button.

You can specify the diagram export options, such as dimensions and building time (the latter applies only to tables and matrices), which affect diagram print/preview, in the **Project Options** dialog. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Diagram image export' space=''] page to learn more.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Diagramming' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Saving as image' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Diagram image export' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

On the Print dialog, <strong>Print what</strong> area, select <strong>Active diagram</strong>, and click the <strong>Print</strong> button.</li></ul></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To print any diagram(s)</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>File</strong> main menu, select <strong>Print</strong>. The <strong>Print</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Print what</strong> area, choose <strong>Selected diagram(s)</strong>. The <strong>Select Diagram</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select diagram(s) you want to print.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Print</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To print all open diagrams</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>File</strong> main menu, select the <strong>Print</strong> command. The <strong>Print</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Print what</strong> area, select <strong>All opened diagram(s)</strong>. </li><li>Click <strong>Print</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To print selected symbols of active diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>On the diagram, select symbols you want to print.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>From the selected symbols' shortcut menu, select <strong>Print Selected</strong>.</li><li>From the <strong>File</strong> menu, select <strong>Print</strong>. In the <strong>Print</strong> dialog, <strong>Print what</strong> area, choose <strong>Selected symbols of active diagram</strong>, and click the <strong>Print</strong> button.<br /><br /></li></ul></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="519fb3a1-8e91-45c4-8872-407c6de756da"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can specify the diagram export options, such as dimensions and building time (the latter applies only to tables and matrices), which affect diagram print/preview, in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Diagram image export" /></ac:link> page to learn more.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Diagramming" /></ac:link><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Saving as image" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Diagram image export" /></ac:link></li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989842 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01428: (2026x Refresh1) Process Definition Diagram

- page_id: `303989842`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303989842/2026x+Refresh1+Process+Definition+Diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:54.377+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Supportive Diagrams Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A Process Definition diagram is a tool for initial process analysis. The diagram allows you to draw business processes, group them into packages, and define the relations between those processes. 
When the initial process definition has been completed, a process flow can be provided in a BPMN Process diagram, which can be created for each process.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Process Definition diagram

**Related elements**

- Package
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) BPMN Relationships' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A Process Definition diagram is a tool for initial process analysis. The diagram allows you to draw business processes, group them into packages, and define the relations between those processes.<br />When the initial process definition has been completed, a process flow can be provided in a BPMN Process diagram, which can be created for each process.</p><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="process_definition_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Process Definition Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Process Definition diagram</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul class="childpages-macro"><li data-uuid="a82b0fd8-3502-411d-b14f-9cacf790d5d5"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Package">Package</a></li><li data-uuid="1b4cff72-0e60-4455-a6ea-e011c92c1f3b"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) BPMN Relationships" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Relationships]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000032 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01429: (2026x Refresh1) Producing and Consuming Activities dialog

- page_id: `304000032`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000032/2026x+Refresh1+Producing+and+Consuming+Activities+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:40.117+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Dialogs
- labels: ['producing-and-consuming-activities-dialog', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Producing and Consuming Activities** dialog allows you to review and modify Producing and Consuming Activities for a selected Operational Exchange.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Producing and Consuming Activities** dialog

- In the Specification window of the Operational Exchange, select Producing Activity / Consuming Activity property value cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
- In the Operational Connectivity Table or Operational Role-based Connectivity Table, select Producing Operational Activity / Consuming Operational Activity cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog , select Producing Operational Activity / Consuming Operational Activity cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .

The buttons of the dialog are described in the following table.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Remove From Table | Click to delete a selected Producing and Consuming Activities pair. |
| Show Full Paths | Toggle either to show or hide the full element paths. |

To remove the realization between listed Activity pairs

1. Select the needed Activity pair.
2. Click the Remove From Table button.

1005096879

INLINE

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']
