<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000047 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01430: (2026x Refresh1) Producing and Consuming Functions dialog

- page_id: `304000047`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000047/2026x+Refresh1+Producing+and+Consuming+Functions+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:41.817+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Dialogs
- labels: ['producing-and-consuming-functions-dialog', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Producing and Consuming Functions** dialog allows you to review and modify the list of Producing and Consuming Functions for a selected Resource Exchange.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Producing and Consuming Functions** dialog

- In the Specification window of the Resource Exchange, select Producing Function / Consuming Function property value cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
- In the Resources Connectivity Table, Resource Role-based Connectivity Table, Personnel Connectivity Table, Personnel Role-based Connectivity Table, Security Connectivity Table, or Security Role-based Connectivity Table, select Producing Function / Consuming Function cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager dialog** , Select Producing Function / Consuming Function cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .

The buttons of the dialog are described in the following table.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Remove From Table | Click to delete a selected Producing and Consuming Functions pair. |
| Show Full Paths | Toggle either to show or hide the full element paths. |

To remove the realization between listed Function pairs

1. Select the needed Functions pair.
2. Click the Remove From Table button.

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000041 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01431: (2026x Refresh1) Producing and Consuming Service Functions dialog

- page_id: `304000041`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000041/2026x+Refresh1+Producing+and+Consuming+Service+Functions+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:41.186+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Dialogs
- labels: ['producing-and-consuming-functions-dialog', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Producing and Consuming Functions** dialog allows you to review and modify the list of Producing and Consuming Service Functions for a selected Service Exchange.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Producing and Consuming Service Functions** dialog

- In the Specification window of the Resource Exchange, select Producing Service Function / Consuming Service Function property value cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
- In the Services Connectivity Table or Service Role-based Connectivity Table, select Producing Service Function / Consuming Service Function cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Service Exchange Manager dialog** , Select Producing Service Function / Consuming Service Function cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .

The buttons of the dialog are described in the following table.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Remove From Table | Click to delete a selected Producing and Consuming Service Functions pair. |
| Show Full Paths | Toggle either to show or hide the full element paths. |

To remove the realization between listed Service Function pairs

1. Select the needed Service Functions pair.
2. Click the Remove From Table button.

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999089 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01432: (2026x Refresh1) Products Mapping

- page_id: `303999089`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999089/2026x+Refresh1+Products+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:29.008+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF Migration to UPDM 1.x > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF Projects Migration
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Products mapping converts DoDAF products into UPDM ones. It changes a product's name and representation form whenever required by the UPDM 1.1 plugin. DoDAF product migration is described in details in Table 1 on page 6 below.

| DoDAF 1.5 | UPDM 1.x |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Product | Representation Form | Product | Representation Form |
| OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic | UML Class Diagram | OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic | UML Composite Structure Diagram |
| OV-2 Operational Node Connectivity Description | UML Class Diagram | OV-2 Operational Node Relationship Description | UML Class Diagram |
| OV-3 Operational Information Exchange Matrix | Report | OV-3 Operational Information Exchange Matrix | Table |
| OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart | UML Class Diagram | OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart | UML Class Diagram |
| OV-5 Operational Activity Model | UML Class DiagramUML Activity Diagram | OV-5 Operational Activity Model | UML Class DiagramUML Activity Diagram |
| OV-6a Operational Rules Model | Report | OV-6a Operational Rules Model | Table |
| OV-6b Operational State Transition Description | UML State Machine Diagram | OV-6b Operational State Transition Description | UML State Machine Diagram |
| OV-6c Operational Event-TraceDescription | UML Sequence Diagram | OV-6c Operational Event-TraceDescription | UML Sequence Diagram |
| OV-7 Logical Data Model | UML Class Diagram | OV-7 Information Model | UML Class Diagram |
| SV-1 Systems Interface Description | UML Class DiagramUML Composite Structure Diagram | SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification | UML Class DiagramUML Composite Structure Diagram |
| SV-2 Systems Communications Description | UML Class DiagramUML Composite Structure Diagram | SV-2 Resource Communicationsdescription | UML Class DiagramUML Composite Structure Diagram |
| SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix | Matrix | SV-3 Resource Interaction Matrix | Matrix |
| SV-4 Systems Functionality Description | UML Class DiagramUML Activity Diagram | SV-4 Functionality Description | UML Class DiagramUML Activity Diagram |
| SV-5 Operational Activity to SystemsFunction Traceability Matrix | Matrix | SV-5 Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix | Editable Matrix |
| SV-6 Systems Data Exchange Matrix | Report | SV-6 Systems Data Exchange Matrix | Table |
| SV-7 Systems Performance Parameters Matrix | Table | SV-7 Resource Performance Parameters Matrix | Actual and Typical Tables |
| SV-8 Systems Evolution Description | UML State Machine Diagram | SV-8 Capability Configuration Management | UML Class Diagram |
| SV-9 Systems Technology Forecast | Table | SV-9 Technology and Skills Forecast | Table |
| SV-10a Systems Rules Model | Report | SV-10a Resource ConstraintsSpecification | Table |
| SV-10b Systems State Transition Description | UML State Machine Diagram | SV-10b Resource State TransitionDescription | UML State Machine Diagram |
| SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description | UML Sequence Diagram | SV-10c Resource Event-Trace Description | UML Sequence Diagram |
| SV-11 Physical Schema | UML Class Diagram | SV-11 Physical Schema | UML Class Diagram |
| TV-1 Technical Standards Profile | Table | Does not map | - |
| TV-2 Technical Standards Forecast | Table | TV-2 Standards Forecast | Table |

Do not be surprised, if you will not find any OV-3, OV-6a, SV-6 and SV-10a reports after migrating to UPDM 1.x. These reports do not exist in UPDM 1.x. There are tables instead. They can be easily filled with data, conveniently updated, and printed as any other MagicDraw diagrams.

These tables, however, will not be created automatically after migrating to UPDM 1.x. We suggest you to create them manually by selecting the migrated data you would like to see in each one.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It changes a product's name and representation form whenever required by the UPDM 1.1 plugin. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994004 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01433: (2026x Refresh1) Profile diagram

- page_id: `303994004`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994004/2026x+Refresh1+Profile+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:48.227+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) UML diagrams
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

308723076

308723078

308723077

**Overview**

In general, a Profile diagram has been introduced in UML 2.0 to display the usage of profiles. It is an extensibility mechanism that allows extending and customizing UML. To be more specific, the Profiles package contains mechanisms that allow metaclasses from existing metamodels to be extended to adapt them for different purposes.

**Purpose**

A Profile Diagram extends UML by adding new building blocks, creating new properties, and specifying new semantics so that the language would be suitable to the specific domain, for example, medicine, financial services, or engineering.

**Usage**

A Profile diagram extends UML to allow the use of:

- user-defined stereotypes
- meta attributes
- constraints.

**Summary**

Profile diagrams are valuable because they allow for adaptation of the UML metamodel for:

- different platforms (e.g., Java Platform, Microsoft .NET Framework, Enterprise Edition (Java EE), etc.)
- domains (e.g., business process modeling, medical applications, service-oriented architecture)

If the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED'],[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Type' space='MED'],[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Primitive' space='MED'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enumeration' space='MED'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MED'], Direct Association, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='MED'] are not available on the diagram palette, make sure you are working in the Expert mode.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Profile diagram

308723075

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating diagrams' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998212 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01434: (2026x Refresh1) Profile Management dialog

- page_id: `303998212`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998212/2026x+Refresh1+Profile+Management+dialog
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:47.144+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Generating Reports from Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Uploading reports to remote locations
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section explains the following items

22

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Profile Management dialog.

#### Profile Management dialog buttons

The Profile Management dialog contains five buttons:

|  | Adds a new profile to the list of existing Profiles. You can neither create a profile with a blank name, nor with a name that already been used. You can add and edit multiple Profiles. However, these profiles will not be saved until you click the Save Profiles button. |
| --- | --- |
|  | Removes the currently selected profile. Note that you cannot remove the "No Upload" profile. |
|  | Saves all profiles. If new profiles have been added or existing Profiles have been modified, then these changes will be saved. All profiles will be checked for consistency or information correctness. You will be asked to fix issues, if any. It is not possible to save profiles as long as invalid information is detected. See Profile Management dialog fields below for the description of "valid profile". |
|  | Abandons all newly-added profiles and all changes made to existing profiles. If you do not make any changes to any profiles, clicking this button will bring you back to the Output Options pane of the Report Wizard dialog. Otherwise, the Confirm Cancel dialog will open. In this dialog, you will see the names of the profiles whose content you have changed. You can choose to either abandon all changes, or return to the Profile Management dialog and save the changes. |
|  | Works similar to the Cancel button. |

#### Profile Management dialog fields

Profiles describe information necessary for sending a report to a remote location. The report will be saved remotely with the same name as the locally-generated report, and will overwrite any file with the same name on the remote location.

The Address field is URL sensitive, and can hold protocol as well as username information. If the protocol or username information is found in the Address field, the corresponding fields, namely the Protocol and Username fields, will be updated with the values from the Address field. At the very least, a profile must have a hostname in the Address field.

You cannot save a profile unless this condition is met. The Username and Password fields contain sensitive data and, therefore, are optional. If these fields are empty in a profile, you will be prompted for your username and password when the server asks for your authentication.

- Profile name : This drop-down list holds all the existing Profiles. They are alphabetically sorted. The "No upload" profile is always available, and you can neither modify, nor remove it. Select the "No Upload" profile if you do not want to upload a report.
- Protocol : Five different protocols are supported: FTP, FTP over SSL (Secure Socket Layer), Webdav, Webdav over SSL, and SSH (Secure Shell) . You can either choose to select a protocol from the protocol drop-down list, or fill in the scheme in the address part of the profile. Certificates that are sent by servers using secure traffic (FTP over SSL, Webdav over SSL, and SSH) are silently accepted. The scheme identifiers and default port numbers of the five supported protocols are listed in the table below.

The table below lists Scheme Identifier and Default Port Numbers of Five Supported Protocols

| Protocol Name | Scheme | Default Port Number | Traffic Mode |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ftp | ftp:// | 21 | Plain text |
| webdav | http:// | 80 | Plain text |
| ftp + ssl | ftps:// | 990 | Encrypted & Secure |
| webdav + ssl | https:// | 443 | Encrypted & Secure |
| ssh | ssh:// or sftp:// | 22 | Encrypted & Secure |

- Address

The Address field is the central part of a profile. It can set other fields, except the Password field. An Address contains five different parts: scheme, username, hostname, port (number), and (remote) path. Except hostname, every part is optional. Depending on the circumstances, you might need to add a special port number and/or the remote path where you have write access. Even though an address is valid without the path part, most servers will require a path, and will not let you write to the root directory. In every case, scheme and username parts will take precedence over the selection in the Protocol Field or the content in the Username field. The following is the basic pattern for the address field:

[scheme://][username@]hostname[:port][path]

1. scheme : The supported schemes are ftp, ftps, http, https, ssh, or sftp. A scheme must be followed by “://”.
2. username : A username is needed for user authentication. It must be followed by “@”.
3. hostname : Must be either an IP address (for example, 10.1.1.195) or a human readable URL (for example, www.ftpserver.com ), and a valid address.
4. port : A port number where the server is listed. It is only necessary if the port number is different from the default port for well-known services. It must be preceded by ":".
5. path : The predefined path will start from the home directory of the specified username. Therefore, the report will be generated at the home directory of the username, e.g., if the address is [ssh://10.1.1.97:22/mymodel/publish](ssh://10.1.1.97:22/mymodel/publish) , and the username is developer , the report will be generated at the home directory of the developer username. The designated path will be /home/developer/mymodel/publish .

Examples of valid addresses:

1. 10.1.1.195 (hostname only)
2. 10.1.1.195/companyDav (hostname and path)
3. 10.1.1.195:80/companyDav (hostname, port number, and path)
4. ftp://www.ftpserver.com (scheme and hostname)
5. john@10.1.1.195 (username and hostname)
6. john@www.ftpserver.com /users/john/reports (username, hostname, and path)
7. ssh://john@10.1.2.35:22/reports (all parts)

- User Name

This field specifies a username that will be used for authentication. If the address contains a username part, the content of the Username field will be ignored. It will be either filled in or overwritten with the username from the Address. A Profile with an empty username field is valid, but you will be prompted for a username and password every time you upload to the server using this profile.

- Password

A profile with a password must always be accompanied by a username. A Profile with a password, but not a username, is invalid and, therefore, cannot be saved. The password field uses an '*' to represent a character in a password. Saving a profile with a password will save the password in an encrypted form. For example, if the password is 'test', it will be encrypted to '0013BCA5590DE'. A Profile with an empty password field is valid, but you will be prompted for a password every time you upload a report to the server in this profile.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This section explains the following items</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f20ee0f4-0cc4-4022-afe9-db3005577203"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">2</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Profile Management Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Profile Management dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Profile Management dialog.</h6><h2>Profile Management dialog buttons</h2><p>The Profile Management dialog contains five buttons:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Profile Management dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></td><td><span>Adds a new profile to the list of existing Profiles. You can neither create a profile with a blank name, nor with a name that already been used. You can add and edit multiple Profiles. However, these profiles will not be saved until you click the </span><strong>Save Profiles</strong><span> button.</span></td></tr><tr><td><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Delete_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Profile Management dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></td><td><span>Removes the currently selected profile. Note that you cannot remove the &quot;No Upload&quot; profile.</span></td></tr><tr><td><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="SaveProfiles_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Profile Management dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></td><td><span>Saves all profiles. If new profiles have been added or existing Profiles have been modified, then these changes will be saved. All profiles will be checked for consistency or information correctness. You will be asked to fix issues, if any. It is not possible to save profiles as long as invalid information is detected. See </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Uploading+reports+to+remote+locations#Uploadingreportstoremotelocations-ProfileManagementFi">Profile Management dialog fields</a><span> below for the description of &quot;valid profile&quot;.</span></td></tr><tr><td><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Profile Management dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></td><td><span>Abandons all newly-added profiles and all changes made to existing profiles. If you do not make any changes to any profiles, clicking this button will bring you back to the </span><strong>Output Options</strong><span> pane of the Report Wizard dialog. Otherwise, the </span><strong>Confirm Cancel</strong><span> dialog will open. In this dialog, you will see the names of the profiles whose content you have changed. You can choose to either abandon all changes, or return to the </span><strong>Profile Management</strong><span> dialog and save the changes.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Exit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Profile Management dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></td><td colspan="1"><span>Works similar to the </span><strong>Cancel</strong><span> button.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><h2>Profile Management dialog fields</h2><p>Profiles describe information necessary for sending a report to a remote location. The report will be saved remotely with the same name as the locally-generated report, and will overwrite any file with the same name on the remote location.</p><p>The Address field is URL sensitive, and can hold protocol as well as username information. If the protocol or username information is found in the Address field, the corresponding fields, namely the Protocol and Username fields, will be updated with the values from the Address field. At the very least, a profile must have a hostname in the Address field.</p><p>You cannot save a profile unless this condition is met. The Username and Password fields contain sensitive data and, therefore, are optional. If these fields are empty in a profile, you will be prompted for your username and password when the server asks for your authentication.</p><ul><li><strong>Profile name</strong>: This drop-down list holds all the existing Profiles. They are alphabetically sorted. The &quot;No upload&quot; profile is always available, and you can neither modify, nor remove it. Select the &quot;No Upload&quot; profile if you do not want to upload a report.</li><li><strong>Protocol</strong>: Five different protocols are supported: <em>FTP, FTP over SSL (Secure Socket Layer), Webdav, Webdav over SSL, and SSH (Secure Shell)</em>. You can either choose to select a protocol from the protocol drop-down list, or fill in the scheme in the address part of the profile. Certificates that are sent by servers using secure traffic (FTP over SSL, Webdav over SSL, and SSH) are silently accepted. The scheme identifiers and default port numbers of the five supported protocols are listed in the table below.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The table below lists Scheme Identifier and Default Port Numbers of Five Supported Protocols</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th colspan="1">Protocol Name</th><th colspan="1"> Scheme</th><th colspan="1">Default Port Number</th><th colspan="1">Traffic Mode</th></tr><tr><td>ftp</td><td> ftp://</td><td> 21</td><td>Plain text </td></tr><tr><td>webdav </td><td> http:// </td><td> 80</td><td>Plain text </td></tr><tr><td>ftp + ssl </td><td> ftps://</td><td>990 </td><td>Encrypted &amp; Secure </td></tr><tr><td>webdav + ssl </td><td> https://</td><td>443 </td><td>Encrypted &amp; Secure</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">ssh</td><td colspan="1">ssh:// or sftp://</td><td colspan="1">22</td><td colspan="1">Encrypted &amp; Secure</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><ul><li><strong>Address</strong></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The Address field is the central part of a profile. It can set other fields, except the Password field. An Address contains five different parts: scheme, username, hostname, port (number), and (remote) path. Except hostname, every part is optional. Depending on the circumstances, you might need to add a special port number and/or the remote path where you have write access. Even though an address is valid without the path part, most servers will require a path, and will not let you write to the root directory. In every case, scheme and username parts will take precedence over the selection in the Protocol Field or the content in the Username field. The following is the basic pattern for the address field:  </p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">[scheme://][username@]hostname[:port][path]</p><ol><li><strong>scheme</strong>: <br />The supported schemes are ftp, ftps, http, https, ssh, or sftp. It must be followed by “@”. </li><li><strong>hostname</strong>: <br />Must be either an IP address (for example, 10.1.1.195) or a human readable URL (for example, <a class="external-link" href="http://www.ftpserver.com/" rel="nofollow">www.ftpserver.com</a>), and a valid address.</li><li><strong>port</strong>: <br />A port number where the server is listed. Therefore, the report will be generated at the home directory of the username, e.g., if the address is <em><a href="ssh://10.1.1.97:22/mymodel/publish" rel="nofollow">ssh://10.1.1.97:22/mymodel/publish</a></em>, and the username is <em>developer</em>, the report will be generated at the home directory of the <em>developer</em> username. The designated path will be <em>/home/developer/mymodel/publish</em>.</li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Examples of valid addresses:</p><ol><li>10.1.1.195 (hostname only)</li><li>10.1.1.195/companyDav (hostname and path)</li><li>10.1.1.195:80/companyDav (hostname, port number, and path)</li><li><a href="ftp://www.ftpserver.com/" class="external-link" rel="nofollow">ftp://www.ftpserver.com</a> (scheme and hostname)</li><li><a class="external-link" href="mailto:john@10.1.1.195" rel="nofollow">john@10.1.1.195</a> (username and hostname)</li><li><a class="external-link" href="mailto:john@www.ftpserver.com" rel="nofollow">john@www.ftpserver.com</a>/users/john/reports (username, hostname, and path)</li><li><a href="ssh://john@10.1.2.35:22/reports" rel="nofollow">ssh://john@10.1.2.35:22/reports</a> (all parts)</li></ol><ul><li><strong>User Name</strong></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">This field specifies a username that will be used for authentication. ## PAGE 01435: (2026x Refresh1) Profile Migration Transformation

- page_id: `303995581`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995581/2026x+Refresh1+Profile+Migration+Transformation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:48.981+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools > (2026x Refresh1) Model Transformation Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Transformation provides a way to migrate your model according specified mapping rules.

Profile migration transformation search-and-replaces the following elements:

- Applied stereotypes
- Tagged values
- Usages of one type with another type

Element symbols are updated according to model changes.

Before starting transformation you have to define transformation mapping.

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Transformation provides a way to migrate your model according specified mapping rules.</p><p>Profile migration transformation search-and-replaces the following elements:</p><ul><li>Applied stereotypes</li><li>Tagged values</li><li><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">Usages of one type with another type</span></li></ul><p>Element symbols are updated according to model changes.</p><p>Before starting transformation you have to define transformation mapping.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1559d803-b9a2-44d9-ba79-24b69de5050a"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995585 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01436: (2026x Refresh1) Profile Migration Transformation mapping

- page_id: `303995585`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995585/2026x+Refresh1+Profile+Migration+Transformation+mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:49.957+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools > (2026x Refresh1) Model Transformation Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Profile Migration Transformation
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before starting transformation you have to create transformation mapping rules.

To create mapping rules you need to create Dependency relationship between elements you want to transform.

Mapping rules can be created (dependency relationship can be created) between the following elements:

1. Stereotypes
2. Tags
3. Types

###### Creating mapping rules for Stereotype transformation

This type of transformation is used to replace Stereotype.

To create Stereotype transformation mapping rule

1. Create Dependency relationship between Stereotypes which you want to transform.
2. Apply ReplaceStereotype stereotype to Dependency.
3. Perform transformation.

Tag values of old stereotype are preserved when tag name and type of tag value is the same. For tag values with different names create mapping rule for tag transformation.

*ReplaceStereotype* stereotype has the following tags:

- disableNewTypeCreation tag. By default false. Set this tag to true if you do not want to perform transformation when target and source metaclasses are not compatible. For example, if you do not want that Class would be changed to Use Case.
- disableReplaceWhereSaveAsElementValue tag. By default false. Set this tag to true if you do not want that stereotype would be changed where it is used as Tag value (tag value is stereotype itself, for which you perform transformation).

###### Creating mapping rules for Tag transformation

This type of transformation is used to replace Tag (when tag names differs). For example, source stereotype has author Tag and target stereotype has name Tag.

To create Tag transformation mapping rule

1. Create Dependency relationship between Tags which you want to transform.
2. Apply ReplaceTaggedValue stereotype to Dependency.
3. Perform transformation.

To create mapping rule correctly, you have to create Dependency relationship not only between Tags, but also between Stereotypes of these tags (see mapping between stereotypes, which is described above).

###### Creating mapping rules for types transformation

This type of transformation is used to replace type. For example, to replace type of Attribute.

To create types transformation mapping rule

1. Create Dependency relationship between Types which you want to transform.
2. Apply ReplaceType stereotype to Dependency.
3. Perform transformation.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

For tag values with different names create mapping rule for tag transformation.</p><p><em>ReplaceStereotype</em> stereotype has the following tags:</p><ul><li><strong>disableNewTypeCreation</strong> tag. By default false. Set this tag to true if you do not want to perform transformation when target and source metaclasses are not compatible. For example, if you do not want that Class would be changed to Use Case. </li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">disableReplaceWhereSaveAsElementValue</strong> tag. By default false. Set this tag to true if you do not want that stereotype would be changed where it is used as Tag value (tag value is stereotype itself, for which you perform transformation).</li></ul><h4>Creating mapping rules for Tag transformation</h4><p>This type of transformation is used to replace Tag (when tag names differs). For example, source stereotype has author Tag and target stereotype has name Tag.</p><p> </p><p>To create Tag transformation mapping rule</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Dependency relationship between Tags which you want to transform.</li><li>Apply <em>ReplaceTaggedValue</em> stereotype to Dependency.</li><li>Perform transformation.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e2a42d0-cd17-4429-80d5-f568021f30ef"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create mapping rule correctly, you have to create Dependency relationship not only between Tags, but also between Stereotypes of these tags (see mapping between stereotypes, which is described above).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><h4>Creating mapping rules for types transformation</h4><p>This type of transformation is used to replace type. For example, to replace type of Attribute.</p><p> </p><p>To create types transformation mapping rule</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Dependency relationship between Types which you want to transform.</li><li>Apply <em>ReplaceType</em> stereotype to Dependency.</li><li>Perform transformation.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999762 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01437: (2026x Refresh1) Profiling changes

- page_id: `303999762`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999762/2026x+Refresh1+Profiling+changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:15.007+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) UML 2.5.1 support
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The previously implemented UML profiling metamodelused Instance Specifications, Slots and Value Specificationsto store the tagged values ofappliedstereotypes.Since that approach required two model elements (Slot and Value Specification) to store each tagged value, it has been optimized.

The InstanceSpecifications, Slots, and ValueSpecifications are no longer used to store profiling data.Instead,theElementdirectlyreferences the TaggedValue that is used to specify the Boolean, Integer, Real, String, or Elementtaggedvalues of the applied stereotype.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Profiling changes in the UML metamodel.

As a consequence, expressions based on the previously implemented profiling data storing metamodel no longer work; they have to be updated manually.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998357 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01438: (2026x Refresh1) Profiling tool

- page_id: `303998357`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998357/2026x+Refresh1+Profiling+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:32.083+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Helper tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2049646004

2049646007

2049646000

**On this page**

43

2049646006

You can use $profiling to access meta-model information.

****

###### **$profiling.getGeneralizationName(modelName)**

Return a generalization model of modelName.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | modelName | java.lang.String | A meta-model name. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection<String> | A list of generalization model names. |

****

###### **$profiling.getDeclaringElementName (modelName, propertyName)**

Retrieve the meta-model name which is the declared property name.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | modelName | java.lang.String | A meta-model name. |
|  | propertyName | java.lang.String | A property name. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A meta-model name. |

****

###### **$profiling.getPropertyTypeName (modelName, propertyName)**

Retrieve property types from meta-model names.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | modelName | java.lang.String | A meta-model name. |
|  | propertyName | java.lang.String | A property name. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A property type name. |

****

###### **$profiling.getPropertyTypeName (element, propertyName)**

Retrieve property types from an element.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An element. |
|  | propertyName | java.lang.String | A property name. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A property type name. |

****

###### **$profiling.getElementProperties(modelName)**

Retrieve a collection of element property names from meta-model names.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | modelName | java.lang.String | A meta-model name. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection<String> | A collection of element property names. |

****

###### **$profiling.getElementProperties(element)**

Retrieve a collection of element property names from elements.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An element. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection<String> | A collection of element property names. |

****

###### **$profiling.getElementProperty(element, propertyName)**

Retrieve property values of specified elements and property names.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An element. |
|  | propertyName | java.lang.String | A property name. |
| Return | - | java.lang.Object | A property object. |

****

###### **$profiling. getHumanPropertyName(element, propertyName)**

Return text representing a property name.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | propertyName | java.lang.String | A property name. |
|  | element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An element. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | Text representing a property name. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990110 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01439: (2026x Refresh1) Project characteristics

- page_id: `303990110`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990110/2026x+Refresh1+Project+characteristics
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:15.696+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Use Case driven
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Technical and Environmental factors are associated with the weight of the project. You have to assign a value to each factor. The value assigned to a particular factor depends on the degree of influence a factor has. The relevance values range from 0 to 5, where 0 means no influence, 3 is the average level of influence, and 5 means a strong influence.

#### Technical Factors

The table below lists Technical Factors

| Factor | Technical Factors | Weight | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| T1 | Distributed system | 2.0 | The system distributed architecture or centralized architecture. |
| T2 | Response adjectives | 1.0 | The response time is one of the important criteria. |
| T3 | End-user efficiency | 1.0 | The end-user efficiency. |
| T4 | Complex processing | 1.0 | The business process is very complex. |
| T5 | Reusable code | 1.0 | The project maintains high reusability. The design is complex. |
| T6 | Easy to install | 0.5 | The project requires simple installation. |
| T7 | Easy to use | 0.5 | User-friendly is one of the important criteria. |
| T8 | Portable | 2.0 | The project requires cross-platform implementation. |
| T9 | Easy to change | 1.0 | The project is highly customizable in the future. The architectural design is complex. |
| T10 | Concurrent | 1.0 | The project has a large numbers of users working with locking support. The architecture increases the project complexity. |
| T11 | Security features | 1.0 | The project has heavy security. |
| T12 | Access for third parties | 1.0 | The project is dependent on the third party’s control. Studying and understanding the third party is required. |
| T13 | Special training required | 1.0 | The application is so complex for the user that training must be provided. |

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Use Case Driven Model Data E3.

##### Technical Factor Value

Technical Factor Value (TFactor) is obtained with the multiplication of the value of each Technical factor by its weight.

```text
TFactor = value x weight
```

##### Technical Complexity Factor

Technical Complexity Factor (TCF) is obtained with the addition of 0.6 to the sum of TFactor multiplied by 0.01.

```text
TCF = 0.6 + (0.01 * TFactor)
```

#### Environmental Factors

The Table below lists Environmental Factors

| Factor | Environmental Factors | Weight | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| E1 | Familiar with RUP | 1.5 | Staff in the project are familiar with domain and technical details of the project. |
| E2 | Application experience | 0.5 | The application experience level. |
| E3 | Object-oriented experience | 1.0 | Staff in the project have basic knowledge of the OOP concept. The project is implemented on the object oriented design. |
| E4 | Lead analyst capability | 0.5 | The analyst who is leading the project has enough domain knowledge. |
| E5 | Motivation | 1.0 | The project motivates staff to work including how the software industry is going on. |
| E6 | Stable requirements | 2.0 | The requirements are clear, stable, and unlikely to change in the future. |
| E7 | Part-time workers | -1.0 | Part-time staff are working on the project. |
| E8 | Difficult programming language | -1.0 | Complexity of a programming language. |

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Use Case Driven Model Data E3.

##### Environmental Factor Value

Environmental Factor Value (EFactor) is obtained with the multiplication of the value of each Environmental factor by its weight.

##### Environmental Factor

Environmental Factor (EF) is obtained with the addition of 1.4 to the sum of EFactor multiplied by -0.03.

```text
EF = 1.4 + (-0.03 * EFactor)
```

#### Project Estimation

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Variables of Use Case Project Estimation.

##### Adjusted Use Case Points

The Adjusted Use Case Point (UCP) is determined with the multiplication of Unadjusted Use Case Point (UUCP) by Technical Complexity Factor (TCF) and Environmental Factor (EF).

```text
UCP = UUCP * TCF * EF
```

##### Estimated Effort in Person Hours

The person hours multiplier or X hours is a ratio of the number of man hours (PHM) per Use Case Point based on past projects. If no historical data has been collected, industry experts suggest using a figure between 15 and 30. A typical value is 20.

```text
X hours = UCP * PHM
```

##### Estimated Effort in Scheduled Time

Divide X hours by the number of developers working on the project and working hours per day to determine Estimated Effort in Scheduled Time or Y days. This means that with nDev developers, it would take Y days to complete the project.

```text
Y days = X / nDev / hay
```

##### Estimated Effort in Working Days

Divide Y days by working days per month to get the estimated effort in working days or Z months. This means that with nDev developers, it would take Z month to complete the project.

```text
Z months = Y / d Month
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992804 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01440: (2026x Refresh1) Project disclaimer configuration

- page_id: `303992804`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992804/2026x+Refresh1+Project+disclaimer+configuration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:22.615+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create a project disclaimer configuration to display custom disclaimers upon project opening. The disclaimers can contain any information you want the project users to be aware of before opening the project. The project can only be opened once all of the specified disclaimers have been read.

#### NOTE: Disclaimers in used projects

Disclaimers in used projects

- If the disclaimer configuration is created in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using other projects in a project' space=''] , it must be located in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Sharing project data' space=''] of the used project for the disclaimer to be shown when opening the main project.
- If you are using multiple projects that contain disclaimers, the order in which they are displayed is determined by the order in which the projects were used (however, disclaimers created in the main project are shown first). The same order applies if you create multiple classes with the «disclaimerConfiguration» stereotype in the same project; the disclaimers of a class that was created first are also displayed first.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating project disclaimer configuration

To create a project disclaimer configuration

1. Create a Class element and name it (e.g. 'Project Disclaimer Configuration').
2. Right-click the Class element. In the shortcut menu, click Stereotype > select «disclaimerConfiguration» from the menu, and click Apply . [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating_project_disclaimer_configurations_1.png']
3. Right-click the Class element and open its Specification window.
4. In the Specification window, select the Tags property group.
5. Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select text and click the Create Value button. [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating_project_disclaimer_configurations_2.png']
6. In the Value box, type the message you want to have displayed in the disclaimer. [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating_project_disclaimer_configurations_3.png']
7. (Optional) To add more disclaimer messages, click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
8. Click **Close**.

##### Adding more messages to the project disclaimer configuration

To add more messages to the project disclaimer configuration

1. Right-click the project disclaimer configuration Class element and open its Specification window.
2. In the Specification window, select the Tags property group.
3. Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select text, and on the right side of the window, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='add_button.png'] button.
4. In the Value box, type the message you want to have displayed in the disclaimer.
5. Click Close .

##### Removing messages from the project disclaimer configuration

To remove messages from the project disclaimer configuration

1. Right-click the project disclaimer configuration Class element and open its Specification window.
2. In the Specification window, select the Tags property group.
3. Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select text .
4. On the right side of the window select the text value you want to remove, and click the [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_button.png'] button.
5. Click Close .

##### Editing messages in the project disclaimer configuration

To edit messages in the project disclaimer configuration

1. Right-click the project disclaimer configuration Class element and open its Specification window.
2. In the Specification window, select the Tags property group.
3. Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select text .
4. On the right side of the window select the text value you want to edit, and click the [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] button.
5. Click Close .

##### Reordering messages in the project disclaimer configuration

The order of the messages displayed in the disclaimer depends on the order specified for the text values in the configuration's **text**tag.

To reorder messages in the project disclaimer configuration

1. Right-click the project disclaimer configuration Class element and open its Specification window.
2. In the Specification window, select the Tags property group.
3. Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select text .
4. On the right side of the window select the text value you want to move, and do one of the following:
  1. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='move_up_button.png'] button to move the text value up.
  2. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='move_down_button.png'] button to move the text value down.
5. Click Close .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create a project disclaimer configuration to display custom disclaimers upon project opening. The disclaimers can contain any information you want the project users to be aware of before opening the project. The project can only be opened once all of the specified disclaimers have been read.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d4b99cb0-cac5-4f66-ae31-2d5a8739f549"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Disclaimers in used projects</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the disclaimer configuration is created in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using other projects in a project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[used local project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, it must be located in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Sharing project data" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[shared package]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the used project for the disclaimer to be shown when opening the main project.</li><li>If you are using multiple projects that contain disclaimers, the order in which they are displayed is determined by the order in which the projects were used (however, disclaimers created in the main project are shown first). The same order applies if you create multiple classes with the «disclaimerConfiguration» stereotype in the same project; the disclaimers of a class that was created first are also displayed first. </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="disclaimers_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project disclaimer configuration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h3>Creating project disclaimer configuration</h3><p>To create a project disclaimer configuration</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a <strong>Class </strong>element and name it (e.g. 'Project Disclaimer Configuration').</li><li>Right-click the Class element. In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Stereotype</strong> &gt; select «disclaimerConfiguration» from the menu, and click <strong>Apply</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating_project_disclaimer_configurations_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project disclaimer configuration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Right-click the Class element and open its Specification window.</li><li>In the Specification window, select the <strong>Tags </strong>property group.</li><li>Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select <strong>text</strong> and click the <strong>Create Value</strong> button. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating_project_disclaimer_configurations_2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project disclaimer configuration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the <strong>Value </strong>box, type the message you want to have displayed in the disclaimer.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating_project_disclaimer_configurations_3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project disclaimer configuration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">(Optional) To add more disclaimer messages, click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project disclaimer configuration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Close</strong>.</p></li></ol><h3>Adding more messages to the project disclaimer configuration</h3><p>To add more messages to the project disclaimer configuration</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the project disclaimer configuration Class element and open its Specification window.</li><li>In the Specification window, select the <strong>Tags </strong>property group.</li><li>Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select <strong>text,</strong> and on the right side of the window, click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project disclaimer configuration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. </li><li>In the <strong>Value </strong>box, type the message you want to have displayed in the disclaimer.</li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Removing messages from the project disclaimer configuration</h3><p>To remove messages from the project disclaimer configuration</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the project disclaimer configuration Class element and open its Specification window.</li><li>In the Specification window, select the <strong>Tags </strong>property group.</li><li>Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select <strong>text</strong>.</li><li>On the right side of the window select the text value you want to remove, and click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project disclaimer configuration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. </li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Editing messages in the project disclaimer configuration</h3><p>To edit messages in the project disclaimer configuration</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the project disclaimer configuration Class element and open its Specification window.</li><li>In the Specification window, select the <strong>Tags </strong>property group.</li><li>Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select <strong>text</strong>.</li><li>On the right side of the window select the text value you want to edit, and click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project disclaimer configuration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. </li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Reordering messages in the project disclaimer configuration</h3><p>The order of the messages displayed in the disclaimer depends on the order specified for the text values in the configuration's <strong>text </strong>tag.</p><p>To reorder messages in the project disclaimer configuration</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the project disclaimer configuration Class element and open its Specification window.</li><li>In the Specification window, select the <strong>Tags </strong>property group.</li><li>Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select <strong>text</strong>.</li><li>On the right side of the window select the text value you want to move, and do one of the following:<ol><li>Click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_up_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project disclaimer configuration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button to move the text value up. </li><li>Click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_down_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project disclaimer configuration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button to move the text value down. </li></ol></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998546 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01441: (2026x Refresh1) Project migration issues on Teamwork Cloud

- page_id: `303998546`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998546/2026x+Refresh1+Project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:24.718+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are some issues with the Teamwork Cloud projects migration to UAF 1.3 version when those projects contain used projects:

- Used projects are not migrated.
- Used projects are migrated, but not updated to the latest version on Teamwork Cloud.

##### Issues of manual projects migration

Before the migration, you need to make sure you have the necessary permissions. [Learn more about roles and permissions >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud#ProjectmigrationissuesonTeamworkCloud-Rolesandpermissions)

###### Used projects are not migrated

If you are trying to migrate a project containing not migrated used projects, you will get a notification informing you which used projects are not migrated. To proceed with the migration of the main project, you need to migrate the used projects mentioned in the notification message first.

###### Used projects are migrated, but not updated

If you are trying to migrate a project containing used projects not updated to the latest version, you will get a notification informing you which used projects are not updated. In this case, you can choose from the following options:

- Update and Migrate. Used projects will be updated to the latest version, and the project will be migrated. This option is strongly recommended.
- Migrate. The main project will be migrated without updating the used projects to the latest version. In this case, the used projects will remain outdated, and you will not be able to work with them.
- Open as Read-Only. The migration is canceled, and the project opens as read-only. In this case, the project will not be migrated.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

For more information about manual project migration, see [Migrating server projects manually](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/243969919/Migrating+projects#Migratingprojects-Migratingserverprojectsmanually).

##### Issues of automatic project migration

Before the migration, you need to make sure you have the necessary permissions. [Learn more about roles and permissions >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud#ProjectmigrationissuesonTeamworkCloud-Rolesandpermissions)

If the main project contains used projects, you need to migrate the used projects first. Then you can perform the automatic migration of the main project. After the migration is complete, you need to switch your used project versions to the latest (if the latest versions were used before migration).

When you migrate projects automatically and receive a message that some projects were not migrated, it might be that those projects are using other projects. In this case, do one of the following:

- Migrate the listed projects manually.
- Repeat the automatic migration.

For more information about automatic project migration, see [Migrating server projects automatically](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/243969919/Migrating+projects#Migratingprojects-Migratingserverprojectsautomatically).

##### Roles and permissions

For the migration of server projects, certain roles and permissions are needed. Before migrating, make sure you have **Resource Locks Administrator** and **Resource Manager roles.**Also, you need to have **Administer Resources** and **Release Resource Locks** permissions.

To learn more, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='MCS'].

For more information about migrating server projects, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Migrating Server Projects' space=''].

##### Special migration options

There are special migration options for the server projects:

- The UPDM project migration from the modeling tool with the UAF environment will not be performed. However, if you want to migrate the UPDM project to UAF, you need to open the magicdraw.properties (or cea.properties ) file and change the value of the system property -Dmigrate.project.from.updm2.to.uaf\=false to True . By default, the value of this property is False , which means, that your UPDM projects will not be migrated.

[Learn more>>](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/243969919/Migrating+projects#Migratingprojects-Migratingserverprojectsautomatically)

- You can suspend the used projects updating by adding **-Dsuspend.modules.update.during.migration\=false** in the properties file and set it to *true*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

In this case, you can choose from the following options:</p><ul><li data-uuid="80dcacc4-4052-48d7-b8a0-9cefbaff9f61"><strong>Update and Migrate.</strong> Used projects will be updated to the latest version, and the project will be migrated. This option is <strong>strongly recommended.</strong></li><li data-uuid="cee214d7-2fe2-4f6e-b41b-ec0a9fcff8eb"><strong>Migrate.</strong> The main project will be migrated without updating the used projects to the latest version. In this case, the used projects will remain outdated, and you will not be able to work with them.</li><li data-uuid="736fd451-a626-4fd9-80bb-63c2c700500f"><strong>Open as Read-Only.</strong> The migration is canceled, and the project opens as read-only. In this case, the project will not be migrated.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="pic2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project migration issues on Teamwork Cloud" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c23af08a-a384-4796-96e7-915a630379f1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about manual project migration, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/243969919/Migrating+projects#Migratingprojects-Migratingserverprojectsmanually">Migrating server projects manually</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Issues of automatic project migration</h3><p>Before the migration, you need to make sure you have the necessary permissions. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud#ProjectmigrationissuesonTeamworkCloud-Rolesandpermissions">Learn more about roles and permissions &gt;&gt;</a></p><p>If the main project contains used projects, you need to migrate the used projects first. In this case, do one of the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="55b036aa-09b9-44ad-8549-49f84383f27c">Migrate the listed projects manually.</li><li data-uuid="df98917f-74fc-423e-a086-df2dbe8d9722">Repeat the automatic migration. </li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0999d47e-f1b2-4015-a180-a5101015706a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about automatic project migration, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/243969919/Migrating+projects#Migratingprojects-Migratingserverprojectsautomatically">Migrating server projects automatically</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Roles and permissions </h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">For the migration of server projects, certain roles and permissions are needed. Before migrating, make sure you have <strong>Resource Locks Administrator</strong> and <strong>Resource Manager roles. </strong>Also, you need to have <strong>Administer Resources</strong> and <strong>Release Resource Locks</strong> permissions.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To learn more, see<span> </span></span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Permissions" /></ac:link>.</p><p>For more information about migrating server projects, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Migrating Server Projects" /></ac:link>.</p><h3>Special migration options</h3><p>There are special migration options for the server projects:</p><ul><li data-uuid="3682e446-fa39-4a3e-97dd-459d79f4e979">The UPDM project migration from the modeling tool with the UAF environment will not be performed. However, if you want to migrate the UPDM project to UAF, you need to open the<span> </span><strong>magicdraw.properties</strong><span> </span>(or<span> </span><strong>cea.properties</strong>) file and change the value of the system property<span> </span><strong>-Dmigrate.project.from.updm2.to.uaf\=false</strong><span> </span>to<span> </span><em>True</em>. By default, the value of this property is<span> </span><em>False</em>, which means, that your UPDM projects will not be migrated.</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 80.0px;"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/243969919/Migrating+projects#Migratingprojects-Migratingserverprojectsautomatically">Learn more&gt;&gt;</a></p><ul><li data-uuid="e36f7f2f-6ff0-4a86-9e44-e9970f49f745">You can suspend the used projects updating by adding <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>-Dsuspend.modules.update.during.migration\=false</strong> in the properties file and set it to <em>true</em>.</span> </li></ul><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992111 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01442: (2026x Refresh1) Project partitioning

- page_id: `303992111`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992111/2026x+Refresh1+Project+partitioning
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:34.070+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

In life, all large things are built from smaller parts. This also applies to large projects. For large models with several weakly dependent parts, it is advisable to split them into several separate project files (or project resources in a collaborative environment), also called used projects.

Project partitioning opens up additional possibilities, such as:

- Reusing the same model part (e.g. library) in several projects.
- Separate administration of different project parts. You can define different access rights in the collaborative environment for each used project. Separate developers or teams of developers can be assigned for each resource, clearly establishing responsibility boundaries and preventing inadvertent modifications when these projects are used in the read-only mode.
- Separate versioning. Each used project can have its own version history with its own tagging and branching, life cycle of development, feature-freezes, and stable releases.
- Increased performance on very large projects. Whenever used projects are marked as loaded manually, a user can selectively load only the necessary parts of the project. He/she can also open the isolated used project separately.

A model can be decomposed into separate projects. A used project is a part that can be separated from the main project and can be used for a specific purpose.

The modeling tool supports the following used project accessibility modes:

- Read-only
- Read-write

The decision to use a project as read-only or read-write depends on the maturity of a project and the organization ownership or responsibility rules for projects using other projects. If the library in the used project is complete (changes to it are not expected / likely / possible) it should be used in read-only mode. If a project is developed by a separate team and this team is responsible for this project and the project is reused in another project, it is recommended to use this project as read-only. This prevents inadvertent changes in the project by another team.

If a project is actively developed and evolves together with the projects that are using it, this project can be used in read-write accessibility mode. In this case you must be careful and remember that your changes to the used project will be reflected in other projects. Using Teamwork Cloud might be advisable in this case. And, of course, there can be mixed usage situations - when a project is used as read-only in some projects and as read-write in others.

Instructions on how to partition your project can be found in the following pages:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
