This also applies to large projects. For large models with several weakly dependent parts, it is advisable to split them into several separate project files (or project resources in a collaborative environment), also called used projects.</p><p>Project partitioning opens up additional possibilities, such as:</p><ul><li><span>Reusing the same model part (e.g. library) in several projects.</span></li><li>Separate administration of different project parts. You can define different access rights in the collaborative environment for each used project. Separate developers or teams of developers can be assigned for each resource, clearly establishing responsibility boundaries and preventing inadvertent modifications when these projects are used in the read-only mode.</li><li>Separate versioning. Each used project can have its own version history with its own tagging and branching, life cycle of development, feature-freezes, and stable releases.</li><li>Increased performance on very large projects. Whenever used projects are marked as loaded manually, a user can selectively load only the necessary parts of the project. He/she can also open the isolated used project separately.</li></ul><p>A model can be decomposed into separate projects. A used project is a part that can be separated from the main project and can be used for a specific purpose.</p><p>The modeling tool supports the following used project accessibility modes:</p><ul><li>Read-only</li><li>Read-write</li></ul><p>The decision to use a project as read-only or read-write depends on the maturity of a project and the organization ownership or responsibility rules for projects using other projects. If the library in the used project is complete (changes to it are not expected / likely / possible) it should be used in read-only mode. If a project is developed by a separate team and this team is responsible for this project and the project is reused in another project, it is recommended to use this project as read-only. This prevents inadvertent changes in the project by another team.</p><p>If a project is actively developed and evolves together with the projects that are using it, this project can be used in read-write accessibility mode. In this case you must be careful and remember that your changes to the used project will be reflected in other projects. Using Teamwork Cloud might be advisable in this case. And, of course, there can be mixed usage situations - when a project is used as read-only in some projects and as read-write in others.</p><p>Instructions on how to partition your project can be found in the following pages:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="83a571da-de4a-4433-a85f-360eea3a5425" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991875 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01443: (2026x Refresh1) Project Properties dialog

- page_id: `303991875`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991875/2026x+Refresh1+Project+Properties+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:18.556+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Creating projects
- labels: ['forward-compatibility', 'compatibility-range', 'count-elements', 'project-statistics', 'detailed-statistics', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1428371638

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1428371640

1228009252

**On this page**

**53**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1428371639

#### EXCERPT: Note

BLOCK

The **Project** **Properties** dialog provides general information about a project. You can add a title, author, version, and project description using this dialog. 
 
 To open the **Project Properties**dialog

1. Open the project.
2. Select the Project Properties command from the File men u.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Project properties dialog.

#### TIP: Title

Title

The project title is assigned as a value of the project [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Documentation tab' space=''].

##### Compatibility range

To specify compatibility range

1. Enter the Version value.
2. Enter the Compatible with. The value must be less or equal to the Version value.
3. Click OK after you are done.

#### NOTE: Compatible With

Compatible With

**Compatible With** defines the compatibility range between two Standard/System profile versions. Once specified, it helps to avoid read-only constraints in TWCloud projects when you use earlier profile versions locally rather than those used in the server project.

When the **Version** of the Standard/System profile on your computer is earlier (e.g. 19.0 SP1) than the **Version** of this profile on TWCloud (e.g. 19.0 SP3), but higher or equal to the value of **Compatible with** (e.g. 19.0), you will still be able to edit TWCloud projects using the profile.

##### Advanced options

To use advanced options

1. Click More to see more options:

- 
  - See the list of shared packages in the Shared Packages tab. In this tab you can:
    - Define a preferred path to the particular package. Click the ... button [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Selecting elements' space=''] in the Preferred Path value box.
    - Set the project as a standard/system profile by selecting the **Standard/System Profile** check box. NoteThe **Shared Packages** tab is available for local projects only.
  - The Used Projects tab shows the projects and standard/system profiles used in the open project. Click the Open Project Usages button to open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing used projects' space='']**Used Projects** dialog , where you can modify project usages.
  - The Advanced tab shows project meta information. In this tab you can:
    - Reset all project element IDs. All project elements have their own ID (a unique identifier). While working with several related projects, you may receive a message about duplicated element IDs. After clicking the **Reset IDs** button, new IDs will be created for all project elements. NoteMake sure the project you are resetting IDs for is not used in other projects.
    - Reset the project ID. After clicking the **Reset Project ID** button, new IDs are created for the project. You cannot reset the project ID for server projects.

1428371636

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating projects' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with multiple projects' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47f3a9fb-76d0-4df4-ba90-ffb469dfdc52"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1428371638</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06e0fbfc-0fe4-4bae-9319-beed337254ef"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1428371640</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5c4709a-a213-421d-bf8c-839ba97dd05b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1228009252</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><strong> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5997e7f-8f3b-42d1-acd7-31d5e265d218"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">5</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro> </strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1110eea-6107-4918-87cd-9f4dce55be97"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1428371639</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5c95507-df4e-44cb-9a7c-031a776cae06"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">BLOCK</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Project</strong> <strong>Properties</strong> dialog provides general information about a project. You can add a title, author, version, and project description using this dialog. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To open the <strong>Project Properties </strong>dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the project.</li><li>Select the <strong>Project Properties</strong> command from the <strong>File</strong> men<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="327af7ca-4d32-4e48-8097-5e5ee7ec11fb">u.</ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="327af7ca-4d32-4e48-8097-5e5ee7ec11fb"><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ol><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="327af7ca-4d32-4e48-8097-5e5ee7ec11fb"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_properties_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project Properties dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><h6 class="auto-cursor-target" style="text-align: center;">Project properties dialog.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a5d687c-07c4-49f6-bbcb-c4b4a3184ad0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Title</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The project title is assigned as a value of the project <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Documentation tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Documentation property]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3>Compatibility range</h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="10de0c1b-c6f1-4ede-bafc-077e34cf8c27">To</ac:inline-comment-marker> specify compatibility range</p><hr /><ol><li>Enter the<strong> Version </strong>value.</li><li>Enter the<strong> Compatible with. </strong>The value must be less or equal to the<strong> Version </strong>value.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> after you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68ebf696-3c61-4c7e-8905-90da56ae8061"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Compatible With</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="72e1bf56-832d-4ba2-86d7-0a16b65b9f6f"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Compatible With</strong> defines the compatibility range between two Standard/System profile versions. Once specified, it helps to avoid read-only constraints in TWCloud projects when you use earlier profile versions locally rather than those used in the server project.</span></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p>When the <strong>Version</strong> of the Standard/System profile on your computer is earlier (e.g. 19.0 SP1) than the <strong>Version</strong> of this profile on TWCloud (e.g. 19.0 SP3), but higher or equal to the value of <strong>Compatible with</strong> (e.g. 19.0), you will still be able to edit TWCloud projects using the profile.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Advanced options</h3><p>To use <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c742fd9f-2d6a-42d7-841e-614c7f4ceaad">advanced</ac:inline-comment-marker> options</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>More</strong> to see more options:</li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>See the list of shared packages in the <strong>Shared Packages</strong> tab. In this tab you can:<ul><li>Define a preferred path to the particular package. Click the <strong>...</strong> button <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Selecting elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[to select the package]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in the <strong>Preferred Path </strong>value box.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Set the project as a standard/system profile by selecting the <strong>Standard/System Profile</strong> check box.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d006b130-6fed-4c33-aa2d-fc74ac9b0ee2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Shared Packages</strong> tab is available for local projects only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li>The <strong>Used Projects</strong> tab shows the projects and standard/system profiles used in the open project. Click the <strong>Open Project Usages</strong> button to open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing used projects" /><ac:link-body><strong>Used Projects</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, where you can modify project usages.</li><li>The <strong>Advanced</strong> tab shows project meta information. In this tab you can:<ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Reset all project element IDs. All project elements have their own ID (a unique identifier). While working with several related projects, you may receive a message about duplicated element IDs. After clicking the <strong>Reset IDs</strong> button, new IDs will be created for all project elements.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b4ce30d-b3e2-467c-a370-04c80e217d89"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Make sure the project you are resetting IDs for is not used in other projects.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Reset the project ID. After clicking the <strong>Reset Project ID</strong> button, new IDs are created for the project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b0e2d4c-8df9-4308-bf92-d042fedf055c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You cannot reset the project ID for server projects.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33b51e8b-7d63-4832-bc54-a253ca8e7211"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1428371636</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating projects" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with multiple projects" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995464 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01444: (2026x Refresh1) Project Statistics

- page_id: `303995464`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995464/2026x+Refresh1+Project+Statistics
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:36.483+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Detailed project statistics

The project statistics feature calculates detailed statistics for the entire project and exports the results to a separate file. The exported statistical report will help us solve performance problems you may be experiencing. Project statistics are calculated in the main project and, if selected, in used projects.

#### NOTE: Confidential Information

Confidential Information

The exported file contains the names of the main project, used projects (if selected), and used elements. If they are confidential, you should select **Confidential**to keep their names hidden.

To export detailed statistics

1. In the main menu, select Tools > Project Statistics . [ATTACHMENT filename='project_statistics_tools.png']
2. In the Project Statistics dialog, click the Export Detailed Statistics button. [ATTACHMENT filename='project_statistics_dialog.png']
3. Select data to export along with the OS and hardware, diagrams/elements by types, and project options statistics that are included by default. [ATTACHMENT filename='export_detailed_statistics_options.png']
4. Specify other export options:
  - Select Include Used Project Statistics to generate and export used project statistics as well.
  - Select Confidential to keep the project element names hidden in the exported statistics report.
5. Click Export .
6. When the statistics file is generated, click Close .

The generated detailed project statistics file includes the following quantitative data:

**Diagrams**

- Diagram dimensions
- Diagram count by type
- Diagram count by kind
- Symbol count by diagram

**Elements**

- Element count by UML type
- Element count by DSL type
- Derived properties count
- Property count by size
- Elements used for CDH mapping count
- Elements used for transformation mapping count
- Longest flat list count (longest flat list of elements under one element's root, such as under a package or a classifier)
- Smart package count
- Number of stereotype applications by stereotype name
- Number of stereotype tagged values by defining feature name and tagged value size
- Number of strings in the model by size and metaProperty
- Number of multi-references by size and metaProperty
- Number of profiling elements (stereotype instance, slots, and value specifications)

**Project/Environment options**

- Active/passive validation options
- Number of active validation rules by implementation language
- Number of code engineering options by language
- Symbol styles count
- Symbol styles properties count

**Hardware & system**

- Hardware (RAM, OS, disks size and type) and Java info (heap)
- Restricted dir size

**Teamwork Cloud projects**

- Teamwork Cloud project local cache size on disk
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache size in a binary blob
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache latest blob size
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob size by revision
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob count by revision
- Teamwork Cloud project history depth
- Locked elements count
- Used projects count
- Root object count by type

**Performance**

- Time to build diagrams

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The exported statistical report will help us solve performance problems you may be experiencing. If they are confidential, you should select <strong>Confidential </strong>to keep their names hidden.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To export detailed statistics</p><hr button. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_statistics_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project Statistics" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Select data to <strong>Include Used Project Statistics</strong> to generate and export used project statistics as well.</li><li>Select <strong>Confidential </strong>to keep the project element names hidden in the exported statistics report. </li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Export</strong>.</li><li>When the statistics file is generated, click <strong>Close</strong>.     </li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The generated detailed project statistics file includes the following quantitative data:</p><p><strong>Diagrams</strong></p><ul><li>Diagram dimensions</li><li>Diagram count by type </li><li>Diagram count by count by size</li><li>Elements used for CDH mapping count</li><li>Elements used for transformation mapping count</li><li>Longest flat list count (longest flat list of elements under one element's name and tagged value size</li><li>Number of strings in the model by size and metaProperty</li><li>Number of multi-references by size and metaProperty</li><li>Number of profiling elements (stereotype instance, slots, and value specifications)</li></ul><p><strong>Project/Environment options</strong></p><ul><li>Active/passive validation options</li><li>Number of active validation rules by size</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob size by revision</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob count by revision</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project history depth</li><li>Locked elements count</li><li>Used projects count</li><li>Root object count by type</li></ul><p><strong>Performance</strong></p><ul><li>Time to build diagrams</li></ul>

## PAGE 01445: (2026x Refresh1) Project Status Application Wizard

- page_id: `304000068`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000068/2026x+Refresh1+Project+Status+Application+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:44.290+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Dialogs
- labels: ['project-status-application-wizard', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1011370957

1011370959

1011370958

The **Project Status Application Wizard** is used to apply the DLOD Milestone, DOTMLPF Milestone, or custom Milestone type to the selected element, which can be one of the following milestone kinds:

- In Service
- Deployed
- No Longer Used
- Out Of Service
- Other

To open the **Project Status Application Wizard**

1. On the diagram pane, right-click the Milestone and select Edit Status . The Project Status Application Wizard opens.

The wizard consists of two steps:

truenone

##### Actual Project Milestone Type selection

In this step, select DLOD Milestone, DOTMLPF Milestone, or create your own Milestone type.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Project Status at a particular Milestone specification

In this step, specify project threads and status indicators of the selected Actual Project Milestone Type.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

|  | Project threads | Status indicators |
| --- | --- | --- |
| DLOD Milestone type | EquipmentLogisticsInfrastructureOrganizationDoctrine/ConceptsInformationPersonnelTraining | DLOD AbsentNot RequiredNo Outstanding IssuesManageable IssuesCritical Issues |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

|  | Project threads | Status indicators |
| --- | --- | --- |
| DOTMLPF Milestone type | DoctrineOrganizationTrainingMaterielLeadershipPersonnelFacilities | Materiel Solution AnalysisTechnology DevelopmentEngineering and Manufacturing DevelopmentProduction & DeploymentOperations & Support |

If you selected your own milestone type, you can create your own projects threads and status indicators.

To create project threads and status indicators

1. In the Project Thread area, click the Add button.
2. Specify the thread name.
3. In the Status Indicator area, click the Add button.
4. Specify the indicator name.
5. Click the Fill Color button.
6. In the Color dialog choose the color and click OK .

To create a second thread with the same status indicators, select a previously created thread and click the **Clone**button. A new thread with previously defined status indicators is created.

To remove the applied Project Status

- In the right bottom corner of the Preview area, click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_project_status_indicating_mark.png'] .

1011370955

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Edit Project Status in Lr diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing PV-2 diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Removing Project Status from Lr diagram' space='']

## PAGE 01446: (2026x Refresh1) Project tool

- page_id: `303998166`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998166/2026x+Refresh1+Project+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:05.707+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Helper tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Sample code

1266893033

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Sample code

1266893035

1266893031

**On this page**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Sample code

1266893035

$project is a project reference enabling a template to return the project information.

##### **$project.getName()**

Returns a project name.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A project name. |

##### **$project.getTitle()**

Gets a project title.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A project title. |

##### **$project.getFileName()**

Gets a project filename.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A project filename. |

##### **$project.getExtension()**

Gets a project filename extension.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

This function is only for local projects.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A project filename extension. |

##### **$project.getDirectory()**

Note

$project.getDiagrams() returns all the diagrams in the selected scope, including diagrams in auxiliary resources.

#### Project Version

The full class name is com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectVersion. 
ProjectVersion is a class that represents some version of the project or module. It contains date, user, number, etc.

ProjectVersion contains the following properties:

| Property | Return type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| comment | java.lang.String | Returns a version committed comment. |
| date | java.util.Date | Returns a committed date. |
| dateAsString | java.lang.String | Returns a committed date as text. |
| number | long | Returns a committed version. |
| numberAsString | java.lang.String | Returns a committed version as text. |
| user | java.lang.String | Returns a committer’s name. |
| version | java.lang.Object | Returns a version object com.nomagic.magicdraw.esi.project.project.IEsiVersionDescriptor for a Teamwork Cloud project. |
| tags | java.util.List<java.lang.String> | Returns all tags. |

```text

```

#### Project Module

The full class name is com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectModule. 
ProjectModule is a wrapper Class of an attached project (com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IAttachedProject) that represents a special kind of the project that is used as a component in the current project.

The following are some properties and methods of ProjectModule:

| Property / Method | Return type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| representationString | java.lang.String | Returns user-friendly representation string about the module. |
| description | java.lang.String | Returns project description of the module. |
| version | long | Returns a version number of the module. |
| remoteID | java.lang.String | Returns a remote ID for the module. |
| URI | java.net.URI | Converts EMF URI to java.net.URI. |
| versionList | java.util.List<> | Returns a list of version descriptors of the module. |
| isRemote() | boolean | Checks if the module is remote - from Teamwork Cloud. |

```text

```

/><col style="width: 16.483%;" /><col style="width: 63.468%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br this value is true (false by default).</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of <ac:link ac:anchor="ProjectModule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectModule]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$project.getLocalModule()</strong></h3><p>Returns a list of used local modules.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 80.0024%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 5.90895%;" /><col style="width: 5.44465%;" /><col style="width: 18.5935%;" /><col style="width: 70.0923%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of <ac:link ac:anchor="ProjectModule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectModule]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$project.getLocalModule(includeAuxiliary)</strong></h3><p>Returns a list of used local modules as well as auxiliary resources.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 91.46%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 8.33862%;" /><col style="width: 9.36508%;" /><col style="width: 13.9643%;" /><col style="width: 68.3664%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></td><td>includeAuxiliary</td><td>boolean</td><td>Returns local modules including auxiliary resources if this value is true (false by default).</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of <ac:link ac:anchor="ProjectModule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectModule]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$project.getAuxiliaryResource()</strong></h3><p>Returns a list of auxiliary resources.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 79.8574%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 5.9197%;" /><col style="width: 5.45455%;" /><col style="width: 18.8985%;" /><col style="width: 69.7667%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of <ac:link ac:anchor="ProjectModule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectModule]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><h2>Project Version</h2><p>The full class name is <span>com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectVersion</span>.<br />ProjectVersion is a class that represents some version of the project or module. It contains date, user, number, etc.</p><p><br />ProjectVersion contains the following properties:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Property</th><th>Return type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>comment</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Returns a version committed comment.</td></tr><tr><td>date</td><td>java.util.Date</td><td>Returns a committed date.</td></tr><tr><td>dateAsString</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Returns a committed date as text.</td></tr><tr><td>number</td><td>long</td><td>Returns a committed version.</td></tr><tr><td>numberAsString</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Returns a committed version as text.</td></tr><tr><td>user</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Returns a committer’s name.</td></tr><tr><td>version</td><td>java.lang.Object</td><td>Returns a version object com.nomagic.magicdraw.esi.project.project.IEsiVersionDescriptor for a Teamwork Cloud project.</td></tr><tr><td>tags</td><td>java.util.List&lt;java.lang.String&gt;</td><td>Returns all tags.</td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="472aa08a-04cb-4ed8-92f4-c062256dd26d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample code</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Current version : $project.version
All version:
#foreach ($version in $project.getVersionList())
	Date : 				$version.date
	Number : 			$version.number
	Number As String : 	$version.numberAsString
	User : 				$version.user
	Comment : 			$version.comment
	Tags:
#foreach($tag in $version.tags) 
		- tag
#end
---
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Project Module</h2><p>The full class name is <span>com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectModule</span>.<br />ProjectModule is a wrapper Class of an attached project (com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IAttachedProject) that represents a special kind of the project that is used as a component in the current project.</p><p><br />The following are some properties and methods of ProjectModule:</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 99.9444%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 10.6754%;" /><col style="width: 20.6899%;" /><col style="width: 68.6662%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Property / Method</th><th>Return type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>representationString</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Returns user-friendly representation string about the module.</td></tr><tr><td>description</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Returns project description of the module.</td></tr><tr><td>version</td><td>long</td><td>Returns a version number of the module.</td></tr><tr><td>remoteID</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Returns a remote ID for the module.</td></tr><tr><td>URI</td><td>java.net.URI</td><td>Converts EMF URI to java.net.URI.</td></tr><tr><td>versionList</td><td>java.util.List&lt;<ac:link ac:anchor="ProjectVersion" />&gt;</td><td>Returns a list of version descriptors of the module.</td></tr><tr><td>isRemote()</td><td>boolean</td><td>Checks if the module is remote - from Teamwork Cloud.</td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0c8dad29-d010-40c1-b029-6a38467dc0fe"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample code</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($module in $project.getModuleList())
	Name : $module.representationString
	Description : $module.description
	Version : $module.version
	RemoteID : $module.remoteID
	URI : $module.URI 
#if($module.isRemote())
#set($version = $module.projectVersion)
	Current module version :
		date : $version.date
		number : $version.number 
		numberAsString : $version.numberAsString
		user : $version.user
		comment : $version.comment
		tags: 
#foreach($tag in $version.tags)
- $tag
#end
	All module versions :
#foreach($version in $module.versionList)
		date : $version.date
		number : $version.number
		numberAsString : $version.numberAsString
		user : $version.usercomment : $version.comment 
		tags:
#foreach($tag in $version.tags)
- $tag
#end
----------
#end
#end 
	Shared module:
#foreach ($child in $project.getSharedModule($module))
		- $child.representationString
#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305730323

305730325

305730324

“The DoDAF-described Models within the Project Viewpoint describe how programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives deliver capabilities, the organizations contributing to them, and dependencies between them. Previous versions of DoDAF took a traditional model of architecture in which descriptions of programs and projects were considered outside scope. To compensate for this, various DoDAF models represented the evolution of systems, technologies and standards (e.g., Systems and Services Evolution Description, Systems Technology Forecast, and Technical Standards Forecast).” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

305730322

**Views**

Previous versions of DoDAF took a traditional model of architecture in which descriptions of programs and projects were considered outside scope. To compensate for this, various DoDAF models represented the evolution of systems, technologies and standards (e.g., Systems and Services Evolution Description, Systems Technology Forecast, and Technical Standards Forecast).” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df353d91-8ee4-4977-b371-d1abf38a2edd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305730322</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="42db5711-c6c6-4926-b4c7-727698cdb705" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 01449: (2026x Refresh1) Projects Connectivity

- page_id: `303993494`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993494/2026x+Refresh1+Projects+Connectivity

- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Projects
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

912913740

912913742

912913741

**Description**

The Projects Connectivity (Pj-Cn) domain shows the relationships between projects and project milestones.

The Projects Connectivity (Pj-Cn) domain enables the user to model the organizational structures needed to manage programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. It shows dependency relationships between the actual organizations that own the programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. This model could be used to represent organizational relationships associated with transformation initiatives along with those who are responsible for managing programs, projects, and portfolios. The Projects Connectivity (Pj-Cn) domain provides a means of analyzing the main dependencies between acquisition elements or transformation elements.

The intended usage of the Projects Connectivity (Pj-Cn) domain includes, but is not limited to:

- Program management (specified acquisition program structure).
- Project organization.
- Cross-cutting initiatives to be tracked across portfolios.

**Implementation**

The Projects Connectivity (Pj-Cn) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Projects Connectivity diagram' space=''].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Projects Connectivity diagram

912913739

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Milestone' space='MED']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Milestone' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Milestone Role' space='MED']

1159352855

**Description**

The Projects Parameters (Pr-Pm) domain depicts the measurements of resources.

The intended usage of the Projects Parameters (Pr-Pm) domain includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measurements.
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

The Projects Parameters (Pr-Pm) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Typical Measurements table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Actual Measurements table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='']
- Actual Measurements (.xlsx)

1159352834

- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Projects
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

913359236

913359239

913359238

**Description**

The Projects Roadmap (Pj-Rm) domain shows the product portfolio management; planning of capability delivery. It provides a timeline perspective on programs or projects. This domain is not limited to the acquisition and fielding processes.

The intended usage of the Projects Roadmap (Pj-Rm) domain includes:

- Project management and control (including delivery timescales).
- Project dependency risk identification.
- Management of dependencies.
- Portfolio management.

**Implementation**

The Projects Roadmap (Pj-Rm) domain is represented by a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Projects Roadmap diagram' space=''] which is realized as a Gantt Chart.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Projects Roadmap diagram

913359234

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993529 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01453: (2026x Refresh1) Projects Structure

- page_id: `303993529`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993529/2026x+Refresh1+Projects+Structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:14.957+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Projects
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

913540941

913540943

913540942

**Description**

The Projects Structure (Pj-Sr) domain shows relationships between types of projects and project milestones. It provides a template for an actual project(s) road map(s) to be implemented. The Projects Structure (Pj-Sr) domain represents an organizational perspective on programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. The Projects Structure (Pj-Sr) domain enables the user to model the organizational structures needed to manage programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. It shows dependency relationships between the actual organizations that own the programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. This model could be used to represent organizational relationships associated with transformation initiatives along with those who are responsible for managing programs, projects, and portfolios. The Projects Structure (Pj-Sr) domain provides a means of analyzing the main dependencies between acquisition elements or transformation elements.

The intended usage of the Projects Structure (Pj-Sr) domain includes, but is not limited to:

- Program management (specified acquisition program structure).
- Project organization.
- Cross-cutting initiatives to be tracked across portfolios.

**Implementation**

The Projects Structure (Pj-Sr) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Projects Structure diagram' space=''].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Projects Structure diagram

913540940

913814665

913814664

**Description**

The Projects Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain shows the taxonomy of types of projects and project milestones. It represents an organizational perspective on programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. The Projects Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain enables the user to model the organizational structures needed to manage programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. This model could be used to represent organizational relationships associated with transformation initiatives along with those who are responsible for managing programs, projects, and portfolios. The Projects Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain provides a means of analyzing the main dependencies between acquisition elements or transformation elements.

The intended usage of the The Projects Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain includes, but is not limited to:

- Program management (specified acquisition program structure).
- Project organization.
- Cross-cutting initiatives to be tracked across portfolios.

**Implementation**

The Projects Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Projects Taxonomy diagram' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Projects Taxonomy table' space=''] . This table can be used to create and describe major domain elements faster.

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb0d809e-1fb4-4a85-b989-00a18719f7fd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">913814663</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dfc0bb88-122b-4732-abd6-b262f3053890"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">913814665</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3e5f516b-88cc-435b-8393-17619b08e982"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">913814664</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Projects Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain shows the taxonomy of types of projects and project milestones. It represents an organizational perspective on programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. The Projects Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain enables the user to model the organizational structures needed to manage programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. This model could be used to represent organizational relationships associated with transformation initiatives along with those who are responsible for managing programs, projects, and portfolios. The Projects Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain provides a means of analyzing the main dependencies between acquisition elements or transformation elements.</p><p>The intended usage of the The Projects Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain includes, but is not limited to:</p><ul><li>Program management (specified acquisition program structure).</li><li>Project organization.</li><li>Cross-cutting initiatives to be tracked across portfolios.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Projects Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain is represented by:</p><ul><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Projects Taxonomy diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Projects Taxonomy diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Projects Taxonomy table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Projects Taxonomy table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. This table can be used to create and describe major domain elements faster.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Project Taxonomy" ac:alt="Project Taxonomy"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_taxonomy.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Projects Taxonomy" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Projects Taxonomy diagram</h6><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="projects_taxonomy_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Projects Taxonomy" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Projects Taxonomy Table</h6><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="156d79af-eefe-46fd-adf9-f200a1ac1191"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">913814662</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Project" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Project Milestone" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Project Milestone Role" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Performer" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="d48703ae-356c-4bbe-a2f4-ae0d4511c558" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997175 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01455: (2026x Refresh1) Projects Traceability

- page_id: `303997175`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997175/2026x+Refresh1+Projects+Traceability
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:54.936+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Projects
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

913969804

913969806

913969805

**Description**

The Projects Traceability (Pj-Tr) domain shows the traceability between capabilities and projects that deliver them.The Projects Traceability (Pj-Tr) domain supports the acquisition and deployment processes, including the management of dependencies between projects and the integration of all relevant project and program elements to achieve a capability.

Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives are mapped to the capability for a particular timeframe. Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives may contribute to multiple capabilities and may mature across time. The analysis can be used to identify capability redundancies and shortfalls, highlight phasing issues, expose organizational or system interoperability problems, and support program decisions, such as when to phase out a legacy system.

The intended usage of the Projects Traceability (Pj-Tr) domain includes:

- Tracing capability requirements to projects.
- Capability audit.

**Implementation**

The Projects Traceability (Pj-Tr) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Actual Projects to Capabilities Mapping Matrix' space=''] which is a non-editable Dependency Matrix. Capabilities are used as row elements and Actual Projects are used as column elements.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Actual Responsible Resources to Actual Projects Mapping Matrix' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Project Activities to Capabilities Mapping matrix' space=''] .

**Samples**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Actual Projects to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Actual Organizational Resources to Actual Projects Mapping Matrix

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of theProject Activities to Capabilities Mapping matrix

913969803

## PAGE 01456: (2026x Refresh1) Projects with file attachments

- page_id: `303991971`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991971/2026x+Refresh1+Projects+with+file+attachments
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:22.456+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305056268

305056271

305056270

You can attach any type of file from the local file system to your project.

Attached files are bundled with the project and remain attached after transferring the project, keeping project references to these files not broken.

The contents of the attached file can be modified and the changes are instantly saved to the project. Different versions of a server project can refer to different versions of the same attached file.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

305056267

**Related pages**

## PAGE 01457: (2026x Refresh1) Properties dialog

- page_id: `303994693`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994693/2026x+Refresh1+Properties+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:38.552+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming > (2026x Refresh1) Saving as image
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Image export options

768901281

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Image export options

768901283

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Image export options

768901282

In the **Properties** dialog, you can specify image export properties specific to the image format selected in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Save As Image dialog' space=''][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Save As Image dialog' space=''][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Save As Image dialog' space=''][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Save As Image dialog' space=''][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Save As Image dialog' space=''] dialog. For example, if the selected image format is .jpg, or .jpeg, the **Properties** dialog allows you to specify JPEG compression quality, image resolution (DPI), and exported image size.

To specify image export options

1. From the File menu, select Save as Image .
2. In the Save As Image dialog, specify the desired image format, and click the Options button.
3. In the Properties dialog, specify given properties.
4. Click **OK**. Property related informationIf you need more information about the properties provided in the **Properties** dialog, make sure that the **Show Description** button is pressed (as shown in the figure below), then select the desired property, and read its description in the description area on the bottom of the dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This Properties dialog displays the image export properties specific to the .jpg, .jpeg image format. If the highlighted button is pressed, the description of the selected property is provided in the description area on the bottom of the dialog.

#### NOTE: Image export options

Image export options

Default image export properties can be changed manually both via the **Environment Options** and the **Project Options** dialogs. To learn more, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Diagram image export' space=''] page.

1341338422

**Related pages:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Saving as image' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Save As Image dialog' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999210 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01458: (2026x Refresh1) Properties filename

- page_id: `303999210`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999210/2026x+Refresh1+Properties+filename
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:40.784+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Generating reports from the command line
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example

1092254395

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example

1092254398

1092279517

**On this page**

- [XML Properties File](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Properties+filename#Propertiesfilename-XMLPropertiesFile)

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example

1092254397

When you use -properties, other command actions are no longer necessary; the properties file contains the configuration information. The information in the properties file is the same as the information used in the command line.

Four parameters are needed to specify data for a report: **project**, **output**, **template**, and **package**. There are five more parameters called options, which are additional configuration information such as **report**, **autoImage**, **imageFormat**, **recursive**, and **outputOnBlankField**. All nine parameters are described in*[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using the Generate command to generate reports' space='']*.

A properties file is a simple text file. You can create and maintain a properties file with any text editors (see the following example).

```text

```

In the figure above, the command lines begin with a pound sign (#). The other lines contain key-value pairs. The key is on the left side of the equal sign, and the value is on the right. For instance, template is the key that corresponds to the value of the **Class Specification Report**.

The string representing the key and value in the properties file has a special character. The special character is an escape character. It must be used to prevent interpretation errors. Each escape character starts with a backslash.

| Escape Sequence | Character |
| --- | --- |
| \n | new line |
| \t | tab |
| \b | backspace |
| \f | form feed |
| \r | return |
| \” | “ (double quote) |
| \’ | ‘ (single quote) |
| \\ | \ (backslash) |
| \uDDDD | character from the Unicode character set (DDDD is four hex digits) |

##### XML Properties File

J2SE version 1.5 and Report Wizard version 15.5 allow you to use XML files to load and save key-value pairs.

Properties DTD:

```text
]]>
```

```text
C:\MagicDraw\samples\diagrams\class diagram.mdzip
	C:\\output\\output.rtf<\entry>
	Class Specification Report
	java;Library system;magiclibrary;objects
]]>
```

1092511756

**Related pages**

- Generating a report from Teamwork Cloud Server
- Uploading generated reports to servers
- Syntax rules
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using the Generate command to generate reports' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48abd90f-dc7c-40e8-b876-6c98336bb83c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1092254395</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a4bef34-998f-4aed-b6ba-d341361aeb50"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1092254398</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4d12f0f5-93b4-4ff7-ab9b-444720186861"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1092279517</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><ul><li><span class="toc-item-body"><a class="toc-link" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Properties+filename#Propertiesfilename-XMLPropertiesFile">XML Properties File</a></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3ad6c0a-a1a1-4356-ad34-16570a4c6760"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1092254397</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you use -properties, other command actions are no longer necessary; the properties file contains the configuration information. The information in the properties file is the same as the information used in the command line.</p><p>Four parameters are needed to specify data for a report: <strong>project</strong>, <strong>output</strong>, <strong>template</strong>, and <strong>package</strong>. There are five more parameters called options, which are additional configuration information such as <strong>report</strong>, <strong>autoImage</strong>, <strong>imageFormat</strong>, <strong>recursive</strong>, and <strong>outputOnBlankField</strong>. All nine parameters are described in<em> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using the Generate command to generate reports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generate - the Command to Generate Reports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em>.</p><p>A properties file is a simple text file. You can create and maintain a properties file with any text editors (see the following example).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b3eb2e64-4bec-4780-85a7-f45cda2be16d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Properties file sample</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#----main argument ----#
project = C:\\MagicDraw\\samples\\diagrams\\class diagram.mdzip
output = C:\\output\\output.rtf
template = Class Specification Report
package = java;Library System;magiclibrary;objects


#---- optional ----#
report = Built-in
autoImage = 1
imageFormat = png
recursive = false
outputOnBlankField = #NA]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In the figure above, the command lines begin with a pound sign (#). The other lines contain key-value pairs. The key is on the left side of the equal sign, and the value is on the right. For instance, template is the key that corresponds to the value of the <strong>Class Specification Report</strong>.</p><p>The string representing the key and value in the properties file has a special character. The special character is an escape character. It must be used to prevent interpretation errors. Each escape character starts with a backslash.</p><table class="wrapped" style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><th style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Escape Sequence </th><th style="margin-left: 30.0px;"> Character</th></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;">\n </td><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;">new line </td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;">\t </td><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;">tab </td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">\b</td><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">backspace</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">\f </td><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">form feed</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">\r</td><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">return</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">\”</td><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1"> “ (double quote)</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">\’</td><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">‘ (single quote)</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">\\</td><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">\ (backslash)</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">\uDDDD</td><td style="margin-left: 30.0px;" colspan="1">character from the Unicode character <br />set (DDDD is four hex digits)</td></tr></tbody></table><h3>XML Properties File</h3><p>J2SE version 1.5 and Report Wizard version 15.5 allow you to use XML files to load and save key-value pairs.</p><p>Properties DTD:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb3a1a03-5f03-41f9-97c6-604ccbd0fa48"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!-- DTD for properties -->
<!ELEMENT properties ( comment?, entry* ) >
<!ATTLIST properties version CDATA #FIXED "1.0">
<!ELEMENT comment (#PCDATA) >
<!ELEMENT entry (#PCDATA) >
<!ATTLIST entry key CDATA #REQUIRED>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c0d9833b-40cd-4b36-881a-3d24d78cdb91"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPEproperties SYSTEM
"http://java.sun.com/dtd/properties.dtd">
<properties>
	<entry key = "project">C:\MagicDraw\samples\diagrams\class diagram.mdzip</entry>
	<entry key = "output">C:\\output\\output.rtf<\entry>
	<entry key = "template">Class Specification Report</entry>
	<entry key = "package">java;Library system;magiclibrary;objects</entry>
</properties>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6222d8e5-373a-4a47-ac0f-dd711cd2cc95"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1092511756</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Generating+a+report+from+Teamwork+Cloud+Server">Generating a report from Teamwork Cloud Server</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Uploading+generated+reports+to+servers">Uploading generated reports to servers</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Syntax+rules">Syntax rules</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using the Generate command to generate reports" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995244 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01459: (2026x Refresh1) Properties Inheritance

- page_id: `303995244`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995244/2026x+Refresh1+Properties+Inheritance
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:24.165+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Formatting symbols
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All element properties have the “inherited” check box. This check box indicates whether the property is derived from the base element properties or is it specific.

The **Inherited** column check box value in the element properties pane specifies if the current property is synchronized with its parent property. When the **Inherited** value is “true”, the element property is changed after changing the parent property.

If the property has no correspondent property in the upper (parent) level, the **Inherited** column check box will be cleared and not available.

If the property is modified for the specific element and the value differs from the upper level current property value, the **Inherited** column check box is cleared automatically.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Formatting symbols' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>All element properties have the “inherited” check box. This check box indicates whether the property is derived from the base element properties or is it specific.</p><p>The <strong>Inherited</strong> column check box value in the element properties pane specifies if the current property is synchronized with its parent property. When the <strong>Inherited</strong> value is “true”, the element property is changed after changing the parent property.</p><p>If the property has no correspondent property in the upper (parent) level, the <strong>Inherited</strong> column check box will be cleared and not available.</p><p>If the property is modified for the specific element and the value differs from the upper level current property value, the <strong>Inherited</strong> column check box is cleared automatically.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bfc67ded-5f15-4cbf-b135-39abac861fd2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="84b3992d-f7e3-423e-9378-f287af93aee8"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Formatting symbols" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993788 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01460: (2026x Refresh1) Properties tab

- page_id: `303993788`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993788/2026x+Refresh1+Properties+tab
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:59.609+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Getting started > (2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface > (2026x Refresh1) Quick properties panel
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Multiple selection

1425917920

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Multiple selection

1425917923

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Multiple selection

1425917921

#### EXCERPT: Multiple selection

INLINE

The **Properties** tab at the bottom of the Model Browser allows you to quickly access basic information about a selected element or diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Properties tab in the Quick properties panel

#### TIP: Multiple selection

Multiple selection

- If multiple elements or symbols of different kinds are selected, only their common properties are displayed in the Properties tab.
- If more than 10 elements in the Containment tree or symbols on the diagram pane are selected, properties are displayed only by request. You need to click the link Show common properties for the selected items .

To open the **Properties** tab, do one of the following

- In the Model Browser, click the Properties tab.
- On the Window menu, click Properties .

The **Properties** tab includes the following tabs:

| Tab name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Element | Contains the main properties of the element's or diagram's specification. |
| Symbol | Contains the element symbol's properties. noteThis tab is available for symbols only. |
| Tags | Contains the tagged values of the selected element.When you click the Show Only Applied Stereotypes Tags button, only the tabs with values are listed. |
| Language properties | Contains the same data as the Language Properties property group in the element's Specification window.This tab is available only for the elements used for code generation, that is, class, attribute, and operation. |
| Traceability | Contains the same data as the Traceability property group in the element's Specification window. |

1425917919

**Related pages**

- Model Browser

## PAGE 01461: (2026x Refresh1) Property group toolbar

- page_id: `303994720`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994720/2026x+Refresh1+Property+group+toolbar

- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:43.381+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Specification window
- labels: ['expert-mode', 'standard-mode', 'all-mode', 'expert-property-display-mode', 'standard-property-display-mode', 'all-property-display-mode', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1655141189

1655141191

1655141190

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Icon | Name | Function |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Categorized view | Groups properties by the categories. |
|  | Alphabetical view | Lists properties alphabetically. |
|  | Show Description | Shows or hides the description area of the selected property. |
|  | Expand All | Expand all collapsed categories. |
|  | Collapse All | Collapse all expanded categories. |
|  | Strip Multiline Text | If the text in the property cell (e.g., ToDo) covers more than five rows, it is finished with ... showing only those five rows. The complete text is available in the tooltip or in the cell edit mode. |
|  | Properties | Change the properties display mode to Standard, Expert, or All. Select Customize, to customize displayed properties. |
|  | Show Private Inherited | Show the inherited properties. |
|  | Hide Inherited Relations | Hide the relationships that are inherited. |

The toolbar configuration can vary according to the selected property group.

five rows, it is finished with <strong>... </strong>showing only those five rows. The complete text is available in the tooltip or in the cell edit mode.</span><br /></span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Properties list" ac:alt="Properties list"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Properties.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Property group toolbar" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Properties<br /></span></td><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Change the properties display mode to <span><strong>Standard</strong><span>, <span><strong>Expert</strong><span>, or <span><strong>All</strong><span>. Select <span><strong>Customize</strong><span>, to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Customizing lists of properties and commands" /><ac:link-body>customize <span>displayed properties</span></ac:link-body></ac:link><span>.</span></span><br /></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Show Private Inherited button" ac:alt="Show Private Inherited button"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show_private_inherited.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Property group toolbar" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td colspan="1">Show Private Inherited</td><td colspan="1">Show the inherited properties.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Hide_inherited_relations.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Property group toolbar" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td colspan="1">Hide Inherited Relations</td><td colspan="1">Hide the relationships that are inherited.</td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d1b0b77b-d986-46a2-b36c-88974c38d96b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The toolbar configuration can vary according to the selected property group.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991301 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01462: (2026x Refresh1) Property path notation

- page_id: `303991301`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991301/2026x+Refresh1+Property+path+notation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:10.073+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x Refresh1) Decomposing Blocks
- labels: ['shorthand-notation', 'mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When modeling deeply nested structures, you can display nested properties within a system hierarchy in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Parametric Diagram' space='']. You can represent nested properties by displaying the property shape inside another property shape. For example, on the left side of the figure below, the *wheel*, *brake*, and *caliper* are nested parts represented as nested [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] shapes. Representing deeply nested properties in this manner requires a lot of space in the diagram.

Alternatively, you can represent nested properties by using dot notation, which expresses a structural hierarchy compactly in the form of a text string. Dot notation references a nested property accessible through a sequence of intermediate properties from a referencing context. The name of the referenced property is built by a string of names separated by “.”, resulting in a path name that identifies the property in its local context. This notation is purely a notational shorthand for a property that could otherwise be shown within a structure of nested property shapes. For example, on the right side of the figure below, the nested parts are represented using dot notation with the higher-level part names (*vehicle*, *wheel*, and *brake)*.

The meaning of both of them is the same: *caliper* is a part of *brake* which is a part of *wheel*; *wheel* is a part of *vehicle*.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Two alternative ways to represent nested parts in the SysML Internal Block Diagram*.*

#### INFO: Additional information

Additional information

The representation of nested properties with dot notation form is used on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='MED'] and in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Instance table' space=''] column headers.

##### Displaying type names in a property path

The dot notation separates the property names. However, there are many situations when properties are unnamed (see the right side of the figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The representation of a property path with dot notation of nested part*s*when they are unnamed.

If you use an earlier version than 18.5, you must set the option which shows type names in a property path with dot notation. For a new project, this option is enabled by default.

To show type names in the property path

1. Select Options > Project .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Setting project options' space=''] , set the Show Type Names in Nested Path option to true . The type names of the nested parts are shown in the property path by separating them with a colon (:).

For example, after enabling the **Show Type Names in Nested Path** option, the part with dot notation, on the right side of the figure, provides information about nested part types.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The property type names are shown in the property path with dot notation*.*

##### Converting nested properties to dot notation

You can convert nested properties from the system structure to dot notation form.

To convert nested properties to dot notation form

- Select the nested property shape from the property shape structure, drag it to the empty space on the diagram pane, and in the Question dialog, click the Represent as Shortcut button. [ATTACHMENT filename='converting_nested_part_to_dot_notation_form.png']
- Open the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog or **Display Parameters/Properties** dialog , select an appropriate deep nested property in the element tree, and click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='nested_part_in_display_parts_ports_dialog.png'] The nested property is displayed in dot notation form. [ATTACHMENT filename='part_with_dot_notation.png']

##### Converting properties with dot notation to a structure of nested properties

You can convert a property shape displayed with dot notation form to a nested properties structure.

To convert properties with dot notation form to a nested properties structure

1. From the property shortcut menu, select Refactor > Convert to Nested Parts . The property with dot notation is converted to a structure of nested properties.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The part *caliper* represented with dot notation is converted to a nested structure of Part Properties.

##### Moving a part from the diagram to a structure compartment

You can move a property shape with dot notation from the diagram back to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Compartments' space=''] of a nested property.

To move a property with dot notation to the compartments of a nested property

1. Select the property shape with dot notation.
2. Drag it to the structure compartments. The property is moved to the structure compartments and shown in dot notation form.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The part *caliper* is moved back to the nested property compartments.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Parametric Diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1' space='']Displaying parts and ports
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying parameters and properties' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Connecting parts through interface' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reference Property' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Property' space='MED']Value Property
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Property' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Participant Property' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Bound Reference' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Distributed Property' space='MED']

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the**Car Braking Analysis**sample model.Download [ATTACHMENT filename='CarBrakingAnalysis.mdzip'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
