<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000317 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01463: (2026x Refresh1) Property Set elements for measurement tables

- page_id: `304000317`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000317/2026x+Refresh1+Property+Set+elements+for+measurement+tables
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:00.629+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures > (2026x Refresh1) Measurements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Working with measurement tables, you can create or add Property Sets. The list of available Property Sets according to the domain is provided in the following table:

| Measurement Tables | Property Sets and their specializations |
| --- | --- |
| Strategic Measurement Tables | CapabilityChallengeDriverEffectEnterprise GoalEnterprise ObjectiveEnterprise VisionMeasurement Set or their specializationsOpportunityStrategic InformationStrategic PhaseValue Item |
| Operational Measurement Tables | High Level Operational ConceptKnown ResourceMeasurement Set (except Value Item)Mission TaskMission ThreadOperational ArchitectureOperational InformationOperational InterfaceOperational MitigationOperational PerformerOperational Signal Operational Activity |
| Services Measurement Tables | Measurement Set (except Value Item)ServiceService ArchitectureService Function Service InterfaceService Signal |
| Personnel Measurement Tables | CompetenceFunctionMeasurement Set (except Value Item)OrganizationPersonPostProjectResponsibility |
| Resources Measurement Tables | Capability ConfigurationFunctionKnown ResourceMeasurement Set (except Value Item)Mission Engineering ThreadNatural ResourceOrganizationPersonPostProjectResource ArchitectureResource ArtifactResource InformationResource InterfaceResource MitigationResource ServiceResource Service InterfaceResource SignalResponsibilitySecurity EnclaveSoftwareSystemTechnologyWhole Life Configuration |
| Security Measurement Tables | Measurement Set (except Value Item)Operational MitigationResource MitigationRiskSecurity EnclaveSecurity ProcessSecurity Risk |
| Project Measurement Tables | Measurement Set (except Value Item)ProjectProject ActivityProject Milestone |
| Standards Measurement Tables | Measurement Set (except Value Item)ProtocolProtocol StackStandardTechnology |
| Actual Resources Typical Measurement Tables | CompetenceMeasurement Set (except Value Item)OrganizationPersonPostProject Responsibility |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994044 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01464: (2026x Refresh1) Protocol State Machine diagram

- page_id: `303994044`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994044/2026x+Refresh1+Protocol+State+Machine+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:51.399+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) UML diagrams
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

308031736

308031738

308031737

**Overview**

A Protocol State Machine diagram is, in general, a more narrowly defined [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) State Machine diagram' space='']. It is always defined in the context of a classifier. It specifies which [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='MED'] of the classifier can be called, in which [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MED'], and under which condition, thus specifying the allowable call sequences on the classifier's operations.

The Protocol State Machine presents the possible and permitted [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MED'] on the instances of its context classifier, together with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='MED'] that carry the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MED'].

In this manner, an instance lifecycle can be created for a classifier, by specifying the order in which the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='MED'] can be activated and the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MED'] through which the instance progresses during its existence.

**Purpose**

The Protocol State Machine diagram is created for use with the Protocol State Machine and the Protocol Transitions. It limits the scope of the State Machine diagram to show only the transitions that trigger the state changes.

**Usage**

A Protocol State Machine diagram can be used to:

- show different stable class states of objects that could exist for some time
- explain how objects could change their states over time

**Summary**

Protocol State Machine diagrams are valuable because they:

- place the focus on what it takes to legally change an object
- reveal conditions that ensure the integrity of the object while it is being manipulated by other objects in the system
- keep the designer from focusing on the implementation details before resolving the integrity issues.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a Protocol State Machine diagram

308031735

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating diagrams' space='']

It is always defined in the context of a classifier. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991351 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01465: (2026x Refresh1) Provided and Required Interfaces

- page_id: `303991351`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991351/2026x+Refresh1+Provided+and+Required+Interfaces
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:40.821+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x Refresh1) Defining interfaces using ports
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Provided/Required Interfaces help to identify compatible ports that can be connected together in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''].

##### Creating provided and required interfaces

To create new Provided/Required Interface of a port

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click any kind of port shape and from the shortcut menu select Specification . In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''] , select the Provided/Required Interfaces property group.
  - Select a Port shape and from its smart manipulator, click [ATTACHMENT filename='provided_required_interfaces.png'] .
2. In the Provided/Required Interfaces pane, click the Add button and select either Provided or Required.
3. Two situations are available:

- 
  - If the port is typed, the Select Interface dialog opens (see the following figure). In this dialog you can do the following:
    - Select and existing interface (and flow specifications) to be used as the Provided / Required Interface of the port.
    - Make sure the Creation Mode is switched on, click the Create button, and select Interface . In the specification window, type its name, and click Close . The new interface is created. Select it to be used as the Provided / Required Interface of the port. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_interface_dialog.png']
  - If the port is not typed, the Select Port Type dialog opens (see the following figure). You can do the following:
    - Set provided interface as port type (for Provided Interface only). The Select Interface dialog opens. In this dialog, you can either choose an existing interface or create a new one, to be used as the Provided Interface and the type of the port.
    - Create “dummy” port type automatically . The Select Interface dialog opens. In this dialog, you can either choose an existing interface or create a new one, to be used as the Provided or Required Interface of the port. In addition, a dummy classifier, realizing (for Provided) or using (for Required) the interface, is automatically created and used as the type of the port.
    - Select or create port type manually . If you select this option, the Select Port Type dialog opens. In this dialog, choose a classifier to be used as the type of the port. Click OK , the Select Interface dialog opens. In this dialog, you either choose an existing interface or create a new one, to be used as the Provided or Required Interface of the port. In addition, a Realization (or Usage) dependency is automatically created from the port type to the Provided (or Required) Interface of the port. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_port_type.png']

##### Displaying provided/required interfaces

To display provided/required interfaces

1. Select the port shape that has the provided/required interfaces you want to display.
2. Do one of the following: - From the port shortcut menu, select Display > Display Provided/Required Interfaces . - On the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select Display Provided/Required Interfaces . The Required/Provided Interfaces are displayed on the port, in the form of ball-socket (lollipop) notation.

Ports can provide or require many interfaces, therefore, you can choose which ones to display or hide. Use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 specific compartments' space='']**Edit Compartment** dialog to manage the visibility of these interfaces.

**Sample model**

The model used in the figure of this page is the **Inverted****Pendulum** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download [InvertedPendulum.mdzip](https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9919520/InvertedPendulum.mdzip?version=1&modificationDate=1498477335401&api=v2).
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\Inverted Pendulum\Inverted Pendulum.mdzip.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

In this dialog you can do the following:<br /><ul><li>Select and existing interface (and flow specifications) to be used as the Provided / Required Interface of the port.</li><li>Make sure the <strong>Creation Mode</strong> is switched on, click the <strong>Create</strong> button, and select <strong>Interface</strong>. In the specification window, type its name, and click <strong>Close</strong>. The new interface is created. Select it to be used as the Provided / Required Interface of the port.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_interface_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Provided and Required Interfaces" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></li><li>If the port is not typed, the <strong>Select Port Type</strong> dialog opens (see the following figure). You can do the following:<br /><ul><li><strong>Set provided interface as port type</strong> (for Provided Interface only). The <strong>Select Interface</strong> dialog opens. In this dialog, you can either choose an existing interface or create a new one, to be used as the Provided Interface and the type of the port.</li><li><strong>Create “dummy” port type automatically</strong>. The <strong>Select Interface</strong> dialog opens. In this dialog, you can either choose an existing interface or create a new one, to be used as the Provided or Required Interface of the port. In addition, a dummy classifier, realizing (for Provided) or using (for Required) the interface, is automatically created and used as the type of the port.</li><li><strong>Select or create port type manually</strong>. If you select this option, the <strong>Select Port Type</strong> dialog opens. In this dialog, choose a classifier to be used as the type of the port. Click <strong>OK</strong>, the <strong>Select Interface</strong> dialog opens. In this dialog, you either choose an existing interface or create a new one, to be used as the Provided or Required Interface of the port. In addition, a Realization (or Usage) dependency is automatically created from the port type to the Provided (or Required) Interface of the port.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_port_type.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Provided and Required Interfaces" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><h3>Displaying provided/required interfaces</h3><p><br /></p><p>To display provided/required interfaces</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="fed4118f-385e-4e79-9153-1ee9b797fdb4">Select the port shape that has the provided/required interfaces you want to display.</li><li data-uuid="cb61b56a-08d5-4c62-bc43-9c27ffbeead8">Do one of the following:<br />- From the port shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Provided/Required Interfaces</strong>.<br />- On the diagram toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Provided and Required Interfaces" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> and select <strong>Display Provided/Required Interfaces</strong>.<br /><p>The Required/Provided Interfaces are displayed on the port, in the form of ball-socket (lollipop) notation.</p></li></ol><p>Ports can provide or require many interfaces, therefore, you can choose which ones to display or hide. Use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 specific compartments" /><ac:link-body><strong>Edit Compartment</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> to manage the visibility of these interfaces.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figure of this page is the <strong>Inverted</strong><strong>Pendulum</strong> sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="90996782-62c4-4192-a8b9-8f78af05e555">Download<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9919520/InvertedPendulum.mdzip?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1498477335401&amp;api=v2">InvertedPendulum.mdzip</a>.<br /></span></em></li><li data-uuid="be894d7b-f99a-48be-8a85-74b257dd8755">Find in modeling tool <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\SysML v1\Inverted Pendulum\Inverted Pendulum.mdzip.</span></em></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000732 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01466: (2026x Refresh1) Publishing projects to Cameo Collaborator

- page_id: `304000732`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000732/2026x+Refresh1+Publishing+projects+to+Cameo+Collaborator
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:36.351+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures > (2026x Refresh1) Managing reports
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Publishing models to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloudallows you to present them in a simplified form for users who do not know modeling languages, such as UML or SysML v1.Publishing is the beginning step of the model review workflow, as displayed in the following figure:

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

To publish documents to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, you must [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin' space='MCS'] in your modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Publishing a model is the first step in the workflow for reviewing models and providing feedback.

You can republish a model each time you update it whilereacting to the reviewer's feedback. When republishing the model, you can either create a new document with a different name or update the one already stored in Teamwork Cloud. The updated document includes all the comments from the previous version unless the comments are disabled when republishing.

To learn more, see the following topics:

#### NOTE: Publishing documents from synchronized projects

Publishing documents from synchronized projects

If you want to publish a Cameo Collaborator document from a synchronized project on the target Teamwork Cloud server, the project must already have the Cameo Collaborator profile used in it. In addition, the commenting and model editing capabilities in such a document will be disabled so that the synchronized project on the target server cannot be modified. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cross-cluster resource synchronization' space='MCS'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Publishing models to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">allows you to present them in a simplified form for users who do not know modeling languages, such as UML or SysML v1. </span>Publishing is the beginning step of the model review workflow, as displayed in the following figure:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd518cb5-3be9-4ff7-9fc3-911d561a6cfb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To publish documents to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, you must <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Installing Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin" ri:space-key="MCS" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[install the Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in your modeling tool.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="main_workflow_publishing.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="line-height: 1.66667;text-align: center;">Publishing a model is the first step in the workflow for reviewing models and providing feedback.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></h6><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can republish a model each time you update it while </span>reacting to the reviewer's feedback<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. When republishing the model, you can either create a new document with a different name or update the one already stored in Teamwork Cloud. The updated document includes all the comments from the previous version unless the comments are disabled when republishing.<br /></span></p><p>To learn more, see the following topics:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1af390c3-7a92-4d14-bb6a-49fc02d6d986" /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f646a71d-6e9b-4f95-a76f-ab6b5b220655"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Publishing documents from synchronized projects</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to publish a Cameo Collaborator document from a synchronized project on the target Teamwork Cloud server, the project must already have the Cameo Collaborator profile used in it. In addition, the commenting and model editing capabilities in such a document will be disabled so that the synchronized project on the target server cannot be modified. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Cross-cluster resource synchronization" ri:space-key="MCS" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about cross-cluster resource synchronization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:57.199+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Generating report documents
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Publishing models to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloudallows you to present them in a simplified form for users who do not know modeling languages, such as UML or SysML v1.Publishing is the beginning step of the model review workflow, as displayed in the following figure:

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

To publish documents to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, you must [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin' space='MCS'] in your modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Publishing a model is the first step in the workflow for reviewing models and providing feedback.

You can republish a model each time you update it whilereacting to the reviewer's feedback. When republishing the model, you can either create a new document with a different name or update the one already stored in Teamwork Cloud. The updated document includes all the comments from the previous version unless the comments are disabled when republishing.

To learn more, see the following topics:

#### NOTE: Publishing documents from synchronized projects

Publishing documents from synchronized projects

If you want to publish a Cameo Collaborator document from a synchronized project on the target Teamwork Cloud server, the project must already have the Cameo Collaborator profile used in it. In addition, the commenting and model editing capabilities in such a document will be disabled so that the synchronized project on the target server cannot be modified. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cross-cluster resource synchronization' space='MCS'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The updated document includes all the comments from the previous version unless the comments are disabled when republishing.<br /></span></p><p>To learn more, see the following topics:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1af390c3-7a92-4d14-bb6a-49fc02d6d986" /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f646a71d-6e9b-4f95-a76f-ab6b5b220655"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Publishing documents from synchronized projects</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to publish a Cameo Collaborator document from a synchronized project on the target Teamwork Cloud server, the project must already have the Cameo Collaborator profile used in it. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995116 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01468: (2026x Refresh1) Pusher and magnet

- page_id: `303995116`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995116/2026x+Refresh1+Pusher+and+magnet
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:10.554+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

903750137

903750139

903750138

This feature allows for rearranging element symbols on a diagram easily. The Pusher moves a group of symbols towards the pushing direction, thus creating an empty space for adding new symbols to a diagram.

Magnet works in the opposite way. It moves the selected group of symbols to an empty space on a diagram.

Now you do not need to select separate symbols on a diagram to move them to another location. Just click the **Pusher** or **Magnet** button on the diagram palette and move the whole group of symbols toward the direction you need.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To push the diagram fragment

1. On the diagram palette, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='pusher.png'] Pusher button.
2. On the diagram pane, click the diagram fragment you need to move, and holding the right mouse button, drag it to the left or down.

To pull the diagram fragment

1. On the diagram palette, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='magnet.png'] Magnet button.
2. On the diagram pane, click the diagram fragment you need to move, and holding the right mouse button, drag it to the right or up.

903750136

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Diagramming' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991549 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01469: (2026x Refresh1) PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships

- page_id: `303991549`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991549/2026x+Refresh1+PV-1+Project+Portfolio+Relationships
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:48.362+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Project viewpoint
- labels: ['pv-1', 'pv-1-project-portfolio-relationships', 'pv-1-responsibility-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305775065

305775067

305775066

**Description**

The PV-1 represents an organizational perspective on programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives.

The PV-1 enables the user to model the organizational structures needed to manage programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. It shows dependency relationships between the actual organizations that own the programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. This model could be used to represent organizational relationships associated with transformation initiatives along with those who are responsible for managing programs, projects, and portfolios. The PV-1 provides a means of analyzing the main dependencies between acquisition elements or transformation elements.

The intended usage of the PV-1 includes, but is not limited to:

- Program management (specified acquisition program structure).
- Project organization.
- Cross-cutting initiatives to be tracked across portfolios.

**Implementation**

PV-1 can be represented using:

- PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='pv_1.png']
- PV-1 Responsibility Matrix which is an editable Dependency Matrix. [ATTACHMENT filename='pv_1_matrix.png']

The model is strongly linked with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) CV-4 Capability Dependencies' space=''] model which shows capability groupings and dependencies.

305775064

## PAGE 01470: (2026x Refresh1) PV-2 Project Timelines

- page_id: `303991544`
- space_key: `MT`

- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Project viewpoint
- labels: ['pv-2', 'pv-2-project-timelines', 'pv-2-gantt-chart', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305999026

305999028

305999027

**Description**

The PV-2 provides a timeline perspective on programs. The PV-2 is intended primarily to support the acquisition and fielding processes including the management of dependencies between projects and the integration of DoDD 5000.1 Defense Acquisition System policies to achieve a successfully integrated capability. The PV-2 is not limited to the acquisition and fielding processes.

The intended usage of the PV-2 includes:

- Project management and control (including delivery timescales).
- Project dependency risk identification.
- Management of dependencies.
- Portfolio management.

**Implementation**

PV-2 can be represented using a PV-2 diagram which is realized as a Gantt Chart.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Use of PV-2 should support the management of capability delivery and be aligned with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) CV-3 Capability Phasing' space=''] model, if one exists.

305999025

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Theme' space='MED']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Milestone Dependency' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Milestone' space='MED']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991555 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01471: (2026x Refresh1) PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping

- page_id: `303991555`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991555/2026x+Refresh1+PV-3+Project+to+Capability+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:49.017+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Project viewpoint
- labels: ['pv-3', 'pv-3-project-to-capability-mapping', 'pv-3-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

306143199

306143202

306143200

**Description**

The PV-3 supports the acquisition and deployment processes, including the management of dependencies between projects and the integration of all relevant project and program elements to achieve a capability.

The PV-3 maps programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives to capabilities to show how the specific elements help to achieve a capability. Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives are mapped to the capability for a particular timeframe. Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives may contribute to multiple capabilities and may mature across time. The analysis can be used to identify capability redundancies and shortfalls, highlight phasing issues, expose organizational or system interoperability problems, and support program decisions, such as when to phase out a legacy system.

The intended usage of the PV-3 includes:

- Tracing capability requirements to projects.
- Capability audit.

**Implementation**

PV-3 can be represented using a PV-3 diagram which is a non-editable Dependency Matrix. Capabilities are used as row elements and Actual Projects are used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This model is analogous to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix' space=''], but provides the interface between Capability and Actual Project models rather than Operational to System Models.

306143198

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Maps To Capability' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Dependency' space='']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating PV-3 matrix' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives are mapped to the capability for a particular timeframe. Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives may contribute to multiple capabilities and may mature across time. The analysis can be used to identify capability redundancies and shortfalls, highlight phasing issues, expose organizational or system interoperability problems, and support program decisions, such as when to phase out a legacy system.</p><p>The intended usage of the PV-3 includes:</p><ul><li>Tracing capability requirements to projects.</li><li>Capability audit.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>PV-3 can be represented using a PV-3 diagram which is a non-editable Dependency Matrix. Capabilities are used as row elements and Actual Projects are used as column elements.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping" ac:alt="PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping"><ri:attachment ri:filename="pv_3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>This model is analogous to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-5a Operational Activity to System Function Traceability Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, but provides the interface between Capability and Actual Project models rather than Operational to System Models.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="59709386-c38c-4256-bdd0-ea87e9076c2c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">306143198</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Capability" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Actual Project" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Maps To Capability" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Dependency" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating PV-3 matrix" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997308 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01472: (2026x Refresh1) Qualified name display style

- page_id: `303997308`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997308/2026x+Refresh1+Qualified+name+display+style
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:01.933+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Owner of model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There is an option to show the owner hierarchy starting from the model library as the root. *Model Library*****is a package with the stereotype *«modelLibrary»* applied.

This option is called the **Qualified name display style**.

To change the **Qualified name display style**value

1. From the Options menu, select the Project command. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Setting project options' space='']**Project Option** dialo g opens.
2. In the option group list, select General > General , and then select the Qualified name display style option.
3. For the option, set Model Library Relative (default value for a new project) to display the full qualified name hierarchy of the element starting from the model library as a root element. The model library itself is not displayed. The Qualified name display style property allows for having the relative path for library items used in the project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There is an option to show the owner hierarchy starting from the model library as the root. <em>Model Library<strong> </strong></em>is a package with the stereotype <em>«modelLibrary»</em> applied.</p><p>This option is called the <strong>Qualified name display style</strong>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the <strong>Qualified name display style </strong>value</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select the <strong>Project</strong> command. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Setting project options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Option</strong> dialo</ac:link-body></ac:link>g opens.</li><li>In the option group list, select <strong>General </strong>&gt; <strong>General</strong>, and then select the <strong>Qualified name display style </strong> option.</li><li>For the option, set <strong>Model Library Relative </strong>(default value for a new project) to display the full qualified name hierarchy of the element starting from the model library as a root element. The model library itself is not displayed. The <strong>Qualified name display style</strong> property allows for having the relative path for library items used in the project. </li></ol>
````

## PAGE 01473: (2026x Refresh1) Query methods

- page_id: `303990512`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990512/2026x+Refresh1+Query+methods
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:40.513+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Query tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1353381367

1353381369

1353381366

**On this page**

1353381368

This section provides query methods that you can use to retrieve an array of elements by query pattern.

#### Retrieving an Array of Element by Query Pattern

```text

```

- Parameters : query : String (the query string as described in section Recognizable Query Patterns .
- Returns : java.util.List<Element> (a list or a collection of elements or an empty List if there is no matching element).
- The sample code to print all Use Case elements under a packagable element named foo is as follows:

```text

```

#### Getting a Single Result from Query Functions

Since a model element does not have a unique identified attribute so all methods will be returned in a Collection. Thus we will need convenient methods to get a single result from the Collection. Additional methods for getting a single result from query methods are as follows:

- first() – returns the first element from a collection.
- last() – returns the last element from a collection.
- get(int) – returns the nth element of a collection.
- unique() – returns the first element from a collection (equivalent to first).

##### Getting the First Element from a Collection

```text

```

- Returns : Element (the first element from a query result collection or a null value if there is no matching element).
- A Sample code to print the first package is as follows:

```text

```

##### Getting the Last Element from a Collection

```text

```

- Returns : Element (the last element from a query result collection or a null value if there is no matching element).
- A sample code to print the last package is as follows:

```text

```

##### Getting the nth Element from a Collection

```text

```

- Parameters: index : int (a zero-based integer indicating which element to retrieve).
- Returns: Element (an element from a specified index of a query result collection or if the index is out of bounds, it will print a warning message to console/log and returns a null value)
- A sample code to print the third package is as follows:

```text

```

##### Getting the Unique Element from a Collection

```text

```

This method is equivalent to the one in section **Getting the First Element from a Collection**.

- Returns: Element (a unique element from a query result collection or a null value if there is no matching element).
- A sample code to print a single package is as follows:

```text

```

#### Retrieving an Array of Elements by Name

```text

```

Retrieve an element from a selected package scope whose element name exactly equals a given parameter. This method is equivalent to query pattern *[name=var].

- Parameters : name : String (the element name string).
- Returns : java.util.List<Element> (a List or a collection of elements or an empty List if there is no matching element).
- A sample code to print the first element whose name exactly equals to foo is as follows:

```text

```

#### Retrieving Elements by ID

```text

```

Retrieve an element from a selected package scope whose element ID exactly equals a given parameter. This method is equivalent to query pattern #id.

- Parameters : id : String (an element’s ID).
- Returns : Element (an element or a null value if there is no matching element).
- A sample code to print an element that contains ID 17_0_3_8e9027a_1325220298609_477646 is as follows:

```text

```

The Query tool provides extra functions to retrieve elements by using a query language. Like other Custom tools, the Query tool '*querytool.jar*' must be presented in the 'extensions' folder of the Report Wizard plugin. For further information on Custom tools and the installation, see *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom tool' space='']*.

Type the following code in a template to import the Query tool.

```text

```

## PAGE 01475: (2026x Refresh1) Query tool API

- page_id: `303990503`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990503/2026x+Refresh1+Query+tool+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:39.130+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Query tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Query Tool Class diagram.

**Class QueryTool**

**Constructor**

- 
  - QueryTool() Creates the Query tool from your selected element scope. This method will use a default scope from the variable $elements.
  - QueryTool( scope : Collection<Element>) Creates the Query tool from a specific element scope.

**Methods**

- 
  - +get( query :String) : QueryList
  - +getElementsByName( name : String) : QueryList
  - +getElementById( name : String) : Element

**Class QueryList**

**Methods**

- 
  - +get(index : int) : Element
  - +first() : Element
  - +last() : Element
  - +unique () : Element

This method will use a default scope from the variable $elements.</li><li>QueryTool( scope : Collection&lt;Element&gt;) <br />Creates the Query tool from a specific element scope.</li></ul></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>Methods</strong></p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>+get( query :String) : QueryList</li><li>+getElementsByName( name : String) : QueryList </li><li>+getElementById( name : String) : Element</li></ul></li></ul><p><strong>Class QueryList</strong></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>Methods</strong></p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>+get(index : int) : Element</li><li>+first() : Element </li><li>+last() : Element </li><li>+unique () : Element</li></ul></li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994898 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01476: (2026x Refresh1) Quick filter

- page_id: `303994898`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994898/2026x+Refresh1+Quick+filter
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:52.724+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Specification window
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1655259839

1655259841

1655259840

To find a property, just start to type the desired property name in the open dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A Quick filter box appears after starting to type the name you are searching for.

The quick filter box is displayed only when ten or more properties are displayed in the property list.

The quick filter allows you to quickly find the required property in the property list. This is especially handy with a long properties list. Properties can be filtered by the keyword entered in this field.

You can also use the quick filter in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Customizing environment options' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Setting project options' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog' space=''] dialogs in the Specification window as well.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="44ae134e-44a9-4532-a354-543be30c6ea8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1655259839</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b3336b51-5471-4adc-b886-fef6292b29ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1655259841</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e961ad47-3eeb-464f-9b1e-c9a7bd8154d6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1655259840</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target">To find a property, just start to type the desired property name in the open dialog.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="guick_filter.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Quick filter" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">A Quick filter box appears after starting to type the name you are searching for.</span></h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="880b634f-5be3-420c-a3c5-33f84857ba95"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The quick filter box is displayed only when ten or more properties are displayed in the property list.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The quick filter allows you to quickly find the required property in the property list. This is especially handy with a long properties list. Properties can be filtered by the keyword entered in this field.</p><p>You can also use the quick filter in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Customizing environment options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Environment Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> dialogs in the Specification window as well.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991990 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01477: (2026x Refresh1) Quick Find dialog

- page_id: `303991990`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991990/2026x+Refresh1+Quick+Find+dialog
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:01.314+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Searching
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

You can use a quick search to find an element by its name. A very simple and easy to use dialog is designed to perform a quick search. In this dialog, you just need to type a name of the element you are searching for and select, if it's a type, diagram, or element. 
 
To open the **Quick Find** dialog, do one of the following

- On the toolbar of the Containment , Diagrams , Inheritance , or Model Extensions tab, click [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_find_button.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_find_dialog_.png']
- On the main menu, click Edit > Quick Find .
- Press Ctrl + Alt + F.
- On the main toolbar, click the arrow nearby the [ATTACHMENT filename='find_button_2.png'] button and then select Quick Find . [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_find_dialog_updated.png']

Search results are displayed in the drop-down list, which opens when you start typing the search phrase. Click the element you need in this list or correct the search query to perform a new search. The element will be selected in the Containment tree on the Model Browser as well as on the active diagram, if the element has symbols on the active diagram.

The following table provides descriptions of the **Quick Find** dialog elements.

| Dialog element name/image | Dialog element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Search for | Options group | Click Type to search only in types, that is, classes, interfaces, use cases, and others.Click Diagram to search only in diagrams.Click Any Element to search in all elements of your project. |
| Search By Name | Text box with a drop-down list | Type a phrase to search for in element names. See the search results in the drop-down list.You may use wildcard characters (*,?) in your search phrase:"*" substitutes any range of characters. So if your search phrase is "*dd", all the elements containing "dd" in their names can be found. For example, "Address" and "ThreadDeath"."?" substitutes exactly one character. So if your search phrase is "?dd", only the elements with "dd" in their names starting from the second character can be found. For example, "Address". "ThreadDeath" cannot be found in this case, because it contains more than one character before the search phrase "dd".Be sure the Match Text Anywhere command is unchecked when using wildcard characters! |
|  | Menu button | Click the button and customize search options. Match Text AnywhereTo search for matching phrase in any part of the element name, click the command to add the check mark.To explicitly search from the beginning of element name, click the command to remove the check mark.Use Camel CaseTo search for matching phrase typed in camel case, click the command to add the check mark.To search for matching phrase typed in regular case, click the command to remove the check mark.You can use camel case as follows:Type only the first letters (either capitalized or not) of each word, for example, "and" or "AND" to find "Analysis and Design".Type skipping spacers, for example, "cv3" to find "CV-3".Perform partial search, for example, "ibd" to find SysML v1 Internal Block diagram. Be sure the Match Text Anywhere command is checked! |
| More | Menu button | Click the button to load the next 100 matches. By default, the first 100 matches are shown in the dialog. |
| PreviousNext | Menu button | Available in the Tree view only.Click to navigate to a previous or next result.Alternatively, you can press the Shift + F3 keyboard shortcut to select the previous match or press F3 to select the next match. |
| Switch to Tree | Menu button | Click to switch from the List to Tree view.Alternatively, press the Alt+B keyboard shortcut. |
| Apply Filter (Ctrl+Space) | Check box | Click to clear the check box, if you need to remove the search scope filter. If you clear the Apply Filter check-box once, your selection is remembered for the next time. |
| Filter Options | Menu button | Click the button and customize search scope filter options. Be sure the Apply Filter check box is selected!Hide Uncommon ElementsReduces the search results list by hiding uncommon elements, keeping elements such as Interface, Association Class, Class, and Component in the list.Exclude Auxiliary ResourcesReduces the search results list by excluding elements marked as auxiliary. By default, elements in the standard/system profiles are marked as auxiliary. This option does not hide the following:UML datatypes (placed under MagicDraw Profile) and UML2 Metamodel (placed under UML Standard profile) only for UML project.UML2 Metamodel, SysML v1 value types, and stereotypes for SysML v1 project.Elements with <<ignoreAuxFilter>> stereotype applied and all its elements recursively. How to apply stereotype >> |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993779 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01478: (2026x Refresh1) Quick properties panel

- page_id: `303993779`
- space_key: `MT`

- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1412562694

1412562696

1412562695

The **Quick properties panel** has three tabs, as shown in the figure below:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Quick properties panel

- The**Zoom** tab zooms in on the current diagram.
- The**Documentation** tab shows documentation associated with the selected item.
- The**Properties** tab shows the specification and symbol properties of the selected element or diagram.

1412562692

**Related pages**

## PAGE 01479: (2026x Refresh1) Quickly navigating through conflicting changes

- page_id: `303996767`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996767/2026x+Refresh1+Quickly+navigating+through+conflicting+changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:20.550+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools > (2026x Refresh1) Model Merge > (2026x Refresh1) Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['conflicting-changes', 'merged-result', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1981192965

INLINE

1981192968

INLINE

1981192967

INLINE

You can quickly navigate through the conflicting changes in the **Merged Result** tree/list. Use the filter on the right of the **Merged Result** tree/list to filter conflicting changes by type. You can select to filter conflicting changes resolved by the user or resolved automatically. Moreover, click conflicting changes notification and it will filter conflicting changes resolved automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1981192964

INLINE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997907 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01480: (2026x Refresh1) Realization Diagram Wizard

- page_id: `303997907`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997907/2026x+Refresh1+Realization+Diagram+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:43.115+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Model Visualizer
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.

The **Realization Diagram Wizard** shows a table of the largest element groups that realize an interface.

To create a diagram using the **Realization Diagram Wizard**

1. Open the Realization Diagram Wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the Diagrams menu, select Diagram Wizards > Realization Diagram Wizard .
  - From the Analyze menu, select Model Visualizer . In the open Model Visualizer dialog, select to open the Realization Diagram Wizard and click **Start** .
2. Follow the steps as directed by the wizard.

What you should know when selecting Implementations:

- The **Add all implementations into one diagram** check box creates only one diagram for all selected realizations. This option is available only if the selected realizations can be added into one diagram (the same diagram type).
- The **Show outside specializations** check box shows realizations when derived interfaces are in the scope, but specializations are outside the scope.
- The **Show outside implementations** check box counts outside derived elements from displayed realizations. Otherwise, the realization will not be fully displayed and the diagram will not be valid.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec751953-0d12-400d-906d-0bb0608c6ef0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>The <strong>Realization Diagram Wizard</strong> shows a table of the largest element groups that realize an interface.</p><p> </p><p>To create a diagram using the <strong>Realization Diagram Wizard </strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Realization Diagram Wizard</strong><strong> </strong>by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Diagram Wizards</strong> &gt; <strong>Realization</strong><strong> Diagram Wizard</strong>.</li><li>From the <strong>Analyze</strong> menu, select <strong>Model Visualizer</strong>. In the open Model Visualizer dialog, select to open the <strong>Realization</strong><strong> Diagram Wizard</strong> and click <strong><strong>Start</strong></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps as directed by the wizard.</li></ol><p> </p><p>What you should know when selecting Implementations:</p><ul><li><p>The <strong>Add all implementations into one diagram</strong> check box creates only one diagram for all selected realizations. This option is available only if the selected realizations can be added into one diagram (the same diagram type).</p></li><li><p>The <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Show outside specializations</strong> check box shows realizations when derived interfaces are in the scope, but specializations are outside the scope.</p></li><li><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">The <strong>Show outside implementations</strong> check box counts outside derived elements from displayed realizations. </span>Otherwise, the realization will not be fully displayed and the diagram will not be valid.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000059 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01481: (2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard

- page_id: `304000059`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000059/2026x+Refresh1+Realized+Operational+Exchange+wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:43.483+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Dialogs
- labels: ['realized-operational-exchange-wizard', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Realized Operational Exchange** wizard gives the instructions to follow when adding a new Operational Exchange or selecting one of the existing Operational Exchanges.

The **Realized Operational Exchange** wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Operational Exchange creation (mandatory)

This step is designed to create a new Operational Exchange by specifying a Conveyed Item, both sending and receiving Operational Agents,and a direction of an Operational Exchange. Depending on a fact, if the selected Operational Agentis known or not, requested information differs:

- If sending and receiving Operational Agents are unknown from the wizard invocation context, you need to specify both. [ATTACHMENT filename='realized_operational_exchange_wizard_I_unknown_performers.png']
- If sending and receiving Operational Agent are known, they are added to a Realized Operational Exchange wizard automatically. [ATTACHMENT filename='realized_operational_activity_I.png']

| Box | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Conveyed Item | One or more Operational Exchange Items that are conveyed by the information flow from the sending Operational Agents to receiving Operational Agents.Exchange KindConveyed ItemConfiguration ExchangeCapability ConfigurationEnergy ExchangeNatural ResourceGeo Political Extent ExchangeGeo Political Extent TypeInformation ExchangeInformation ElementOperational SignalMateriel ExchangeTechnologySoftwareResource ArtifactOrganizational ExchangeProjectOrganizationPostResponsibilityPerson |
| Exchange Kind | Conveyed Item |
| Configuration Exchange | Capability Configuration |
| Energy Exchange | Natural Resource |
| Geo Political Extent Exchange | Geo Political Extent Type |
| Information Exchange | Information ElementOperational Signal |
| Materiel Exchange | TechnologySoftwareResource Artifact |
| Organizational Exchange | ProjectOrganizationPostResponsibilityPerson |
| Operational Exchange | Operational Exchange that specifies an information flow for specified Conveyed Items.If you select an existing Operational Exchange from the drop-down list, the Conveyed Item box and Direction drop-down list will be filled with corresponding information automatically. You will be able to edit the values of Conveyed Items.The direction of the information flow won’t be editable.Available if sending and receiving Operational Agents are known from the wizard invocation context. |
| Sending Operational Agent | Operational Agents that send the specified conveyed information.Available if an Operational Agent is unknown from the wizard invocation context. |
| Receiving Operational Agent | Operational Agents that receive the specified conveyed information.Available if an Operational Agent is unknown from the wizard invocation context. |
| Direction | The direction of the information flow.After changing the direction from the default one, the sending Operational Agent is switched with the receiving Operational Agent. The direction of the Operational Exchange is changed accordingly. |
| Add Operational Exchange to corresponding relationships | Select the check box if you want to add the Operational Exchange to the corresponding relationships with the same sending and receiving Operational Agents at their ends as defined in the first step of the wizard. |

##### Producing and Consuming Activities editing

This step is designed to review Producing and Consuming Activities for the created or selected Operational Exchange. Operational Exchanges are produced and consumed by Activities performed by the Operational Agents. You can remove the realization between listed Operational Activity pairs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Affected diagram reviewing and updating

In this step, you can see the list of diagrams where the selected Operational Exchange can be realized and displayed. You can choose one or more diagrams you want the specified exchange to be displayed. If the diagrams are grayed out, this means there are no Conveyed Items of the selected Operational Exchange to be displayed.

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Setting project options' space='']**Show Item Flows on Affected Diagrams** controls how the diagrams will be updated for newly created or modified exchanges. Set the option to true to update all affected diagrams and show the Conveyed Items of Operational Exchanges whenever a new exchange is created or an existing one is modified.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To learn more about previewing affected diagrams, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Preview Affected Diagrams dialog in UAF' space=''].

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Connectivity diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Connectivity Table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Constraints Definition diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Sequences diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Operational Exchanges in L1 Node Types diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Operational Exchanges in L2 Logical Internal Scenario diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Operational Exchanges in L4 Logical Activities diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Operational Exchanges in L6 Logical Sequence diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 Internal diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-5b diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-6c diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']
